#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit rules file
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/11-perm_chng.rules"

# Define the rules we need to add
# For 64-bit systems, we need rules for 64-bit syscalls
# For 32-bit compatibility, we also add 32-bit rules

# The rename-style syscalls we need to audit:
# - rename (old syscall)
# - renameat (newer syscall)
# - renameat2 (newest syscall)
# - unlink (delete file)
# - unlinkat (delete file, newer)

# Rules for 64-bit syscalls
RULE_RENAME_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,renameat,renameat2 -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=delete"
RULE_UNLINK_64="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S unlink,unlinkat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=delete"

# Rules for 32-bit syscalls (for 32-bit binaries on 64-bit systems or 32-bit only systems)
RULE_RENAME_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S rename,renameat,renameat2 -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=delete"
RULE_UNLINK_32="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S unlink,unlinkat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -F key=delete"

# Create the rules file directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Function to check if a rule exists in a file
rule_exists() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Normalize whitespace for comparison
        local normalized_rule=$(echo "$rule" | tr -s ' ')
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            local normalized_line=$(echo "$line" | tr -s ' ')
            if [ "$normalized_rule" = "$normalized_line" ]; then
                return 0
            fi
        done < "$file"
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to add a rule if it doesn't exist
add_rule_if_missing() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    
    if ! rule_exists "$rule" "$file"; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Detect architecture
ARCH=$(uname -m)

# Track if any changes were made
CHANGES_MADE=0

# Create the file if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    touch "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    CHANGES_MADE=1
fi

# Add 32-bit rules (needed on both 32-bit systems and 64-bit systems for 32-bit binaries)
if add_rule_if_missing "$RULE_RENAME_32" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
    CHANGES_MADE=1
fi

if add_rule_if_missing "$RULE_UNLINK_32" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
    CHANGES_MADE=1
fi

# Add 64-bit rules only on 64-bit systems
if [ "$ARCH" = "x86_64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "ppc64le" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "s390x" ]; then
    if add_rule_if_missing "$RULE_RENAME_64" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
        CHANGES_MADE=1
    fi
    
    if add_rule_if_missing "$RULE_UNLINK_64" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"; then
        CHANGES_MADE=1
    fi
fi

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Reload audit rules if changes were made
if [ $CHANGES_MADE -eq 1 ]; then
    # Check if auditd is running
    if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
        # Use augenrules to merge and load rules
        augenrules --load > /dev/null 2>&1
    fi
fi

# Ensure auditd is enabled to start at boot
systemctl enable auditd > /dev/null 2>&1

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure password-quality policy requiring at least one uppercase character
# on RHEL 8 systems using PAM-based pwquality mechanism

set -e

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PARAM_NAME="ucredit"
PARAM_VALUE="-1"

# Ensure the pwquality.conf file exists
if [[ ! -f "$PWQUALITY_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $PWQUALITY_CONF does not exist. Ensure libpwquality is installed."
    exit 1
fi

# Check if ucredit is already set correctly
if grep -q "^${PARAM_NAME}\s*=\s*${PARAM_VALUE}$" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Password quality policy already configured: ${PARAM_NAME} = ${PARAM_VALUE}"
    exit 0
fi

# Check if ucredit exists but with different value (commented or uncommented)
if grep -q "^${PARAM_NAME}\s*=" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Update existing uncommented line
    sed -i "s/^${PARAM_NAME}\s*=.*/${PARAM_NAME} = ${PARAM_VALUE}/" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    echo "Updated existing ${PARAM_NAME} setting to ${PARAM_VALUE}"
elif grep -q "^#\s*${PARAM_NAME}\s*=" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Uncomment and set the value
    sed -i "s/^#\s*${PARAM_NAME}\s*=.*/${PARAM_NAME} = ${PARAM_VALUE}/" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    echo "Uncommented and set ${PARAM_NAME} to ${PARAM_VALUE}"
else
    # Add the setting if it doesn't exist at all
    echo "${PARAM_NAME} = ${PARAM_VALUE}" >> "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    echo "Added ${PARAM_NAME} = ${PARAM_VALUE} to ${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
fi

# Verify the configuration was applied
if grep -q "^${PARAM_NAME}\s*=\s*${PARAM_VALUE}$" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    echo "Password quality policy successfully configured to require at least one uppercase character."
else
    echo "Error: Failed to configure password quality policy."
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to disable Ctrl-Alt-Del burst action on RHEL 8

set -euo pipefail

# The systemd configuration file for ctrl-alt-del burst settings
SYSTEMD_CONF="/etc/systemd/system.conf"
DROPIN_DIR="/etc/systemd/system.conf.d"
DROPIN_FILE="${DROPIN_DIR}/99-ctrl-alt-del-burst.conf"

# Method 1: Use drop-in configuration (preferred method)
# This sets CtrlAltDelBurstAction=none to disable the burst reboot feature

# Create the drop-in directory if it doesn't exist
if [[ ! -d "${DROPIN_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${DROPIN_DIR}"
fi

# Define the desired configuration content
DESIRED_CONTENT="[Manager]
CtrlAltDelBurstAction=none"

# Check if the drop-in file exists and has the correct content
if [[ -f "${DROPIN_FILE}" ]]; then
    CURRENT_CONTENT=$(cat "${DROPIN_FILE}")
    if [[ "${CURRENT_CONTENT}" == "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" ]]; then
        echo "Drop-in configuration already correctly set in ${DROPIN_FILE}"
    else
        echo "Updating drop-in configuration in ${DROPIN_FILE}"
        echo "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${DROPIN_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "Creating drop-in configuration ${DROPIN_FILE}"
    echo "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${DROPIN_FILE}"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the drop-in file
chmod 644 "${DROPIN_FILE}"

# Method 2: Also mask the ctrl-alt-del.target to fully disable Ctrl-Alt-Del
# This provides defense in depth by disabling both the single press and burst actions

CTRL_ALT_DEL_TARGET="ctrl-alt-del.target"
MASK_LINK="/etc/systemd/system/${CTRL_ALT_DEL_TARGET}"

# Check if ctrl-alt-del.target is already masked
if [[ -L "${MASK_LINK}" ]] && [[ "$(readlink -f "${MASK_LINK}")" == "/dev/null" ]]; then
    echo "ctrl-alt-del.target is already masked"
else
    echo "Masking ctrl-alt-del.target"
    systemctl mask "${CTRL_ALT_DEL_TARGET}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Reload systemd to apply the new configuration
echo "Reloading systemd daemon configuration"
systemctl daemon-reload

# Verify the settings are applied
echo ""
echo "Verification:"

# Check CtrlAltDelBurstAction setting
BURST_SETTING=$(systemctl show -p CtrlAltDelBurstAction 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2)
if [[ "${BURST_SETTING}" == "none" ]]; then
    echo "✓ CtrlAltDelBurstAction is set to 'none'"
else
    echo "✗ Warning: CtrlAltDelBurstAction is set to '${BURST_SETTING}' (expected 'none')"
fi

# Check if ctrl-alt-del.target is masked
MASK_STATE=$(systemctl is-enabled "${CTRL_ALT_DEL_TARGET}" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
if [[ "${MASK_STATE}" == "masked" ]]; then
    echo "✓ ctrl-alt-del.target is masked"
else
    echo "✗ Warning: ctrl-alt-del.target state is '${MASK_STATE}' (expected 'masked')"
fi

echo ""
echo "Ctrl-Alt-Del burst action has been disabled. Settings will persist across reboots."

#!/bin/bash

set -euo pipefail

KERNEL_PARAM="slub_debug=P"
GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR="GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX"
GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE="/etc/default/grub"

# Function to check if parameter already exists in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
param_exists_in_grub_default() {
    if grep -q "^${GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR}=" "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE"; then
        local current_value
        current_value=$(grep "^${GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR}=" "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" | sed "s/^${GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR}=\"\(.*\)\"/\1/")
        if echo "$current_value" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])slub_debug=[^[:space:]]*([[:space:]]|$)"; then
            # Check if it's specifically slub_debug=P or contains P
            if echo "$current_value" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])slub_debug=P([[:space:]]|$)"; then
                return 0
            fi
            return 2  # slub_debug exists but with different value
        fi
    fi
    return 1  # param doesn't exist
}

# Backup grub default file
cp "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE" "${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Check current state and update if needed
param_exists_in_grub_default
status=$?

if [ $status -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Parameter ${KERNEL_PARAM} already present in ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
elif [ $status -eq 2 ]; then
    # slub_debug exists with different value, replace it
    echo "Updating existing slub_debug parameter to ${KERNEL_PARAM}"
    sed -i "s/\(^${GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR}=\".*\)slub_debug=[^[:space:]\"]*/\1${KERNEL_PARAM}/" "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE"
else
    # Parameter doesn't exist, add it
    echo "Adding ${KERNEL_PARAM} to ${GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE}"
    if grep -q "^${GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR}=" "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE"; then
        # GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX exists, append to it
        sed -i "s/^\(${GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR}=\".*\)\"/\1 ${KERNEL_PARAM}\"/" "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE"
    else
        # GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX doesn't exist, create it
        echo "${GRUB_CMDLINE_VAR}=\"${KERNEL_PARAM}\"" >> "$GRUB_DEFAULT_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Regenerate grub configuration
# On RHEL 8, grub2-mkconfig is used
# Check for EFI vs BIOS boot
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
fi

echo "Regenerating GRUB configuration at ${GRUB_CFG}"
grub2-mkconfig -o "$GRUB_CFG"

echo "Kernel memory allocator object-poisoning (slub_debug=P) has been configured."
echo "The setting will be applied to all current and future kernels."
echo "A reboot is required for the changes to take effect."

# Ensure Postfix mail server is installed on RHEL 8

# Check if postfix is already installed

    echo "Postfix is already installed."
    exit 0
fi

# Install postfix
echo "Installing Postfix..."
dnf install -y postfix

# Verify installation was successful
if rpm -q postfix &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Postfix has been successfully installed."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Failed to install Postfix."
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
CRYPTO_POLICIES_BACK_END="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"

# Function to remove MACs directive from a file
remove_macs_from_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Check if file contains MACs directive (case-insensitive, not commented)
        if grep -qi "^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Remove lines that set MACs (case-insensitive)
            sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*[Mm][Aa][Cc][Ss][[:space:]]/d' "$file"
            echo "Removed MACs directive from $file"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to check if crypto-policies back-end include exists
check_crypto_policy_include() {
    local file="$1"
    if grep -q "Include[[:space:]]*/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Track if changes were made
CHANGES_MADE=0

# Remove any MACs directives from main sshd_config
if remove_macs_from_file "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    CHANGES_MADE=1
fi

# Remove any MACs directives from drop-in config files
if [[ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    for conf_file in "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"/*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$conf_file" ]]; then

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
CRYPTO_POLICIES_BACK_END="/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config"

# Function to remove MACs directive from a file
remove_macs_from_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Check if file contains MACs directive (case-insensitive, not commented)
        if grep -qi "^[[:space:]]*MACs[[:space:]]" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Remove lines that set MACs (case-insensitive)
            sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*[Mm][Aa][Cc][Ss][[:space:]]/d' "$file"
            echo "Removed MACs directive from $file"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to check if crypto-policies back-end include exists
check_crypto_policy_include() {
    local file="$1"
    if grep -q "Include[[:space:]]*/etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Track if changes were made
CHANGES_MADE=0

# Remove any MACs directives from main sshd_config
if remove_macs_from_file "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    CHANGES_MADE=1
fi

# Remove any MACs directives from drop-in config files
if [[ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    for conf_file in "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"/*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$conf_file" ]]; then
            if remove_macs_from_file "$conf_file"; then

fi

# This should typically be present by default on RHEL 8, but verify

    # Check if Include directive exists for crypto-policies in any form

        # Add the include at the beginning of the file (after any initial comments)
        # The include should be early so that local configs can override if needed
        # But for MACs we want system policy, so we ensure no local MACs override exists
        
        # Check if there's already an Include line for sshd_config.d

        fi

        CHANGES_MADE=1
    fi
fi

if [[ ! -f "$CRYPTO_POLICIES_BACK_END" ]]; then

    echo "Ensure crypto-policies package is installed and run 'update-crypto-policies --set FIPS' or appropriate policy"
fi

if sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "SSH configuration syntax is valid"
else
    echo "Warning: SSH configuration has syntax errors. Please review manually." >&2

    exit 1

fi

# Define the audit rule to monitor /etc/group for writes and attribute changes
AUDIT_RULE="-w /etc/group -p wa -k audit_rules_usergroup_modification"

# Define the audit rules directory and file
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/audit_rules_usergroup_modification.rules"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

RULE_EXISTS=0
if grep -r -q -- "-w /etc/group -p wa" "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/" 2>/dev/null; then
    RULE_EXISTS=1
fi

# If the rule doesn't exist, add it to our dedicated file
if [ "${RULE_EXISTS}" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "${AUDIT_RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Also check and update /etc/audit/audit.rules for systems that might use it directly
AUDIT_RULES_MAIN="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_MAIN}" ]; then
    if ! grep -q -- "-w /etc/group -p wa" "${AUDIT_RULES_MAIN}" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Insert the rule before the final -e line if it exists, otherwise append
        if grep -q "^-e" "${AUDIT_RULES_MAIN}"; then
            sed -i "/^-e/i ${AUDIT_RULE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_MAIN}"
        else
            echo "${AUDIT_RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_MAIN}"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Load the rules into the running audit system
# First try augenrules (preferred method on RHEL 8)
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null
fi

# Also use auditctl to load the rule immediately if not already loaded
if command -v auditctl &>/dev/null; then
    # Check if the rule is already loaded in the running system
    if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q -- "-w /etc/group -p wa"; then
        auditctl -w /etc/group -p wa -k audit_rules_usergroup_modification 2>/dev/null
    fi
fi

# Restart auditd to ensure rules are loaded (use service command as auditd doesn't support systemctl restart)
if command -v service &>/dev/null; then
    service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure audit rules for chcon command
# This script ensures the audit rule is in effect immediately and persists across reboots

set -e

# Define the chcon binary path and rule parameters
CHCON_PATH="/usr/bin/chcon"
RULE_KEY="privileged-chcon"
AUID_MIN="1000"

# Verify chcon exists
if [[ ! -f "${CHCON_PATH}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: ${CHCON_PATH} not found on this system"
    exit 1
fi

# Define the expected audit rule
# -a always,exit: Add rule to exit list, always generate record
# -F path=/usr/bin/chcon: Monitor this specific file
# -F perm=x: Trigger on execution
# -F auid>=1000: Only for users with login UID >= 1000
# -F auid!=unset (or auid!=-1): Only for users who have actually logged in
# -k privileged-chcon: Key for searching audit logs
AUDIT_RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${CHCON_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=${AUID_MIN} -F auid!=unset -k ${RULE_KEY}"
AUDIT_RULE_ALT="-a always,exit -F path=${CHCON_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=${AUID_MIN} -F auid!=-1 -k ${RULE_KEY}"

# Function to check if augenrules is being used
uses_augenrules() {
    if grep -q "^\s*USE_AUGENRULES\s*=\s*yes" /etc/sysconfig/auditd 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    elif grep -q "^\s*USE_AUGENRULES\s*=\s*no" /etc/sysconfig/auditd 2>/dev/null; then
        return 1
    else
        # Default behavior: check if augenrules exists and rules.d directory has files
        if [[ -x /usr/sbin/augenrules ]] && [[ -d /etc/audit/rules.d ]]; then
            return 0
        fi
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to check if a rule already exists in a file
rule_exists_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        # Check for the rule with either auid!=unset or auid!=-1 notation
        if grep -qE "^\s*-a\s+(always,exit|exit,always)\s+.*-F\s+path=${CHCON_PATH}\s+.*-F\s+perm=x\s+.*-F\s+auid>=${AUID_MIN}\s+.*-F\s+auid!=(unset|-1)\s+.*-k\s+${RULE_KEY}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
            return 0
        fi
        # Also check with different parameter ordering
        if grep -qE "^\s*-a\s+(always,exit|exit,always)\s+.*-F\s+path=${CHCON_PATH}.*-F\s+auid>=${AUID_MIN}.*-F\s+auid!=(unset|-1).*-k\s+${RULE_KEY}" "${file}" 2>/dev/null; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to check if rule is currently loaded in the kernel
rule_is_loaded() {
    local current_rules
    current_rules=$(auditctl -l 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    if echo "${current_rules}" | grep -qE "path=${CHCON_PATH}.*perm=x.*auid>=${AUID_MIN}.*auid!=(4294967295|-1|unset).*key=${RULE_KEY}"; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Ensure audit directories exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Determine the rules file to use
if uses_augenrules; then
    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"
    echo "System uses augenrules - adding rule to ${RULES_FILE}"
else
    RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
    echo "System uses auditctl - adding rule to ${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Check if the rule already exists in the appropriate file
if rule_exists_in_file "${RULES_FILE}"; then
    echo "Audit rule for chcon already exists in ${RULES_FILE}"
else
    # Add the rule to the file
    echo "Adding audit rule for chcon to ${RULES_FILE}"
    echo "${AUDIT_RULE}" >> "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# If using augenrules, also ensure the rule is in audit.rules for completeness
# This handles systems that might switch between methods
if uses_augenrules && [[ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ]]; then
    if ! rule_exists_in_file "/etc/audit/audit.rules"; then
        # Don't add directly to audit.rules when using augenrules
        # It will be regenerated by augenrules
        :
    fi
fi

# Ensure proper permissions on rules files
if [[ -f "${RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    chmod 640 "${RULES_FILE}"
    chown root:root "${RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Load the rule into the running kernel if not already loaded
if ! rule_is_loaded; then
    echo "Loading audit rule into running kernel"
    
    # Check if audit rules are locked
    if auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -q "enabled 2"; then
        echo "WARNING: Audit rules are locked (immutable mode). Rule will take effect after reboot."
    else
        # Try to load the rule directly
        if ! auditctl ${AUDIT_RULE} 2>/dev/null; then
            # Try alternative format with -1 instead of unset
            if ! auditctl ${AUDIT_RULE_ALT} 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "WARNING: Could not load rule directly. Attempting to reload all rules."
                
                # Reload rules based on the method in use
                if uses_augenrules; then
                    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
                else
                    # For auditctl method, we need to reload from audit.rules
                    if [[ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ]]; then
                        auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules 2>/dev/null || true
                    fi
                fi
            fi
        fi
    fi
else
    echo "Audit rule for chcon is already loaded in the kernel"
fi

# Verify the rule is now in place
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
echo "============="

echo ""
echo "Rule in configuration file (${RULES_FILE}):"
grep -E "chcon|${RULE_KEY}" "${RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || echo "  (rule not found in file)"

echo ""
echo "Currently loaded rules for chcon:"
auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -E "chcon|${RULE_KEY}" || echo "  (rule not currently loaded - may require reboot if rules are locked)"

echo ""
echo "Configuration complete. The chcon audit rule has been configured."
echo "This rule will persist across reboots and audit daemon restarts."

# and does not permit legacy/insecure protocol versions

set -e

GNUTLS_LOCAL_OVERRIDE="/etc/crypto-policies/local.d/gnutls-override.config"

check_crypto_policy() {

        current_policy=$(update-crypto-policies --show 2>/dev/null || echo "UNKNOWN")

# Final verification

configure_login_defs() {
    local login_defs="/etc/login.defs"
    
    if [[ -f "$login_defs" ]]; then
        # Check if UMASK line exists (commented or not)
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*UMASK" "$login_defs"; then
            # Replace existing UMASK line (whether commented or not)
            sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*UMASK.*/UMASK\t\t$TARGET_UMASK/" "$login_defs"
        else
            # Add UMASK if it doesn't exist
            echo -e "UMASK\t\t$TARGET_UMASK" >> "$login_defs"
        fi
    else
        echo "Warning: $login_defs not found"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to configure /etc/profile.d/ for additional enforcement
configure_profile_d() {
    local profile_script="/etc/profile.d/umask.sh"
    local desired_content="umask $TARGET_UMASK"

    if [[ -f "$profile_script" ]]; then

            return 0

        sed -i "s/umask[[:space:]]\+002/umask $TARGET_UMASK/g" "$bashrc"

configure_login_defs

configure_profile

configure_bashrc

echo "Configuring /etc/csh.cshrc..."
configure_cshrc

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to disable Kerberos authentication in SSH server on RHEL 8

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
DROP_IN_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/01-disable-kerberos.conf"

# Function to check if running as root
check_root() {
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Function to backup the original sshd_config if not already backed up
backup_config() {
    if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig" ]]; then
        cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig"
    fi
}

# Function to disable Kerberos authentication
disable_kerberos_auth() {
    local config_changed=0
    
    # Create sshd_config.d directory if it doesn't exist
    if [[ ! -d "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}"
        chmod 700 "${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}"
    fi
    
    # Check if the main config includes the config.d directory
    if ! grep -q "^Include ${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/\*\.conf" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Add Include directive at the beginning of the file if not present
        if ! grep -q "^Include.*/etc/ssh/sshd_config\.d/" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i '1i Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
            config_changed=1
        fi
    fi
    
    # Remove any existing KerberosAuthentication settings from the main config
    # to avoid conflicts (comment them out)
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*KerberosAuthentication[[:space:]]+" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*KerberosAuthentication[[:space:]].*/#&/' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        config_changed=1
    fi
    
    # Create or update the drop-in configuration file
    local desired_content="# Disable Kerberos authentication for SSH
# This file is managed by security remediation script
KerberosAuthentication no"
    
    if [[ -f "${DROP_IN_FILE}" ]]; then
        # Check if the file already has the correct setting
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*KerberosAuthentication[[:space:]]+no[[:space:]]*$" "${DROP_IN_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "Kerberos authentication is already disabled in ${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        else
            # Update the file with correct setting
            echo "${desired_content}" > "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
            chmod 600 "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
            config_changed=1
            echo "Updated ${DROP_IN_FILE} to disable Kerberos authentication"
        fi
    else
        # Create the drop-in file
        echo "${desired_content}" > "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        chmod 600 "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        config_changed=1
        echo "Created ${DROP_IN_FILE} to disable Kerberos authentication"
    fi
    
    # Also ensure GSSAPIAuthentication is disabled as it's related to Kerberos
    if ! grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*GSSAPIAuthentication[[:space:]]+no[[:space:]]*$" "${DROP_IN_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "GSSAPIAuthentication no" >> "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        config_changed=1
    fi
    
    # Comment out GSSAPIAuthentication in main config if enabled
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*GSSAPIAuthentication[[:space:]]+yes" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*GSSAPIAuthentication[[:space:]]\+yes/#&/' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        config_changed=1
    fi
    
    return ${config_changed}
}

# Function to validate sshd configuration
validate_config() {
    if sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "SSH configuration syntax is valid"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: SSH configuration syntax check failed" >&2
        sshd -t
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to restart sshd if configuration changed
restart_sshd() {
    if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
        systemctl reload sshd
        echo "SSH service reloaded"
    else
        echo "SSH service is not running, skipping reload"
    fi
}

# Main execution
main() {
    check_root
    backup_config
    
    if disable_kerberos_auth; then
        if validate_config; then
            restart_sshd
            echo "Kerberos authentication has been disabled in SSH server configuration"
        else
            echo "Rolling back changes due to configuration error" >&2
            if [[ -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig" ]]; then
                cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
            fi
            exit 1
        fi
    else
        echo "No changes were necessary - Kerberos authentication already disabled"
    fi
    
    # Verify the setting
    echo ""
    echo "Verification:"
    sshd -T 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "kerberosauthentication|gssapiauthentication" || echo "Could not verify settings"
}

main "$@"

        # Add Include directive at the beginning of the file if not present
        if ! grep -q "^Include.*/etc/ssh/sshd_config\.d/" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i '1i Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
            config_changed=1
        fi
    fi
    
    # Remove any existing KerberosAuthentication settings from the main config
    # to avoid conflicts (comment them out)
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*KerberosAuthentication[[:space:]]+" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*KerberosAuthentication[[:space:]].*/#&/' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        config_changed=1
    fi
    
    # Create or update the drop-in configuration file
    local desired_content="# Disable Kerberos authentication for SSH
# This file is managed by security remediation script
KerberosAuthentication no"
    
    if [[ -f "${DROP_IN_FILE}" ]]; then
        # Check if the file already has the correct setting
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*KerberosAuthentication[[:space:]]+no[[:space:]]*$" "${DROP_IN_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "Kerberos authentication is already disabled in ${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        else
            # Update the file with correct setting
            echo "${desired_content}" > "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
            chmod 600 "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
            config_changed=1
            echo "Updated ${DROP_IN_FILE} to disable Kerberos authentication"
        fi
    else
        # Create the drop-in file
        echo "${desired_content}" > "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        chmod 600 "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        config_changed=1
        echo "Created ${DROP_IN_FILE} to disable Kerberos authentication"
    fi
    
    # Also ensure GSSAPIAuthentication is disabled as it's related to Kerberos
    if ! grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*GSSAPIAuthentication[[:space:]]+no[[:space:]]*$" "${DROP_IN_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "GSSAPIAuthentication no" >> "${DROP_IN_FILE}"
        config_changed=1
    fi
    
    # Comment out GSSAPIAuthentication in main config if enabled
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*GSSAPIAuthentication[[:space:]]+yes" "${SSHD_CONFIG}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*GSSAPIAuthentication[[:space:]]\+yes/#&/' "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
        config_changed=1
    fi
    
    return ${config_changed}
}

# Function to validate sshd configuration
validate_config() {
    if sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "SSH configuration syntax is valid"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: SSH configuration syntax check failed" >&2
        sshd -t
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to restart sshd if configuration changed
restart_sshd() {
    if systemctl is-active --quiet sshd; then
        systemctl reload sshd
        echo "SSH service reloaded"
    else
        echo "SSH service is not running, skipping reload"
    fi
}

# Main execution
main() {
    check_root
    backup_config
    
    if disable_kerberos_auth; then
        if validate_config; then
            restart_sshd
            echo "Kerberos authentication has been disabled in SSH server configuration"
        else
            echo "Rolling back changes due to configuration error" >&2
            if [[ -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig" ]]; then
                cp -p "${SSHD_CONFIG}.orig" "${SSHD_CONFIG}"
            fi
            exit 1
        fi
    else
        echo "No changes were necessary - Kerberos authentication already disabled"
    fi
    
    # Verify the setting
    echo ""
    echo "Verification:"
    sshd -T 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "kerberosauthentication|gssapiauthentication" || echo "Could not verify settings"
}

main "$@"
```

#!/bin/bash

set -e

MOUNT_POINT="/dev/shm"
FSTAB_FILE="/etc/fstab"

# Function to check if noexec is already in the mount options
has_noexec() {
    local options="$1"
    echo "$options" | tr ',' '\n' | grep -qx "noexec"
}

# Function to add noexec to mount options
add_noexec() {
    local options="$1"
    if [ -z "$options" ] || [ "$options" = "defaults" ]; then
        echo "defaults,noexec"
    else
        echo "${options},noexec"
    fi
}

# Check current mount status
current_options=$(findmnt -n -o OPTIONS "$MOUNT_POINT" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

# Remount with noexec if not already set
if [ -n "$current_options" ]; then
    if ! has_noexec "$current_options"; then
        echo "Remounting $MOUNT_POINT with noexec option..."
        mount -o remount,noexec "$MOUNT_POINT"
    else
        echo "$MOUNT_POINT already mounted with noexec option."
    fi
else
    echo "Warning: $MOUNT_POINT is not currently mounted."
fi

# Update /etc/fstab for persistence
if grep -qE "^[^#]*\s+${MOUNT_POINT}\s+" "$FSTAB_FILE"; then
    # Entry exists, check if noexec is present
    existing_line=$(grep -E "^[^#]*\s+${MOUNT_POINT}\s+" "$FSTAB_FILE" | head -1)
    existing_options=$(echo "$existing_line" | awk '{print $4}')
    
    if ! has_noexec "$existing_options"; then
        echo "Updating existing $MOUNT_POINT entry in $FSTAB_FILE with noexec..."
        new_options=$(add_noexec "$existing_options")
        # Use sed to update the options field (4th field) for the /dev/shm entry
        sed -i -E "s|^([^#]*\s+${MOUNT_POINT}\s+\S+\s+)\S+(.*)|\1${new_options}\2|" "$FSTAB_FILE"
    else
        echo "$MOUNT_POINT entry in $FSTAB_FILE already has noexec option."
    fi
else
    # No entry exists, add one
    echo "Adding $MOUNT_POINT entry to $FSTAB_FILE with noexec option..."
    echo "tmpfs    ${MOUNT_POINT}    tmpfs    defaults,noexec,nosuid,nodev    0 0" >> "$FSTAB_FILE"
fi

echo "Remediation complete. $MOUNT_POINT is configured with noexec option."

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure smart-card support packages are installed for multifactor authentication on RHEL 8

# List of packages required for smart-card support
SMARTCARD_PACKAGES=(
    "pcsc-lite"
    "opensc"
    "pcsc-lite-ccid"
)

# Function to check if a package is installed
is_package_installed() {
    rpm -q "$1" &>/dev/null
}

# Function to install a package if not already installed
install_package_if_missing() {
    local package="$1"
    if ! is_package_installed "$package"; then
        echo "Installing $package..."
        yum install -y "$package"
        if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "$package installed successfully."
        else
            echo "Failed to install $package." >&2
            return 1
        fi
    else
        echo "$package is already installed."
    fi
    return 0
}

# Main execution
echo "Ensuring smart-card support packages are installed..."

exit_code=0

for package in "${SMARTCARD_PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    install_package_if_missing "$package"
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        exit_code=1
    fi
done

# Enable and start the pcscd service for smart-card daemon
if systemctl is-enabled pcscd.socket &>/dev/null || systemctl is-active pcscd.socket &>/dev/null; then
    echo "pcscd.socket is already enabled/active."
else
    echo "Enabling pcscd.socket..."
    systemctl enable pcscd.socket
fi

echo "Smart-card support configuration complete."
exit $exit_code

    touch "$COREDUMP_CONF"

            sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}/d" "$conf_file"
        fi
        # Also remove commented versions that might cause confusion
        if grep -q "^#.*${SYSCTL_PARAM}" "$conf_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            sed -i "/^#.*${SYSCTL_PARAM}/d" "$conf_file"
        fi
    fi
done

# Create or update the persistent configuration file
echo "${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${SYSCTL_VALUE}" > "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"

# Set proper permissions on the configuration file
chmod 644 "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"

# Verify the runtime setting
current_value=$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}")
if [[ "$current_value" != "${SYSCTL_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to apply runtime setting. Current value: ${current_value}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Verify the persistent configuration
if ! grep -q "^${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_VALUE}$" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to persist setting in ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Successfully disabled IPv6 ICMP redirect acceptance by default"
echo "Runtime value: $(sysctl ${SYSCTL_PARAM})"
echo "Persistent config: ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"

# This script is idempotent and ensures settings persist across reboots

# Idempotent script to configure SSH server to display system-wide warning banner
# on RHEL 8 systems

set -e

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
BANNER_FILE="/etc/issue"

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure the banner file exists
if [[ ! -f "$BANNER_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Warning: $BANNER_FILE does not exist. Creating default banner."
    cat > "$BANNER_FILE" << 'EOF'
*******************************************************************************
*                                                                             *
* This system is for the use of authorized users only. Individuals using      *
* this computer system without authority, or in excess of their authority,    *
* are subject to having all of their activities on this system monitored      *
* and recorded by system personnel.                                           *
*                                                                             *
* In the course of monitoring individuals improperly using this system, or    *
* in the course of system maintenance, the activities of authorized users     *
* may also be monitored.                                                      *
*                                                                             *
* Anyone using this system expressly consents to such monitoring and is       *
* advised that if such monitoring reveals possible evidence of criminal       *
* activity, system personnel may provide the evidence of such monitoring      *
* to law enforcement officials.                                               *
*                                                                             *
*******************************************************************************
EOF
fi

# Backup sshd_config if not already backed up
if [[ ! -f "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" ]]; then
    cp "$SSHD_CONFIG" "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak"
fi

# Check if Banner is already correctly configured
if grep -q "^Banner[[:space:]]\+${BANNER_FILE}$" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
    echo "SSH Banner is already correctly configured to $BANNER_FILE"
else
    # Remove any existing Banner lines (commented or not)
    sed -i '/^[#]*Banner[[:space:]]/d' "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    
    # Add the Banner configuration
    echo "Banner $BANNER_FILE" >> "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    echo "Configured SSH Banner to $BANNER_FILE"
fi

# Verify sshd configuration is valid
if ! sshd -t; then
    echo "ERROR: sshd configuration is invalid. Restoring backup."
    cp "${SSHD_CONFIG}.bak" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    exit 1
fi

systemctl restart sshd

# Enable sshd to ensure it starts on boot
systemctl enable sshd

echo "SSH server configured to display system-wide warning banner."
echo "Configuration will persist across reboots."

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Current Banner configuration:"
grep "^Banner" "$SSHD_CONFIG" || echo "No Banner line found (unexpected)"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure audit rules for /etc/gshadow on RHEL 8
# Monitors write and attribute-change events on the secure group-account database

set -euo pipefail

# Variables
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/50-identity.rules"
TARGET_FILE="/etc/gshadow"
AUDIT_KEY="identity"

# The audit rule to add - monitors writes (w) and attribute changes (a) to /etc/gshadow
AUDIT_RULE="-w ${TARGET_FILE} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Function to check if the rule already exists in a file
rule_exists_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        # Normalize whitespace and check for the rule
        grep -qE "^\s*-w\s+${TARGET_FILE}\s+-p\s+wa\s+-k\s+${AUDIT_KEY}\s*$" "${file}" 2>/dev/null
        return $?
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to check if the rule is currently loaded in the kernel
rule_is_loaded() {
    if command -v auditctl &>/dev/null; then
        auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qE "^-w\s+${TARGET_FILE}\s+-p\s+wa\s+-k\s+${AUDIT_KEY}$"
        return $?
    fi
    return 1
}

# Check if auditd is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing audit package..."
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled
if ! systemctl is-enabled auditd &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Enabling auditd service..."
    systemctl enable auditd
fi

# Check if the rule already exists in the rules file
if rule_exists_in_file "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULE}"; then
    echo "Audit rule for ${TARGET_FILE} already exists in ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
else
    # Check if the rule exists in any other rules file
    rule_found=false
    for rules_file in "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"/*.rules; do
        if [[ -f "${rules_file}" ]] && rule_exists_in_file "${rules_file}" "${AUDIT_RULE}"; then
            echo "Audit rule for ${TARGET_FILE} already exists in ${rules_file}"
            rule_found=true
            break
        fi
    done

    if [[ "${rule_found}" == "false" ]]; then
        echo "Adding audit rule for ${TARGET_FILE} to ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        # Append the rule to the file
        echo "${AUDIT_RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Load the rule into the running kernel if not already loaded
if rule_is_loaded; then
    echo "Audit rule is already loaded in the running kernel"
else
    echo "Loading audit rule into the running kernel..."
    # Use auditctl to load the rule directly (works with direct-load utility)
    auditctl -w "${TARGET_FILE}" -p wa -k "${AUDIT_KEY}" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Regenerate the compiled rules using augenrules (rules-compilation mechanism)
# This ensures the rules are properly compiled for persistence
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Regenerating compiled audit rules with augenrules..."
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Ensure auditd is running
if ! systemctl is-active auditd &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Starting auditd service..."
    systemctl start auditd
fi

# Verify the rule is in place
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
echo "============="
echo "Rules file content (${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}):"
grep -E "^-w\s+${TARGET_FILE}" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || echo "  (rule not found in file)"

echo ""
echo "Currently loaded rules for ${TARGET_FILE}:"
auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -E "${TARGET_FILE}" || echo "  (rule not loaded in kernel)"

echo ""
echo "Audit configuration for ${TARGET_FILE} has been established."
echo "Events will be tagged with key: ${AUDIT_KEY}"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash remediation script to enforce certificate-name authentication
# for remote logging on RHEL 8 systems

set -e

RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"
RSYSLOG_D_DIR="/etc/rsyslog.d"
REMOTE_LOG_CONF="${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/remote-logging-auth.conf"

# Ensure rsyslog.d directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"
fi

# Define the required configuration for certificate-based authentication
# StreamDriverAuthMode must be set to x509/name for certificate name verification
REQUIRED_SETTINGS=(
    '$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls'
    '$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1'
    '$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name'
)

# Function to check if a setting exists in rsyslog configuration
setting_exists() {
    local setting="$1"
    local setting_name
    setting_name=$(echo "$setting" | awk '{print $1}')
    
    # Check in main config and all conf.d files
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    if [[ -d "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}" ]]; then
        if grep -rqE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/"*.conf 2>/dev/null; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    return 1
}

# Function to update or add setting
update_setting() {
    local setting="$1"
    local setting_name
    setting_name=$(echo "$setting" | awk '{print $1}')
    
    # Remove any existing conflicting settings from main config
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}/d" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}"
    fi
    
    # Remove from other conf.d files except our managed file
    if [[ -d "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}" ]]; then
        for conf_file in "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"/*.conf; do
            if [[ -f "$conf_file" ]] && [[ "$conf_file" != "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}" ]]; then
                if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "$conf_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                    sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}/d" "$conf_file"
                fi
            fi
        done
    fi
}

# Create or update the remote logging authentication configuration file
create_config() {
    local config_content=""
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    
    config_content="# Remote logging certificate-based authentication configuration
# Managed by security remediation script
# Last updated: ${timestamp}
#
# This configuration enforces x509/name authentication mode for remote
# logging to prevent sending logs to unauthorized destinations.

# Use GnuTLS as the network stream driver
\$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls

# Enable TLS encryption for forwarding
\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1

# Require x509/name authentication - validates the remote peer's certificate
# and verifies that the certificate's name matches the expected destination
\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name

# Optional: Specify permitted peers (uncomment and modify as needed)
# \$ActionSendStreamDriverPermittedPeer *.example.com
"

    echo "$config_content" > "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"
    chmod 644 "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"
}

# Ensure rsyslog-gnutls package is installed for TLS support
if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing rsyslog-gnutls package for TLS support..."
    dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls
fi

# Clean up any conflicting settings from existing configurations
for setting in "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
    update_setting "$setting"
done

# Check if our configuration file exists and has correct content
needs_update=false

if [[ ! -f "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}" ]]; then
    needs_update=true
else
    # Verify all required settings are present with correct values
    if ! grep -q '^\$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls' "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"; then
        needs_update=true
    fi
    if ! grep -q '^\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1' "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"; then
        needs_update=true
    fi
    if ! grep -q '^\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name' "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"; then
        needs_update=true
    fi
fi

if [[ "$needs_update" == "true" ]]; then
    echo "Creating/updating remote logging authentication configuration..."
    create_config
    echo "Configuration file created: ${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"
else
    echo "Remote logging authentication configuration is already correct."
fi

# Verify rsyslog configuration syntax
echo "Verifying rsyslog configuration syntax..."
if ! rsyslogd -N 1 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Warning: rsyslog configuration syntax check returned warnings (this may be expected)"
fi

# Ensure rsyslog service is enabled and restart to apply changes
echo "Ensuring rsyslog service is enabled..."
systemctl enable rsyslog

echo "Restarting rsyslog service to apply configuration..."
systemctl restart rsyslog

# Verify the service is running
if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    echo "rsyslog service is running with updated configuration."
else
    echo "Error: rsyslog service failed to start. Check configuration."
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "Remediation complete. Remote logging is now configured to use x509/name"
echo "certificate authentication for verifying remote log destinations."
echo ""
echo "Note: Ensure the following are properly configured for full TLS operation:"
echo "  - CA certificate path (\$DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile)"
echo "  - Client certificate (\$DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile)"
echo "  - Client private key (\$DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile)"
echo "  - Permitted peer names (\$ActionSendStreamDriverPermittedPeer)"

# Define the required configuration for certificate-based authentication
# StreamDriverAuthMode must be set to x509/name for certificate name verification
REQUIRED_SETTINGS=(
    '$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls'
    '$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1'
    '$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name'
)

# Function to check if a setting exists in rsyslog configuration
setting_exists() {
    local setting="$1"
    local setting_name
    setting_name=$(echo "$setting" | awk '{print $1}')
    
    # Check in main config and all conf.d files
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    if [[ -d "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}" ]]; then
        if grep -rqE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}/"*.conf 2>/dev/null; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    return 1
}

# Function to update or add setting
update_setting() {
    local setting="$1"
    local setting_name
    setting_name=$(echo "$setting" | awk '{print $1}')
    
    # Remove any existing conflicting settings from main config
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}" 2>/dev/null; then
        sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}/d" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}"
    fi
    
    # Remove from other conf.d files except our managed file
    if [[ -d "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}" ]]; then
        for conf_file in "${RSYSLOG_D_DIR}"/*.conf; do
            if [[ -f "$conf_file" ]] && [[ "$conf_file" != "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}" ]]; then
                if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}" "$conf_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                    sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*\\${setting_name}/d" "$conf_file"
                fi
            fi
        done
    fi
}

# Create or update the remote logging authentication configuration file
create_config() {
    local config_content=""
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    
    config_content="# Remote logging certificate-based authentication configuration
# Managed by security remediation script
# Last updated: ${timestamp}
#
# This configuration enforces x509/name authentication mode for remote
# logging to prevent sending logs to unauthorized destinations.

# Use GnuTLS as the network stream driver
\$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls

# Enable TLS encryption for forwarding
\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1

# Require x509/name authentication - validates the remote peer's certificate
# and verifies that the certificate's name matches the expected destination
\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name

# Optional: Specify permitted peers (uncomment and modify as needed)
# \$ActionSendStreamDriverPermittedPeer *.example.com
"

    echo "$config_content" > "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"
    chmod 644 "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"
}

# Ensure rsyslog-gnutls package is installed for TLS support
if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing rsyslog-gnutls package for TLS support..."
    dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls
fi

# Clean up any conflicting settings from existing configurations
for setting in "${REQUIRED_SETTINGS[@]}"; do
    update_setting "$setting"
done

# Check if our configuration file exists and has correct content
needs_update=false

if [[ ! -f "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}" ]]; then
    needs_update=true
else
    # Verify all required settings are present with correct values
    if ! grep -q '^\$DefaultNetstreamDriver gtls' "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"; then
        needs_update=true
    fi
    if ! grep -q '^\$ActionSendStreamDriverMode 1' "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"; then
        needs_update=true
    fi
    if ! grep -q '^\$ActionSendStreamDriverAuthMode x509/name' "${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"; then
        needs_update=true
    fi
fi

if [[ "$needs_update" == "true" ]]; then
    echo "Creating/updating remote logging authentication configuration..."
    create_config
    echo "Configuration file created: ${REMOTE_LOG_CONF}"
else
    echo "Remote logging authentication configuration is already correct."
fi

# Verify rsyslog configuration syntax
echo "Verifying rsyslog configuration syntax..."
if ! rsyslogd -N 1 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Warning: rsyslog configuration syntax check returned warnings (this may be expected)"
fi

# Ensure rsyslog service is enabled and restart to apply changes
echo "Ensuring rsyslog service is enabled..."
systemctl enable rsyslog

echo "Restarting rsyslog service to apply configuration..."
systemctl restart rsyslog

# Verify the service is running
if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    echo "rsyslog service is running with updated configuration."
else
    echo "Error: rsyslog service failed to start. Check configuration."
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "Remediation complete. Remote logging is now configured to use x509/name"
echo "certificate authentication for verifying remote log destinations."
echo ""
echo "Note: Ensure the following are properly configured for full TLS operation:"
echo "  - CA certificate path (\$DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile)"
echo "  - Client certificate (\$DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile)"
echo "  - Client private key (\$DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile)"
echo "  - Permitted peer names (\$ActionSendStreamDriverPermittedPeer)"
```

check_setting_in_file() {

    local setting="$2"
    local value="$3"
    
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Check for the exact setting (case-insensitive for the setting name)
        if grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*${setting}[[:space:]]+" "$file"; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to remove conflicting settings from main config
remove_setting_from_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local setting="$2"
    
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Remove any existing lines with this setting (commented or not)
        sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*${setting}[[:space:]]/Id" "$file"
        sed -i "/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${setting}[[:space:]]/Id" "$file"
    fi
}

if [[ ! -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then

    chmod 700 "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"

        sed -i "1i Include ${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/*.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
fi

# Remove any existing IgnoreUserKnownHosts settings from main config to avoid conflicts
remove_setting_from_file "$SSHD_CONFIG" "$SETTING_NAME"

# Create or update the drop-in configuration file
echo "${SETTING_NAME} ${SETTING_VALUE}" > "$DROP_IN_FILE"
chmod 600 "$DROP_IN_FILE"

# Validate the SSH configuration
if ! sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: SSH configuration validation failed" >&2
    sshd -t
    exit 1
fi

fi

if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^sshd.service"; then
    systemctl enable sshd
elif systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q "^ssh.service"; then
    systemctl enable ssh
fi

echo "SSH server configured to ignore user known hosts files (IgnoreUserKnownHosts yes)"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure audit rules for fremovexattr syscall
# on RHEL 8 to monitor removal of extended attributes

set -e

# Define the audit rules we need to add
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/30-fremovexattr.rules"
AUDIT_KEY="perm_mod"

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Define the four rules needed:
# 1. 64-bit syscall for unprivileged users (auid >= 1000 and auid != unset)
# 2. 32-bit syscall for unprivileged users (auid >= 1000 and auid != unset)
# 3. 64-bit syscall for root user (auid = 0)
# 4. 32-bit syscall for root user (auid = 0)

RULE_64_UNPRIV="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fremovexattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"
RULE_32_UNPRIV="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fremovexattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"
RULE_64_ROOT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fremovexattr -F auid=0 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"
RULE_32_ROOT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fremovexattr -F auid=0 -k ${AUDIT_KEY}"

# Create or overwrite the rules file with the correct rules
cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << EOF
## Audit rules for fremovexattr syscall
## Records removal of extended attributes for DAC monitoring
## Applies to both 32-bit and 64-bit syscalls
## Covers unprivileged users (auid >= 1000) and root (auid = 0)

# 64-bit syscalls for unprivileged users
${RULE_64_UNPRIV}

# 32-bit syscalls for unprivileged users
${RULE_32_UNPRIV}

# 64-bit syscalls for root user
${RULE_64_ROOT}

# 32-bit syscalls for root user
${RULE_32_ROOT}
EOF

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
chown root:root "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Function to check if a rule exists in the running audit configuration
rule_exists_in_running() {
    local rule="$1"
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF "$rule"
}

# Load the rules into the running kernel audit system
# First, try using augenrules if available and configured
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    # Generate and load rules using augenrules
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Also load directly using auditctl to ensure rules are active immediately
# This handles both mechanisms and ensures rules are loaded regardless of configuration

# Load each rule if not already present (idempotent)
if ! rule_exists_in_running "arch=b64.*fremovexattr.*auid>=1000.*auid!=-1"; then
    auditctl ${RULE_64_UNPRIV} 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if ! rule_exists_in_running "arch=b32.*fremovexattr.*auid>=1000.*auid!=-1"; then
    auditctl ${RULE_32_UNPRIV} 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if ! rule_exists_in_running "arch=b64.*fremovexattr.*auid=0"; then
    auditctl ${RULE_64_ROOT} 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if ! rule_exists_in_running "arch=b32.*fremovexattr.*auid=0"; then
    auditctl ${RULE_32_ROOT} 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled for persistence across reboots
if systemctl is-active auditd &>/dev/null; then
    # Reload audit rules if auditd is running
    # Use SIGHUP to reload rules without restarting the daemon
    pkill -HUP auditd 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Enable auditd to start on boot
systemctl enable auditd 2>/dev/null || true

echo "Audit rules for fremovexattr syscall have been configured successfully."
echo "Rules file: ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
echo "Current fremovexattr rules in running configuration:"
auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep fremovexattr || echo "  (rules pending - may require auditd restart)"

exit 0

    SEMANAGE_PATH="/usr/sbin/semanage"

# Function to check if a rule exists in the running configuration
rule_exists_running() {
    local rule="$1"
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF "$rule"

rule_exists_persistent() {
    local rule="$1"
    if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE" ]; then
        grep -qF "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE"
    else

${AUDIT_RULE_ROOT}
"

    NEEDS_UPDATE=true

       ! grep -qF "auid=0" "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE"; then
        NEEDS_UPDATE=true
    fi
fi

if [ "$NEEDS_UPDATE" = true ]; then
    echo "$RULES_CONTENT" > "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE"
    chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE"
    echo "Persistent audit rules file created/updated: $AUDIT_RULE_FILE"
else
    echo "Persistent audit rules file already contains required rules"
fi

if ! rule_exists_running "path=${SEMANAGE_PATH}.*auid>=1000"; then

else
    echo "Audit rule for root already in running configuration"
fi

else

    echo "auditd service enabled"
fi

if systemctl is-active auditd &>/dev/null; then

    if [ "$NEEDS_UPDATE" = true ]; then
        # Use augenrules to merge and load rules
        augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
        echo "Audit rules reloaded"
    fi
else

    echo "auditd service started"
fi

# Verify the rules are in place

echo "Verification - Current audit rules for semanage:"
auditctl -l | grep -i semanage || echo "No semanage rules found in running config (this may indicate an issue)"

echo "Verification - Persistent rules file content:"
cat "$AUDIT_RULE_FILE"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure auditd space_left threshold on RHEL 8
# Sets space_left to 25% of the audit log partition capacity

set -e

AUDITD_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"

# Check if auditd.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "$AUDITD_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $AUDITD_CONF does not exist. Is auditd installed?"
    exit 1
fi

# Get the log_file location from auditd.conf
LOG_FILE=$(grep -E "^log_file\s*=" "$AUDITD_CONF" | sed 's/^log_file\s*=\s*//' | tr -d ' ')

# Default to /var/log/audit/audit.log if not found
if [[ -z "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    LOG_FILE="/var/log/audit/audit.log"
fi

# Get the directory containing the audit log
LOG_DIR=$(dirname "$LOG_FILE")

# Get the total size of the partition containing the audit logs in MB
PARTITION_SIZE_KB=$(df -P "$LOG_DIR" | awk 'NR==2 {print $2}')
PARTITION_SIZE_MB=$((PARTITION_SIZE_KB / 1024))

# Calculate 25% of the partition size
SPACE_LEFT_MB=$((PARTITION_SIZE_MB * 25 / 100))

# Ensure we have at least 1 MB as minimum
if [[ $SPACE_LEFT_MB -lt 1 ]]; then
    SPACE_LEFT_MB=1
fi

# Check if space_left line exists in auditd.conf
if grep -qE "^space_left\s*=" "$AUDITD_CONF"; then
    # Update existing space_left value
    sed -i "s/^space_left\s*=.*/space_left = $SPACE_LEFT_MB/" "$AUDITD_CONF"
else
    # Add space_left if it doesn't exist
    echo "space_left = $SPACE_LEFT_MB" >> "$AUDITD_CONF"
fi

# Ensure space_left_action is set to trigger notification (syslog is common default)
# Check if space_left_action exists
if grep -qE "^space_left_action\s*=" "$AUDITD_CONF"; then
    # Get current value
    CURRENT_ACTION=$(grep -E "^space_left_action\s*=" "$AUDITD_CONF" | sed 's/^space_left_action\s*=\s*//' | tr -d ' ')
    # If it's set to ignore or nothing, change to syslog
    if [[ "$CURRENT_ACTION" == "ignore" ]] || [[ -z "$CURRENT_ACTION" ]]; then
        sed -i "s/^space_left_action\s*=.*/space_left_action = syslog/" "$AUDITD_CONF"
    fi
else
    # Add space_left_action if it doesn't exist
    echo "space_left_action = syslog" >> "$AUDITD_CONF"
fi

# Restart auditd service to apply changes
# On RHEL 8, auditd requires special handling - use service command or kill -TERM and start
# The recommended way is to use 'service auditd restart' or reload
if command -v service &>/dev/null; then
    service auditd reload 2>/dev/null || service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# If service command didn't work, try systemctl with the special auditd handling
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    # Send SIGHUP to reload configuration
    pkill -HUP auditd 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "Configured auditd space_left to ${SPACE_LEFT_MB} MB (25% of ${PARTITION_SIZE_MB} MB partition)"
echo "Configuration persisted in $AUDITD_CONF"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to disable Bluetooth kernel module on RHEL 8
# This prevents the bluetooth module from being loaded on demand or automatically

set -e

BLACKLIST_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/bluetooth-blacklist.conf"
DISABLE_CONTENT="# Disable Bluetooth kernel module to reduce attack surface
install bluetooth /bin/false
blacklist bluetooth"

# Create or update the modprobe configuration file
if [ -f "$BLACKLIST_FILE" ]; then
    # Check if the file already has the correct content
    if grep -q "^install bluetooth /bin/false$" "$BLACKLIST_FILE" && \
       grep -q "^blacklist bluetooth$" "$BLACKLIST_FILE"; then
        echo "Bluetooth module is already disabled in $BLACKLIST_FILE"
    else
        # File exists but doesn't have correct content, update it
        echo "$DISABLE_CONTENT" > "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
        echo "Updated $BLACKLIST_FILE to disable Bluetooth module"
    fi
else
    # Create the file with the disable configuration
    echo "$DISABLE_CONTENT" > "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    echo "Created $BLACKLIST_FILE to disable Bluetooth module"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the file
chmod 644 "$BLACKLIST_FILE"

# Unload the bluetooth module if it's currently loaded
if lsmod | grep -q "^bluetooth "; then
    echo "Unloading currently loaded bluetooth module..."
    modprobe -r bluetooth 2>/dev/null || echo "Note: Could not unload bluetooth module (may be in use)"
else
    echo "Bluetooth module is not currently loaded"
fi

# Update module dependency database
depmod -a

echo "Bluetooth kernel module has been disabled and will not load on reboot"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure audit rules for /etc/sudoers.d directory
# on RHEL 8 systems

set -euo pipefail

SUDOERS_D_DIR="/etc/sudoers.d"
AUDIT_RULE_KEY="privileged-actions"
AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR}/50-privileged-sudoersd.rules"
AUDIT_CONF="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

# The audit rule we want to ensure exists
# -w: watch the directory
# -p wa: watch for write (w) and attribute changes (a)
# -k: key for filtering
AUDIT_RULE="-w ${SUDOERS_D_DIR} -p wa -k ${AUDIT_RULE_KEY}"

# Function to check if a rule exists in a file
rule_exists_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local rule="$2"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Normalize whitespace for comparison
        local normalized_rule
        normalized_rule=$(echo "$rule" | tr -s ' ')
        while IFS= read -r line; do
            local normalized_line
            normalized_line=$(echo "$line" | tr -s ' ' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//')
            if [[ "$normalized_line" == "$normalized_rule" ]]; then
                return 0
            fi
        done < "$file"
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to check if rule is currently loaded in the kernel
rule_loaded_in_kernel() {
    if command -v auditctl &>/dev/null; then
        # Check if the rule is currently active
        if auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF "${SUDOERS_D_DIR}"; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Ensure audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing audit package..."
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running
if ! systemctl is-enabled auditd &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Enabling auditd service..."
    systemctl enable auditd
fi

if ! systemctl is-active auditd &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Starting auditd service..."
    systemctl start auditd
fi

# Ensure the rules.d directory exists
if [[ ! -d "$AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR" ]]; then
    echo "Creating audit rules directory..."
    mkdir -p "$AUDIT_RULES_D_DIR"
fi

# Check and add rule to /etc/audit/rules.d/ for persistence with augenrules
# This ensures the rule persists when using augenrules (the default on RHEL 8)
if ! rule_exists_in_file "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" "$AUDIT_RULE"; then
    echo "Adding audit rule to ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}..."
    echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    RULES_CHANGED=true
else
    echo "Rule already exists in ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
    RULES_CHANGED=false
fi

# Also ensure the rule exists in /etc/audit/audit.rules for systems that
# might load rules directly without augenrules
if [[ -f "$AUDIT_CONF" ]]; then
    if ! rule_exists_in_file "$AUDIT_CONF" "$AUDIT_RULE"; then
        echo "Adding audit rule to ${AUDIT_CONF}..."
        # Insert before any -e 2 (immutable) line if present, otherwise append
        if grep -q "^-e 2" "$AUDIT_CONF"; then
            sed -i "/^-e 2/i ${AUDIT_RULE}" "$AUDIT_CONF"
        else
            echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> "$AUDIT_CONF"
        fi
        RULES_CHANGED=true
    else
        echo "Rule already exists in ${AUDIT_CONF}"
    fi
fi

# Load the rule into the running kernel if not already present
if ! rule_loaded_in_kernel; then
    echo "Loading audit rule into running kernel..."
    auditctl -w "${SUDOERS_D_DIR}" -p wa -k "${AUDIT_RULE_KEY}" 2>/dev/null || true
elif [[ "$RULES_CHANGED" == "true" ]]; then
    # If rules files changed but rule is loaded, try to reload
    # Note: auditd doesn't support reload via systemctl, use augenrules --load
    echo "Regenerating and loading audit rules..."
    if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
        augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || {
            # If augenrules fails (e.g., rules are locked), try direct load
            auditctl -w "${SUDOERS_D_DIR}" -p wa -k "${AUDIT_RULE_KEY}" 2>/dev/null || true
        }
    fi
fi

# Verify the rule is now active
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
if rule_loaded_in_kernel; then
    echo "✓ Audit rule is active in kernel"
    auditctl -l | grep "${SUDOERS_D_DIR}"
else
    echo "⚠ Audit rule may not be loaded (could be due to immutable rules)"
    echo "  Rule will be active after next reboot"
fi

if rule_exists_in_file "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" "$AUDIT_RULE"; then
    echo "✓ Audit rule persisted in ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

if [[ -f "$AUDIT_CONF" ]] && rule_exists_in_file "$AUDIT_CONF" "$AUDIT_RULE"; then
    echo "✓ Audit rule present in ${AUDIT_CONF}"
fi

echo ""
echo "Audit configuration for ${SUDOERS_D_DIR} complete."
echo "Events will be tagged with key: ${AUDIT_RULE_KEY}"
echo "Search with: ausearch -k ${AUDIT_RULE_KEY}"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure auditd disk full action to single-user mode on RHEL 8

set -e

AUDITD_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"

# Check if auditd.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "$AUDITD_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $AUDITD_CONF does not exist. Is auditd installed?"
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original config if backup doesn't exist
if [[ ! -f "${AUDITD_CONF}.bak" ]]; then
    cp "$AUDITD_CONF" "${AUDITD_CONF}.bak"
fi

# Function to set a configuration parameter idempotently
set_audit_param() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"
    
    # Check if parameter exists (commented or uncommented)
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "$file"; then
        # Parameter exists, update it
        sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "$file"
    else
        # Parameter doesn't exist, append it
        echo "${param} = ${value}" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Configure disk_full_action to SINGLE
# This switches the system to single-user mode when disk is full
set_audit_param "disk_full_action" "SINGLE" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Configure disk_error_action to SINGLE as well for disk errors
set_audit_param "disk_error_action" "SINGLE" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Configure admin_space_left_action to SINGLE for when admin space threshold is reached
# This provides early warning before disk is completely full
set_audit_param "admin_space_left_action" "SINGLE" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Ensure space_left_action is set to email or syslog for early warning
# before admin_space_left threshold
if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*space_left_action[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF"; then
    current_space_left=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*space_left_action[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF" | sed 's/.*=[[:space:]]*//')
    # Only change if it's set to IGNORE or SUSPEND which could lead to data loss
    if [[ "$current_space_left" == "IGNORE" ]] || [[ "$current_space_left" == "SUSPEND" ]]; then
        set_audit_param "space_left_action" "SYSLOG" "$AUDITD_CONF"
    fi
else
    set_audit_param "space_left_action" "SYSLOG" "$AUDITD_CONF"
fi

# Verify the changes
echo "Verifying configuration changes in $AUDITD_CONF:"
grep -E "^(disk_full_action|disk_error_action|admin_space_left_action|space_left_action)[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Enable auditd service to ensure it starts on boot
systemctl enable auditd

# Restart auditd service to apply changes
# Note: auditd requires special handling - use service command or -s signal
# The systemctl restart doesn't work properly for auditd due to security constraints
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    # auditd is running, need to reload/restart it
    # Using service command which properly handles auditd
    service auditd restart || {
        # If service restart fails, try using augenrules and kill -HUP
        pkill -HUP auditd 2>/dev/null || true
        echo "Sent HUP signal to auditd to reload configuration"
    }
else
    # auditd is not running, start it
    systemctl start auditd
fi

echo "Auditd disk full action configuration completed successfully."
echo "The system will switch to single-user mode when:"
echo "  - The audit log disk becomes full (disk_full_action = SINGLE)"
echo "  - A disk error occurs (disk_error_action = SINGLE)"  
echo "  - Admin space threshold is reached (admin_space_left_action = SINGLE)"
echo ""
echo "Configuration will persist across reboots."

# Function to set a configuration parameter idempotently

    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local file="$3"
    
    # Check if parameter exists (commented or uncommented)
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "$file"; then
        # Parameter exists, update it
        sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=.*/${param} = ${value}/" "$file"
    else
        # Parameter doesn't exist, append it
        echo "${param} = ${value}" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Configure disk_full_action to SINGLE
# This switches the system to single-user mode when disk is full
set_audit_param "disk_full_action" "SINGLE" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Configure disk_error_action to SINGLE as well for disk errors
set_audit_param "disk_error_action" "SINGLE" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Configure admin_space_left_action to SINGLE for when admin space threshold is reached
# This provides early warning before disk is completely full
set_audit_param "admin_space_left_action" "SINGLE" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Ensure space_left_action is set to email or syslog for early warning
# before admin_space_left threshold
if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*space_left_action[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF"; then
    current_space_left=$(grep -E "^[[:space:]]*space_left_action[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF" | sed 's/.*=[[:space:]]*//')
    # Only change if it's set to IGNORE or SUSPEND which could lead to data loss
    if [[ "$current_space_left" == "IGNORE" ]] || [[ "$current_space_left" == "SUSPEND" ]]; then
        set_audit_param "space_left_action" "SYSLOG" "$AUDITD_CONF"
    fi
else
    set_audit_param "space_left_action" "SYSLOG" "$AUDITD_CONF"
fi

# Verify the changes
echo "Verifying configuration changes in $AUDITD_CONF:"
grep -E "^(disk_full_action|disk_error_action|admin_space_left_action|space_left_action)[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDITD_CONF"

# Enable auditd service to ensure it starts on boot
systemctl enable auditd

# Restart auditd service to apply changes
# Note: auditd requires special handling - use service command or -s signal
# The systemctl restart doesn't work properly for auditd due to security constraints
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    # auditd is running, need to reload/restart it
    # Using service command which properly handles auditd
    service auditd restart || {
        # If service restart fails, try using augenrules and kill -HUP
        pkill -HUP auditd 2>/dev/null || true
        echo "Sent HUP signal to auditd to reload configuration"
    }
else
    # auditd is not running, start it
    systemctl start auditd
fi

echo "Auditd disk full action configuration completed successfully."
echo "The system will switch to single-user mode when:"
echo "  - The audit log disk becomes full (disk_full_action = SINGLE)"
echo "  - A disk error occurs (disk_error_action = SINGLE)"  
echo "  - Admin space threshold is reached (admin_space_left_action = SINGLE)"
echo ""
echo "Configuration will persist across reboots."
```

#!/bin/bash

# Minimum password age in days
MIN_PASS_AGE=1

# Get all local user accounts from /etc/shadow
while IFS=: read -r username password lastchange mindays maxdays warndays inactive expire reserved; do
    # Skip system accounts (UID < 1000) except root, and skip accounts without passwords
    uid=$(id -u "$username" 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Skip if user doesn't exist or we can't get UID
    if [[ -z "$uid" ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Skip system accounts (UID < 1000) but include root (UID 0)
    if [[ "$uid" -ge 1 && "$uid" -lt 1000 ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Skip accounts that are locked or have no password
    if [[ "$password" == "!" || "$password" == "*" || "$password" == "!!" || -z "$password" ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Check if mindays is empty, not a number, or less than MIN_PASS_AGE
    if [[ -z "$mindays" || ! "$mindays" =~ ^-?[0-9]+$ || "$mindays" -lt "$MIN_PASS_AGE" ]]; then
        echo "Setting minimum password age to $MIN_PASS_AGE day(s) for user: $username (current: ${mindays:-unset})"
        chage -m "$MIN_PASS_AGE" "$username"
    fi
done < /etc/shadow

# Also set the default for new users in /etc/login.defs
if grep -q "^PASS_MIN_DAYS" /etc/login.defs; then
    current_default=$(grep "^PASS_MIN_DAYS" /etc/login.defs | awk '{print $2}')
    if [[ "$current_default" -lt "$MIN_PASS_AGE" ]]; then
        echo "Updating PASS_MIN_DAYS in /etc/login.defs from $current_default to $MIN_PASS_AGE"
        sed -i "s/^PASS_MIN_DAYS.*/PASS_MIN_DAYS\t$MIN_PASS_AGE/" /etc/login.defs
    fi
else
    echo "Adding PASS_MIN_DAYS $MIN_PASS_AGE to /etc/login.defs"
    echo -e "PASS_MIN_DAYS\t$MIN_PASS_AGE" >> /etc/login.defs
fi

echo "Password minimum age enforcement complete."

# Disable Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot sequence on RHEL 8
# This script is idempotent and the changes persist across reboots and system updates

set -euo pipefail

# The ctrl-alt-del.target is what handles the Ctrl-Alt-Del key sequence
# By masking it, we prevent the reboot action

# Check if ctrl-alt-del.target is already masked
if systemctl is-enabled ctrl-alt-del.target 2>/dev/null | grep -q "masked"; then
    echo "ctrl-alt-del.target is already masked. No changes needed."
else
    # Mask the ctrl-alt-del.target to disable the reboot on Ctrl-Alt-Del
    systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target
    echo "ctrl-alt-del.target has been masked."

# Reload systemd daemon to ensure changes take effect
systemctl daemon-reload

# Verify the change
if systemctl is-enabled ctrl-alt-del.target 2>/dev/null | grep -q "masked"; then
    echo "Verification successful: Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot sequence is disabled."
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: Failed to disable Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot sequence."
