#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure audit rules for mount operations
# on RHEL 8 systems to track filesystem mounts by regular users (UID >= 1000)

set -e

# Define the audit rules file path
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/export-mount.rules"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"

# Determine system architecture
ARCH=$(uname -m)

# Define the audit rules based on architecture
# These rules monitor the mount syscall for users with auid >= 1000 and auid != unset (-1)
# The -F auid>=1000 ensures only regular users are tracked
# The -F auid!=unset (or -F auid!=-1) excludes processes without a login UID

if [ "${ARCH}" == "x86_64" ]; then
    # For 64-bit systems, we need both 32-bit (b32) and 64-bit (b64) rules
    RULE_64BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    
    # Create the rules file with both rules
    cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << EOF
# Audit rules for monitoring mount operations by regular users
# Generated by remediation script for data export tracking

# 64-bit mount syscall monitoring
${RULE_64BIT}

# 32-bit mount syscall monitoring (for 32-bit applications on 64-bit system)
${RULE_32BIT}
EOF

elif [ "${ARCH}" == "i686" ] || [ "${ARCH}" == "i386" ]; then
    # For 32-bit systems, only 32-bit rules are needed
    RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    
    cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << EOF
# Audit rules for monitoring mount operations by regular users
# Generated by remediation script for data export tracking

# 32-bit mount syscall monitoring
${RULE_32BIT}
EOF

elif [ "${ARCH}" == "aarch64" ]; then
    # For 64-bit ARM systems
    RULE_64BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    
    cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << EOF
# Audit rules for monitoring mount operations by regular users
# Generated by remediation script for data export tracking

# 64-bit mount syscall monitoring
${RULE_64BIT}

# 32-bit mount syscall monitoring
${RULE_32BIT}
EOF

else
    # For other architectures, try to use appropriate rules
    echo "Warning: Unrecognized architecture ${ARCH}, applying generic rules"
    RULE_64BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S mount -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k export"
    
    cat > "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" << EOF
# Audit rules for monitoring mount operations by regular users
# Generated by remediation script for data export tracking

# 64-bit mount syscall monitoring
${RULE_64BIT}

# 32-bit mount syscall monitoring
${RULE_32BIT}
EOF
fi

# Set appropriate permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"

# Ensure auditd service is enabled
systemctl enable auditd

# Check if auditd is running before trying to reload rules
if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
    # Load the new rules into the running audit daemon
    # Use augenrules to merge and load all rules from /etc/audit/rules.d/
    augenrules --load
else
    # If auditd is not running, start it
    systemctl start auditd
fi

echo "Audit rules for mount operation monitoring have been configured successfully."
echo "Rules are stored in: ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
echo "Events will be logged with the 'export' audit key."

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure RHEL 8 to ignore ICMP echo requests
# sent to broadcast or multicast addresses

set -e

SYSCTL_PARAM="net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts"
DESIRED_VALUE="1"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-icmp-echo-ignore-broadcasts.conf"

# Apply the setting immediately to the running kernel
current_value=$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

if [ "${current_value}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]; then
    sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE}" > /dev/null
    echo "Applied ${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE} to running kernel"
else
    echo "${SYSCTL_PARAM} is already set to ${DESIRED_VALUE} in running kernel"
fi

# Ensure the setting persists across reboots
CONF_LINE="${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}"

if [ -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]; then
    # Check if the file already contains the correct setting
    if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
        # Setting exists, check if it has the correct value
        if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${DESIRED_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
            echo "Persistent configuration already correct in ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
        else
            # Update the existing line with correct value
            sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=.*/${CONF_LINE}/" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
            echo "Updated ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE} with correct value"
        fi
    else
        # Setting doesn't exist in the file, append it
        echo "${CONF_LINE}" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
        echo "Added ${CONF_LINE} to ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    fi
else
    # File doesn't exist, create it with the setting
    echo "${CONF_LINE}" > "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    chmod 644 "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    echo "Created ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE} with ${CONF_LINE}"
fi

# Reload sysctl configuration to ensure consistency
sysctl --system > /dev/null 2>&1

echo "Configuration complete: System will ignore ICMP echo requests to broadcast/multicast addresses"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent remediation script to ensure account lockout events are logged on RHEL 8
# This script configures PAM faillock to audit account lockouts

set -e

# Function to check if running as root
check_root() {
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Function to backup a file if not already backed up
backup_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local backup="${file}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        cp -p "$file" "$backup"
        echo "Backed up $file to $backup"
    fi
}

# Function to ensure faillock.conf has audit enabled
configure_faillock_conf() {
    local faillock_conf="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
    
    if [[ -f "$faillock_conf" ]]; then
        # Check if audit is already enabled (uncommented)
        if grep -qE "^\s*audit\s*$" "$faillock_conf"; then
            echo "audit is already enabled in $faillock_conf"
        elif grep -qE "^\s*#\s*audit\s*$" "$faillock_conf"; then
            # Uncomment the audit line
            backup_file "$faillock_conf"
            sed -i 's/^\s*#\s*audit\s*$/audit/' "$faillock_conf"
            echo "Uncommented audit in $faillock_conf"
        else
            # Add audit option if not present
            backup_file "$faillock_conf"
            echo "audit" >> "$faillock_conf"
            echo "Added audit to $faillock_conf"
        fi
    else
        # Create faillock.conf with audit enabled
        echo "audit" > "$faillock_conf"
        chmod 644 "$faillock_conf"
        echo "Created $faillock_conf with audit enabled"
    fi
}

# Function to ensure pam_faillock has audit option in PAM files
configure_pam_faillock() {
    local pam_files=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth")
    
    for pam_file in "${pam_files[@]}"; do
        if [[ -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
            # Check if pam_faillock.so is configured
            if grep -q "pam_faillock.so" "$pam_file"; then
                # Check if any pam_faillock line is missing the audit option
                # Only modify if audit is not present and we're not using faillock.conf
                if grep -E "pam_faillock\.so" "$pam_file" | grep -qv "audit"; then
                    # Check if faillock.conf exists and has audit - if so, PAM will use that
                    if [[ -f "/etc/security/faillock.conf" ]] && grep -qE "^\s*audit\s*$" "/etc/security/faillock.conf"; then
                        echo "audit is configured in faillock.conf, PAM file $pam_file will use that configuration"
                    else
                        backup_file "$pam_file"
                        # Add audit option to pam_faillock.so lines that don't have it
                        sed -i '/pam_faillock\.so/ {/audit/! s/pam_faillock\.so/pam_faillock.so audit/}' "$pam_file"
                        echo "Added audit option to pam_faillock.so in $pam_file"
                    fi
                else
                    echo "audit option already present in pam_faillock.so lines in $pam_file"
                fi
            else
                echo "WARNING: pam_faillock.so not found in $pam_file - faillock may not be configured"
            fi
        else
            echo "WARNING: $pam_file does not exist"
        fi
    done
}

# Function to verify authselect is being used and configure if needed
configure_authselect() {
    # Check if authselect is managing PAM
    if command -v authselect &>/dev/null; then
        local current_profile
        current_profile=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null || true)
        
        if [[ -n "$current_profile" ]]; then
            echo "authselect is managing PAM with profile: $current_profile"
            
            # Check if with-faillock feature is enabled
            if authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
                echo "with-faillock feature is already enabled"
            else
                echo "Enabling with-faillock feature via authselect"
                authselect enable-feature with-faillock 2>/dev/null || true
            fi
            
            # The audit option should be set in /etc/security/faillock.conf
            # which we already configured above
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    # If authselect is not managing PAM, configure PAM files directly
    echo "authselect is not managing PAM, configuring PAM files directly"
    configure_pam_faillock
}

# Function to ensure auditd rules for faillock (additional logging)
configure_audit_rules() {
    local audit_rules_file="/etc/audit/rules.d/faillock.rules"
    local faillock_dir="/var/run/faillock"

    # Create audit rules for faillock directory access

    if [[ -d "/run/faillock" ]]; then

    if [[ -n "$rules_content" ]]; then

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent remediation script to ensure account lockout events are logged on RHEL 8
# This script configures PAM faillock to audit account lockouts

set -e

# Function to check if running as root
check_root() {
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Function to backup a file if not already backed up
backup_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local backup="${file}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        cp -p "$file" "$backup"
        echo "Backed up $file to $backup"
    fi
}

# Function to ensure faillock.conf has audit enabled
configure_faillock_conf() {
    local faillock_conf="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
    
    if [[ -f "$faillock_conf" ]]; then
        # Check if audit is already enabled (uncommented)
        if grep -qE "^\s*audit\s*$" "$faillock_conf"; then
            echo "audit is already enabled in $faillock_conf"
        elif grep -qE "^\s*#\s*audit\s*$" "$faillock_conf"; then
            # Uncomment the audit line
            backup_file "$faillock_conf"
            sed -i 's/^\s*#\s*audit\s*$/audit/' "$faillock_conf"
            echo "Uncommented audit in $faillock_conf"
        else
            # Add audit option if not present
            backup_file "$faillock_conf"
            echo "audit" >> "$faillock_conf"
            echo "Added audit to $faillock_conf"
        fi
    else
        # Create faillock.conf with audit enabled
        echo "audit" > "$faillock_conf"
        chmod 644 "$faillock_conf"
        echo "Created $faillock_conf with audit enabled"
    fi
}

# Function to ensure pam_faillock has audit option in PAM files
configure_pam_faillock() {
    local pam_files=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth")
    
    for pam_file in "${pam_files[@]}"; do
        if [[ -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
            # Check if pam_faillock.so is configured
            if grep -q "pam_faillock.so" "$pam_file"; then
                # Check if any pam_faillock line is missing the audit option
                # Only modify if audit is not present and we're not using faillock.conf
                if grep -E "pam_faillock\.so" "$pam_file" | grep -qv "audit"; then
                    # Check if faillock.conf exists and has audit - if so, PAM will use that
                    if [[ -f "/etc/security/faillock.conf" ]] && grep -qE "^\s*audit\s*$" "/etc/security/faillock.conf"; then
                        echo "audit is configured in faillock.conf, PAM file $pam_file will use that configuration"
                    else
                        backup_file "$pam_file"
                        # Add audit option to pam_faillock.so lines that don't have it
                        sed -i '/pam_faillock\.so/ {/audit/! s/pam_faillock\.so/pam_faillock.so audit/}' "$pam_file"
                        echo "Added audit option to pam_faillock.so in $pam_file"
                    fi
                else
                    echo "audit option already present in pam_faillock.so lines in $pam_file"
                fi
            else
                echo "WARNING: pam_faillock.so not found in $pam_file - faillock may not be configured"
            fi
        else
            echo "WARNING: $pam_file does not exist"
        fi
    done
}

# Function to verify authselect is being used and configure if needed
configure_authselect() {
    # Check if authselect is managing PAM
    if command -v authselect &>/dev/null; then
        local current_profile
        current_profile=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null || true)
        
        if [[ -n "$current_profile" ]]; then
            echo "authselect is managing PAM with profile: $current_profile"
            
            # Check if with-faillock feature is enabled
            if authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
                echo "with-faillock feature is already enabled"
            else
                echo "Enabling with-faillock feature via authselect"
                authselect enable-feature with-faillock 2>/dev/null || true
            fi
            
            # The audit option should be set in /etc/security/faillock.conf
            # which we already configured above
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    # If authselect is not managing PAM, configure PAM files directly
    echo "authselect is not managing PAM, configuring PAM files directly"
    configure_pam_faillock
}

# Function to ensure auditd rules for faillock (additional logging)
configure_audit_rules() {
    local audit_rules_file="/etc/audit/rules.d/faillock.rules"
    local faillock_dir="/var/run/faillock"
    local tally_dir="/var/log/tallylog"
    
    # Create audit rules for faillock directory access
    local rules_content=""
    
    # Add rule for faillock directory if it exists or will exist
    if [[ -d "$faillock_dir" ]] || [[ -d "/run/faillock" ]]; then
        rules_content+="-w /var/run/faillock -p wa -k logins\n"
    fi
    
    # Add rule for /run/faillock (symlink target on some systems)
    if [[ -d "/run/faillock" ]]; then
        rules_content+="-w /run/faillock -p wa -k logins\n"
    fi
    
    if [[ -n "$rules_content" ]]; then
        if [[ -f "$audit_rules_file" ]]; then
            # Check if rules already exist
            if grep -qF "/var/run/faillock" "$audit_rules_file" 2>/dev/null || \
               grep -qF "/run/faillock" "$audit_rules_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "Audit rules for faillock already exist in $audit_rules_file"
            else
                backup_file "$audit_rules_file"
                echo -e "$rules_content" >> "$audit_rules_file"
                echo "Added audit rules to $audit_rules_file"
            fi
        else
            echo -e "$rules_content" > "$audit_rules_file"
            chmod 640 "$audit_rules_file"
            echo "Created $audit_rules_file with faillock audit rules"
        fi
        
        # Reload audit rules if auditd is running
        if systemctl is-active --quiet auditd; then
            augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || auditctl -R /etc/audit/audit.rules 2>/dev/null || true
            echo "Reloaded audit rules"
        fi
    fi
}

# Main execution
main() {
    echo "Starting RHEL 8 account lockout logging remediation..."
    
    check_root
    
    # Configure faillock.conf with audit option
    configure_faillock_conf
    
    # Configure authselect or PAM files directly
    configure_authselect
    
    # Configure additional audit rules
    configure_audit_rules
    
    echo ""
    echo "Remediation complete. Account lockout events will now be logged."
    echo "Lockout events will be recorded in:"
    echo "  - /var/log/secure (via PAM)"
    echo "  - audit log (via auditd if configured)"

    echo "  2. Check /var/log/secure for 'pam_faillock' entries"

}

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to enforce screen lock on screensaver activation
# for RHEL 8 with GNOME desktop environment

set -e

# Define the dconf database directory and files
DCONF_LOCAL_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"

# Create directories if they don't exist
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCAL_DB_DIR"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_LOCKS_DIR"
mkdir -p "$DCONF_PROFILE_DIR"

# Ensure the user profile exists and includes the local database
PROFILE_FILE="$DCONF_PROFILE_DIR/user"
if [ ! -f "$PROFILE_FILE" ]; then
    cat > "$PROFILE_FILE" << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
else
    # Check if system-db:local is already in the profile
    if ! grep -q "^system-db:local$" "$PROFILE_FILE"; then
        # Add system-db:local after user-db:user if it exists, otherwise append
        if grep -q "^user-db:user$" "$PROFILE_FILE"; then
            sed -i '/^user-db:user$/a system-db:local' "$PROFILE_FILE"
        else
            echo "system-db:local" >> "$PROFILE_FILE"
        fi
    fi
    # Ensure user-db:user exists
    if ! grep -q "^user-db:user$" "$PROFILE_FILE"; then
        sed -i '1i user-db:user' "$PROFILE_FILE"
    fi
fi

# Create or update the screensaver lock settings file
SETTINGS_FILE="$DCONF_LOCAL_DB_DIR/00-screensaver"
cat > "$SETTINGS_FILE" << 'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-enabled=true
lock-delay=uint32 0
EOF

# Create or update the locks file to prevent user override
LOCKS_FILE="$DCONF_LOCKS_DIR/screensaver"
cat > "$LOCKS_FILE" << 'EOF'
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
EOF

# Set proper permissions
chmod 644 "$SETTINGS_FILE"
chmod 644 "$LOCKS_FILE"
chmod 644 "$PROFILE_FILE"

# Update the dconf database
dconf update

echo "Screen lock on screensaver activation has been enforced system-wide."
echo "Settings will persist across reboots and cannot be overridden by users."

# Ensure the drop-in directory exists
if [[ ! -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"
fi

# Define the desired configuration
DESIRED_SETTING="ClientAliveCountMax 0"

# Function to check if Include directive exists in main config
check_include_directive() {
    grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/\*\.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null
}

# Function to update or add setting in a file
update_setting_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local setting_name="$2"
    local full_setting="$3"
    
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Check if setting exists (commented or uncommented)
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${setting_name}[[:space:]]" "$file"; then
            # Replace existing setting (commented or not)
            sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*${setting_name}[[:space:]].*$/${full_setting}/" "$file"
        else
            # Append setting
            echo "$full_setting" >> "$file"
        fi
    else
        # Create file with setting
        echo "$full_setting" > "$file"
    fi
}

if check_include_directive; then
    # Use drop-in configuration file

    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*ClientAliveCountMax[[:space:]]" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then

set -euo pipefail

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
DROP_IN_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/99-client-alive-countmax.conf"

# Ensure the drop-in directory exists
if [[ ! -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"
fi

# Define the desired configuration
DESIRED_SETTING="ClientAliveCountMax 0"

# Function to check if Include directive exists in main config
check_include_directive() {
    grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*Include[[:space:]]+${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/\*\.conf" "$SSHD_CONFIG" 2>/dev/null
}

# Function to update or add setting in a file
update_setting_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local setting_name="$2"
    local full_setting="$3"
    
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        # Check if setting exists (commented or uncommented)
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*#?[[:space:]]*${setting_name}[[:space:]]" "$file"; then
            # Replace existing setting (commented or not)
            sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*${setting_name}[[:space:]].*$/${full_setting}/" "$file"
        else
            # Append setting
            echo "$full_setting" >> "$file"
        fi
    else
        # Create file with setting
        echo "$full_setting" > "$file"
    fi
}

# Check if RHEL 8 uses drop-in config directory (it does by default)
if check_include_directive; then
    # Use drop-in configuration file
    # First, comment out any existing ClientAliveCountMax in main config
    if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*ClientAliveCountMax[[:space:]]" "$SSHD_CONFIG"; then
        sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*ClientAliveCountMax[[:space:]]/#ClientAliveCountMax /' "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    fi
    
    # Create or update drop-in file
    echo "$DESIRED_SETTING" > "$DROP_IN_FILE"
    chmod 600 "$DROP_IN_FILE"
else
    # Modify main sshd_config directly
    update_setting_in_file "$SSHD_CONFIG" "ClientAliveCountMax" "$DESIRED_SETTING"
fi

# Validate sshd configuration
if ! sshd -t; then

    exit 1
fi

systemctl enable sshd

#!/bin/bash

# AIDE configuration for audit tools on RHEL 8
# This script ensures audit tool binaries are monitored by AIDE for integrity verification

set -e

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Install AIDE if not already installed
if ! rpm -q aide &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y aide
fi

# Define the AIDE configuration file for audit tools
AIDE_AUDIT_CONF="/etc/aide.conf.d/audit_tools.conf"
AIDE_MAIN_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Create the aide.conf.d directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/aide.conf.d

# Define the audit tools to monitor
AUDIT_TOOLS=(
    "/usr/sbin/auditctl"
    "/usr/sbin/auditd"
    "/usr/sbin/ausearch"
    "/usr/sbin/aureport"
    "/usr/sbin/autrace"
    "/usr/sbin/augenrules"
    "/usr/sbin/rsyslogd"
    "/usr/bin/auvirt"
)

# Define the AIDE rule for audit tools
# This rule checks: permissions (p), inode (i), number of links (n), user (u), group (g),
# size (s), md5 hash (md5), sha256 hash (sha256), sha512 hash (sha512), access control lists (acl),
# selinux context (selinux), and extended attributes (xattrs)
AIDE_AUDIT_RULE="AUDIT_TOOLS = p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512"

# Create or update the audit tools AIDE configuration
cat > "${AIDE_AUDIT_CONF}" << 'EOF'
# AIDE configuration for audit tool integrity monitoring
# This file ensures cryptographic verification of audit tool binaries
# covering permissions, ownership, and content hashes

# Define the rule for audit tools monitoring
# p = permissions
# i = inode
# n = number of links
# u = user
# g = group
# s = size
# b = block count
# acl = access control lists
# xattrs = extended attributes
# sha512 = SHA-512 hash for content integrity

AUDIT_TOOLS = p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512

# Audit tool binaries to monitor
/usr/sbin/auditctl AUDIT_TOOLS
/usr/sbin/auditd AUDIT_TOOLS
/usr/sbin/ausearch AUDIT_TOOLS
/usr/sbin/aureport AUDIT_TOOLS
/usr/sbin/autrace AUDIT_TOOLS
/usr/sbin/augenrules AUDIT_TOOLS
/usr/sbin/rsyslogd AUDIT_TOOLS
/usr/bin/auvirt AUDIT_TOOLS
EOF

# Ensure the main aide.conf includes the aide.conf.d directory
# Check if the @@include directive for aide.conf.d already exists
if ! grep -q "@@include /etc/aide.conf.d/" "${AIDE_MAIN_CONF}" 2>/dev/null; then
    # Add include directive at the end of the main config
    echo "" >> "${AIDE_MAIN_CONF}"
    echo "# Include additional configuration files from aide.conf.d" >> "${AIDE_MAIN_CONF}"
    echo "@@include /etc/aide.conf.d/" >> "${AIDE_MAIN_CONF}"
fi

# Alternatively, check for and add individual audit tool entries directly to aide.conf
# if the include mechanism doesn't work properly on this system
# This ensures idempotency by checking if entries already exist

add_aide_entry() {
    local tool_path="$1"
    local config_file="${AIDE_MAIN_CONF}"
    
    # Check if the tool exists
    if [[ -f "${tool_path}" ]]; then
        # Check if already configured in main config (not in comments)
        if ! grep -E "^${tool_path}[[:space:]]+" "${config_file}" &>/dev/null; then
            # Check if AUDIT_TOOLS rule is defined in main config
            if ! grep -E "^AUDIT_TOOLS[[:space:]]*=" "${config_file}" &>/dev/null; then
                # Add the rule definition before adding entries
                echo "" >> "${config_file}"
                echo "# Rule for audit tools integrity monitoring" >> "${config_file}"
                echo "AUDIT_TOOLS = p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512" >> "${config_file}"
            fi
            echo "${tool_path} AUDIT_TOOLS" >> "${config_file}"
        fi
    fi
}

# Add each audit tool to the main AIDE configuration if not already present
for tool in "${AUDIT_TOOLS[@]}"; do
    add_aide_entry "${tool}"
done

# Set proper permissions on AIDE configuration files
chmod 600 "${AIDE_AUDIT_CONF}"
chown root:root "${AIDE_AUDIT_CONF}"

# Initialize or update the AIDE database if it doesn't exist
AIDE_DB="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz"
AIDE_DB_NEW="/var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz"

if [[ ! -f "${AIDE_DB}" ]]; then
    echo "Initializing AIDE database..."
    aide --init
    
    # Move the new database to be the current database
    if [[ -f "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" ]]; then
        mv "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" "${AIDE_DB}"
    fi
else
    echo "AIDE database already exists. Running update to incorporate new configuration..."
    # Update the database with the new configuration
    aide --init
    
    # Backup old database and use new one
    if [[ -f "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" ]]; then
        mv "${AIDE_DB}" "${AIDE_DB}.bak"
        mv "${AIDE_DB_NEW}" "${AIDE_DB}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure AIDE check can be run via cron or systemd timer for persistence
# Create a systemd timer for daily AIDE checks if it doesn't exist
AIDE_SERVICE="/etc/systemd/system/aide-check.service"
AIDE_TIMER="/etc/systemd/system/aide-check.timer"

if [[ ! -f "${AIDE_SERVICE}" ]]; then
    cat > "${AIDE_SERVICE}" << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=AIDE File Integrity Check
After=local-fs.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/aide --check
Nice=19
IOSchedulingClass=idle

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
fi

if [[ ! -f "${AIDE_TIMER}" ]]; then
    cat > "${AIDE_TIMER}" << 'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Daily AIDE File Integrity Check

[Timer]
OnCalendar=daily
Persistent=true
RandomizedDelaySec=1800

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
EOF
fi

# Reload systemd and enable the timer
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable aide-check.timer
systemctl start aide-check.timer

echo "AIDE configuration for audit tools completed successfully."
echo "Audit tools are now monitored for integrity verification."
echo "The following tools are being tracked:"
for tool in "${AUDIT_TOOLS[@]}"; do
    if [[ -f "${tool}" ]]; then
        echo "  - ${tool}"
    fi
done
echo ""
echo "AIDE will perform daily integrity checks via systemd timer."
echo "To manually check integrity, run: aide --check"

# Idempotent script to configure password-quality policy on RHEL 8
# Sets maxclassrepeat=4 to reject passwords with more than 4 consecutive
# characters from the same character class

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
SETTING="maxclassrepeat"
VALUE="4"

# Ensure the pwquality.conf file exists
if [[ ! -f "$PWQUALITY_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $PWQUALITY_CONF does not exist"
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the setting already exists in the file
if grep -qE "^\s*${SETTING}\s*=" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    # Setting exists, update it to the correct value
    sed -i "s/^\s*${SETTING}\s*=.*/${SETTING} = ${VALUE}/" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
elif grep -qE "^\s*#\s*${SETTING}\s*=" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    # Setting exists but is commented out, uncomment and set the value
    sed -i "s/^\s*#\s*${SETTING}\s*=.*/${SETTING} = ${VALUE}/" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
else
    # Setting doesn't exist, append it to the file
    echo "${SETTING} = ${VALUE}" >> "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
fi

# Verify the change was applied correctly
if grep -qE "^\s*${SETTING}\s*=\s*${VALUE}\s*$" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    echo "Successfully configured ${SETTING} = ${VALUE} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: Failed to configure ${SETTING} in ${PWQUALITY_CONF}"
    exit 1
fi

# for the automated crash-reporting framework (ABRT)

# Define the audit rule for modprobe
AUDIT_RULE="-a always,exit -F path=/usr/sbin/modprobe -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged-command"

# Define the rules file path
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged-command-modprobe.rules"

# Function to check if a rule exists in loaded rules
rule_loaded() {
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF "path=/usr/sbin/modprobe" && \
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep "path=/usr/sbin/modprobe" | grep -qF "perm=x" && \
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep "path=/usr/sbin/modprobe" | grep -qF "auid>=1000" && \
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep "path=/usr/sbin/modprobe" | grep -qE "auid!=-1|auid!=unset|auid!=4294967295" && \
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep "path=/usr/sbin/modprobe" | grep -qF "key=privileged-command"
}

# Function to check if the rule exists in the rules file
rule_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        grep -qE "^-a\s+(always,exit|exit,always)\s+.*-F\s+path=/usr/sbin/modprobe\s+.*-F\s+perm=x\s+.*-F\s+auid>=1000\s+.*-F\s+auid!=(unset|-1|4294967295)\s+.*-k\s+privileged-command" "$file" 2>/dev/null

    fi
    return 1
}

fi

# Regenerate rules using augenrules if available
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then

    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
fi

    echo "Rules file exists: $RULES_FILE"
    echo "Content:"
    cat "$RULES_FILE"
else
    echo "Rules file not found at $RULES_FILE"
fi

echo ""

# Check loaded rules
echo "Loaded rules for modprobe:"
auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep "modprobe" || echo "No modprobe rules currently loaded"

echo ""
echo "Remediation complete."

AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Check if AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &>/dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Installing..."
    dnf install -y aide
fi

# Check if aide.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $AIDE_CONF does not exist"
    exit 1
fi

# Create a backup of the original file
cp -p "$AIDE_CONF" "${AIDE_CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Function to check if xattrs is already in a ruleset definition
check_xattrs_in_rule() {
    local rule_value="$1"
    # Check if xattrs is present in the rule (as a word boundary)
    if echo "$rule_value" | grep -qE '(^|\+)xattrs($|\+)'; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Create a temporary file for processing
TEMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
trap "rm -f $TEMP_FILE" EXIT

# Process the aide.conf file
# We need to identify ruleset definitions which are in the format:
# RULENAME = value1+value2+value3
# And add xattrs if not present

while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
    # Skip empty lines and comments
    if [[ -z "$line" ]] || [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]]; then
        echo "$line" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
        continue
    fi
    
    # Check if this is a ruleset definition (UPPERCASE_NAME = something with + signs)
    # AIDE rulesets are typically uppercase and contain attribute flags joined by +
    # They should not start with / (which would be a file/directory rule)
    # and should not be special directives like @@define
    if [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*)[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*(.+)$ ]]; then
        rulename="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        rulevalue="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        
        # Strip trailing whitespace and comments from rulevalue
        rulevalue_clean=$(echo "$rulevalue" | sed 's/[[:space:]]*#.*$//' | sed 's/[[:space:]]*$//')
        
        # Check if this looks like an attribute ruleset (contains + or known AIDE attributes)
        # Known AIDE attributes include: p, i, n, u, g, s, b, m, c, S, md5, sha256, sha512, etc.
        # We check if it contains + or if it's a combination of known attribute letters
        if [[ "$rulevalue_clean" =~ \+ ]] || [[ "$rulevalue_clean" =~ ^[pinugsbmcSaclMHRE0-9]+$ ]] || \
           [[ "$rulevalue_clean" =~ (sha|md5|rmd|tiger|haval|gost|crc32|whirlpool|xattrs|acl|selinux|caps) ]]; then
            
            # This appears to be an attribute ruleset definition
            if ! check_xattrs_in_rule "$rulevalue_clean"; then
                # xattrs not found, add it
                # Preserve any inline comment
                if [[ "$rulevalue" =~ ([[:space:]]*#.*)$ ]]; then
                    comment="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
                    new_line="${rulename} = ${rulevalue_clean}+xattrs${comment}"
                else
                    new_line="${rulename} = ${rulevalue_clean}+xattrs"
                fi
                echo "$new_line" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
                echo "Modified ruleset '$rulename': added xattrs"
            else
                # xattrs already present
                echo "$line" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
            fi
        else
            # Not an attribute ruleset (might be a path variable or other definition)
            echo "$line" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
        fi
    else
        # Not a ruleset definition, keep as is
        echo "$line" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
    fi
done < "$AIDE_CONF"

# Replace the original file with the modified one
mv "$TEMP_FILE" "$AIDE_CONF"

# Restore original permissions
chmod 600 "$AIDE_CONF"
chown root:root "$AIDE_CONF"

# Verify the configuration is valid
echo "Verifying AIDE configuration..."
if aide --config-check &>/dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE configuration is valid."
else
    echo "Warning: AIDE configuration check returned non-zero, but this may be expected if database doesn't exist yet."
fi

echo "Extended attribute checking (xattrs) has been enabled for all rulesets in $AIDE_CONF"
echo "Configuration will persist across reboots."
echo ""
echo "Note: You may need to reinitialize the AIDE database with: aide --init"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to add audit rule for /var/log/lastlog
# This script ensures the audit rule is present for both augenrules and auditctl mechanisms

AUDIT_RULE="-w /var/log/lastlog -p wa -k logins"
AUDIT_RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}/logins.rules"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
if [ ! -d "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Function to check if the rule already exists in a file
rule_exists_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        grep -qF -- "-w /var/log/lastlog" "$file" && \
        grep -F -- "-w /var/log/lastlog" "$file" | grep -qF -- "-p wa" && \
        grep -F -- "-w /var/log/lastlog" "$file" | grep -qF -- "-k logins"
        return $?
    fi
    return 1
}

# Check if the rule already exists in any rules.d file
rule_found=0
for rules_file in "${AUDIT_RULES_DIR}"/*.rules; do
    if [ -f "$rules_file" ]; then
        if rule_exists_in_file "$rules_file"; then
            rule_found=1
            break
        fi
    fi
done

# If rule not found, add it to the logins.rules file
if [ $rule_found -eq 0 ]; then
    # Check if file exists and doesn't end with newline
    if [ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ] && [ -s "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]; then
        # Ensure file ends with newline before appending
        if [ "$(tail -c1 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" | wc -l)" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
        fi
    fi
    echo "${AUDIT_RULE}" >> "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" 2>/dev/null

# Check if the rule is currently loaded in the running audit system
if ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF -- "-w /var/log/lastlog" || \
   ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -F -- "-w /var/log/lastlog" | grep -qF -- "perm=wa" || \
   ! auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -F -- "-w /var/log/lastlog" | grep -qF -- "key=logins"; then
    # Load the rule into the running system
    auditctl -w /var/log/lastlog -p wa -k logins 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Reload audit rules to ensure persistence mechanism is working
# Try augenrules first (preferred on RHEL 8), fall back to service restart

else

    service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart auditd 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure GNOME Display Manager login banner
# for RHEL 8 systems with graphical environment

set -euo pipefail

# Define the approved organizational warning banner text
# This should be customized to match your organization's approved message
BANNER_TEXT="WARNING: This system is for authorized users only. By accessing this system, you consent to monitoring and agree that evidence of unauthorized activity may be provided to law enforcement. Unauthorized use is prohibited and may result in criminal prosecution."

# GDM dconf database directory and file paths
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db"
GDM_PROFILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/gdm"
GDM_DB_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/gdm.d"
BANNER_CONF="${GDM_DB_DIR}/01-banner-message"
LOCKS_DIR="${GDM_DB_DIR}/locks"
LOCKS_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/01-banner-message"

# Function to check if GNOME Display Manager is installed
check_gdm_installed() {
    if ! rpm -q gdm &>/dev/null; then
        echo "GDM is not installed. Exiting."
        exit 0
    fi
}

# Function to create directories if they don't exist
create_directories() {
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"
    mkdir -p "${GDM_DB_DIR}"
    mkdir -p "${LOCKS_DIR}"
}

# Function to configure the GDM dconf profile
configure_gdm_profile() {
    local profile_content="user-db:user
system-db:gdm
file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults"

    if [[ -f "${GDM_PROFILE}" ]]; then
        local current_content
        current_content=$(cat "${GDM_PROFILE}")
        if [[ "${current_content}" == "${profile_content}" ]]; then
            echo "GDM profile already configured correctly."
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    echo "Configuring GDM dconf profile..."
    echo "${profile_content}" > "${GDM_PROFILE}"
    chmod 644 "${GDM_PROFILE}"
}

# Function to configure the banner message settings
configure_banner_settings() {
    # Escape special characters in banner text for dconf
    local escaped_banner_text="${BANNER_TEXT//\'/\'\\\'\'}"
    
    local banner_config="[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${escaped_banner_text}'"

    if [[ -f "${BANNER_CONF}" ]]; then
        local current_content
        current_content=$(cat "${BANNER_CONF}")
        if [[ "${current_content}" == "${banner_config}" ]]; then
            echo "Banner configuration already set correctly."
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    echo "Configuring banner message settings..."
    echo "${banner_config}" > "${BANNER_CONF}"
    chmod 644 "${BANNER_CONF}"
    return 1
}

# Function to configure locks to prevent user override
configure_locks() {
    local locks_content="/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text"

    if [[ -f "${LOCKS_FILE}" ]]; then
        local current_content
        current_content=$(cat "${LOCKS_FILE}")
        if [[ "${current_content}" == "${locks_content}" ]]; then
            echo "Locks already configured correctly."
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    echo "Configuring locks to prevent user override..."
    echo "${locks_content}" > "${LOCKS_FILE}"
    chmod 644 "${LOCKS_FILE}"
    return 1
}

# Function to update the dconf database
update_dconf_database() {
    echo "Updating dconf database..."
    dconf update
}

# Function to verify the configuration
verify_configuration() {
    echo "Verifying configuration..."
    
    # Check if the database was created
    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_DB_DIR}/gdm" ]]; then
        echo "Warning: GDM dconf database not found. Configuration may not be applied."
        return 1
    fi
    
    echo "Configuration verified successfully."
    return 0
}

# Main execution
main() {

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure GNOME Display Manager login banner
# for RHEL 8 systems with graphical environment

set -euo pipefail

# Define the approved organizational warning banner text
# This should be customized to match your organization's approved message
BANNER_TEXT="WARNING: This system is for authorized users only. By accessing this system, you consent to monitoring and agree that evidence of unauthorized activity may be provided to law enforcement. Unauthorized use is prohibited and may result in criminal prosecution."

# GDM dconf database directory and file paths
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db"
GDM_PROFILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/gdm"
GDM_DB_DIR="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/gdm.d"
BANNER_CONF="${GDM_DB_DIR}/01-banner-message"
LOCKS_DIR="${GDM_DB_DIR}/locks"
LOCKS_FILE="${LOCKS_DIR}/01-banner-message"

# Function to check if GNOME Display Manager is installed
check_gdm_installed() {
    if ! rpm -q gdm &>/dev/null; then
        echo "GDM is not installed. Exiting."
        exit 0
    fi
}

# Function to create directories if they don't exist
create_directories() {
    mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"
    mkdir -p "${GDM_DB_DIR}"
    mkdir -p "${LOCKS_DIR}"
}

# Function to configure the GDM dconf profile
configure_gdm_profile() {
    local profile_content="user-db:user
system-db:gdm
file-db:/usr/share/gdm/greeter-dconf-defaults"

    if [[ -f "${GDM_PROFILE}" ]]; then
        local current_content
        current_content=$(cat "${GDM_PROFILE}")
        if [[ "${current_content}" == "${profile_content}" ]]; then
            echo "GDM profile already configured correctly."
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    echo "Configuring GDM dconf profile..."
    echo "${profile_content}" > "${GDM_PROFILE}"
    chmod 644 "${GDM_PROFILE}"
}

# Function to configure the banner message settings
configure_banner_settings() {
    # Escape special characters in banner text for dconf
    local escaped_banner_text="${BANNER_TEXT//\'/\'\\\'\'}"
    
    local banner_config="[org/gnome/login-screen]
banner-message-enable=true
banner-message-text='${escaped_banner_text}'"

    if [[ -f "${BANNER_CONF}" ]]; then
        local current_content
        current_content=$(cat "${BANNER_CONF}")
        if [[ "${current_content}" == "${banner_config}" ]]; then
            echo "Banner configuration already set correctly."
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    echo "Configuring banner message settings..."
    echo "${banner_config}" > "${BANNER_CONF}"
    chmod 644 "${BANNER_CONF}"
    return 1
}

# Function to configure locks to prevent user override
configure_locks() {
    local locks_content="/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-enable
/org/gnome/login-screen/banner-message-text"

    if [[ -f "${LOCKS_FILE}" ]]; then
        local current_content
        current_content=$(cat "${LOCKS_FILE}")
        if [[ "${current_content}" == "${locks_content}" ]]; then
            echo "Locks already configured correctly."
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    echo "Configuring locks to prevent user override..."
    echo "${locks_content}" > "${LOCKS_FILE}"
    chmod 644 "${LOCKS_FILE}"
    return 1
}

# Function to update the dconf database
update_dconf_database() {
    echo "Updating dconf database..."
    dconf update
}

# Function to verify the configuration
verify_configuration() {
    echo "Verifying configuration..."
    
    # Check if the database was created
    if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_DB_DIR}/gdm" ]]; then
        echo "Warning: GDM dconf database not found. Configuration may not be applied."
        return 1
    fi
    
    echo "Configuration verified successfully."
    return 0
}

# Main execution
main() {
    echo "Starting GNOME Display Manager login banner configuration..."
    
    # Check if running as root
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root."
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check if GDM is installed
    check_gdm_installed
    
    # Create necessary directories
    create_directories
    
    # Track if any changes were made
    local changes_made=false
    
    # Configure GDM profile
    if ! configure_gdm_profile; then
        changes_made=true
    fi
    
    # Configure banner settings
    if ! configure_banner_settings; then
        changes_made=true
    fi
    
    # Configure locks
    if ! configure_locks; then
        changes_made=true
    fi
    
    # Update dconf database (always run to ensure consistency)
    update_dconf_database
    
    # Verify configuration
    verify_configuration
    
    if [[ "${changes_made}" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "Configuration changes applied successfully."
    else
        echo "System already configured correctly. No changes needed."
    fi
    
    echo "GNOME Display Manager login banner configuration complete."
    echo "The banner will be displayed on the GDM login screen."
    echo "Changes will persist across reboots."
}

# Run main function
main

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to set minimum password length to 15 characters on RHEL 8

MINLEN=15
PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
LOGIN_DEFS="/etc/login.defs"

# Function to set or update a parameter in a config file
set_config_param() {
    local file="$1"
    local param="$2"
    local value="$3"
    local delimiter="$4"
    
    if grep -q "^${param}${delimiter}" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Parameter exists and is not commented, update it
        sed -i "s/^${param}${delimiter}.*/${param}${delimiter}${value}/" "$file"
    elif grep -q "^#.*${param}${delimiter}" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Parameter exists but is commented, uncomment and update it
        sed -i "s/^#.*${param}${delimiter}.*/${param}${delimiter}${value}/" "$file"
    else
        # Parameter doesn't exist, append it
        echo "${param}${delimiter}${value}" >> "$file"
    fi
}

# Configure pwquality.conf for PAM-based password quality checks
if [ -f "$PWQUALITY_CONF" ]; then
    set_config_param "$PWQUALITY_CONF" "minlen" "$MINLEN" " = "
else
    echo "minlen = $MINLEN" > "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
fi

# Configure login.defs for useradd defaults
if [ -f "$LOGIN_DEFS" ]; then
    set_config_param "$LOGIN_DEFS" "PASS_MIN_LEN" "$MINLEN" "	"
fi

# Verify the changes
echo "Password minimum length configuration applied:"
echo "---"
echo "pwquality.conf minlen setting:"
grep -E "^minlen" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" 2>/dev/null || echo "Not found in $PWQUALITY_CONF"
echo "---"
echo "login.defs PASS_MIN_LEN setting:"
grep -E "^PASS_MIN_LEN" "$LOGIN_DEFS" 2>/dev/null || echo "Not found in $LOGIN_DEFS"

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure auditing for ssh-keysign execution
# on RHEL 8 systems

set -e

# Find the ssh-keysign binary path
SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH=$(which ssh-keysign 2>/dev/null || find /usr /lib -name ssh-keysign -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)

if [ -z "$SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: ssh-keysign not found on this system"
    exit 1
fi

# Define the audit rule
AUDIT_RULE="-a always,exit -F path=${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH} -F perm=x -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k privileged"

# Function to check if rule exists in a file
rule_exists_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Check for the rule, accounting for variations in whitespace
        grep -q "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]].*always,exit.*-F[[:space:]]*path=${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH}.*-F[[:space:]]*perm=x.*-F[[:space:]]*auid>=1000.*-F[[:space:]]*auid!=unset.*-k[[:space:]]*privileged" "$file" 2>/dev/null || \
        grep -q "^[[:space:]]*-a[[:space:]].*always,exit.*-F[[:space:]]*path=${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH}.*-F[[:space:]]*perm=x.*-F[[:space:]]*auid>=1000.*-F[[:space:]]*auid!=-1.*-k[[:space:]]*privileged" "$file" 2>/dev/null
        return $?
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to check if rule is currently loaded in kernel
rule_loaded_in_kernel() {
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "path=${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH}.*perm=x.*auid>=1000.*auid!=-1.*key=privileged" || \
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "path=${SSH_KEYSIGN_PATH}.*perm=x.*auid>=1000.*auid!=unset.*key=privileged"
    return $?
}

# Ensure audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Installing audit package..."
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Ensure auditd is enabled
systemctl enable auditd

# Create audit rules directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Define the rules file for privileged commands
RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/privileged.rules"

# Check if rule already exists in any rules.d file
RULE_EXISTS=false
for file in /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules; do
    if rule_exists_in_file "$file"; then
        RULE_EXISTS=true
        echo "Rule already exists in $file"
        break
    fi
done

# Add rule to rules file if not present
if [ "$RULE_EXISTS" = false ]; then
    echo "Adding audit rule to ${RULES_FILE}..."
    echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> "$RULES_FILE"
fi

# Also add to /etc/audit/audit.rules for systems that use this directly
# (older auditd configurations)
if [ -f /etc/audit/audit.rules ]; then
    if ! rule_exists_in_file /etc/audit/audit.rules; then
        # Insert before any -e 2 line (which locks the rules)
        if grep -q "^-e 2" /etc/audit/audit.rules; then
            sed -i "/^-e 2/i $AUDIT_RULE" /etc/audit/audit.rules
        else
            echo "$AUDIT_RULE" >> /etc/audit/audit.rules
        fi
    fi
fi

# Generate rules from rules.d if augenrules is available
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Running augenrules to compile rules..."
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Load the rule immediately into the running kernel if not already loaded
if ! rule_loaded_in_kernel; then
    echo "Loading rule into kernel..."
    auditctl $AUDIT_RULE 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Restart auditd to ensure all rules are properly loaded
# Note: auditd requires service command instead of systemctl for restart on RHEL
echo "Restarting auditd service..."
service auditd restart 2>/dev/null || systemctl restart auditd 2>/dev/null || true

# Verify the rule is loaded
echo "Verifying rule is loaded..."
if rule_loaded_in_kernel; then
    echo "SUCCESS: Audit rule for ssh-keysign is active"
    auditctl -l | grep "ssh-keysign"
else
    echo "WARNING: Rule may not be immediately visible, but will be loaded on next auditd restart"
fi

echo "Configuration complete. The audit rule will persist across reboots."

# Check if chrony.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "$CHRONY_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $CHRONY_CONF does not exist"
    exit 1
fi

# Check if cmdport 0 is already configured (idempotency check)
if grep -q "^cmdport 0$" "$CHRONY_CONF"; then
    echo "cmdport 0 is already configured in $CHRONY_CONF"
else
    # Remove any existing cmdport directives (commented or uncommented)
    sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*cmdport/d' "$CHRONY_CONF"
    
    # Add cmdport 0 to disable the command port
    echo "cmdport 0" >> "$CHRONY_CONF"
    echo "Added 'cmdport 0' to $CHRONY_CONF"
fi

# Restart chronyd to apply changes
if systemctl is-active --quiet chronyd; then
    systemctl restart chronyd
    echo "chronyd service restarted"
elif systemctl is-enabled --quiet chronyd 2>/dev/null; then
    systemctl start chronyd
    echo "chronyd service started"
fi

if chronyc -n cmdport 2>&1 | grep -q "506 Cannot talk to daemon"; then
    echo "Verification: Command port is disabled (cannot connect to daemon via network)"
elif chronyc -c tracking >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Verification: chronyd is running (command port disabled for network access)"
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure audit rules for file deletion via rename syscall
# Covers both 32-bit and 64-bit syscalls, for UIDs >= 1000, excluding unset UIDs

set -e

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/delete.rules"

# Define the audit rules for rename syscall
# -a always,exit: Add rule to syscall exit list
# -F arch=b32/b64: Architecture filter
# -S rename: The rename syscall
# -F auid>=1000: Audit UID is 1000 or greater (regular users and root actions on behalf of users)
# -F auid!=unset (or auid!=-1): Exclude processes with unset audit UID
# -k delete: Key for searching/grouping these events

RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S rename -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"
RULE_64BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"

# Create or update the rules file
# Using a dedicated file in rules.d for better organization

# Check if rules already exist and are correct
RULES_NEEDED=0

if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]]; then
    # Check if both rules are present
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_32BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        RULES_NEEDED=1
    fi
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_64BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        RULES_NEEDED=1
    fi
else
    RULES_NEEDED=1
fi

if [[ $RULES_NEEDED -eq 1 ]]; then
    # Create the rules file with proper content
    cat > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" << EOF
## Audit rules for file deletion via rename syscall
## Generated by remediation script

# 32-bit syscall rule for rename
$RULE_32BIT

# 64-bit syscall rule for rename
$RULE_64BIT
EOF

    # Set proper permissions on the rules file
    chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    chown root:root "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

    echo "Audit rules file created/updated: $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
else
    echo "Audit rules already configured correctly in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Also check and add rules to /etc/audit/audit.rules if it exists and is used
# This ensures compatibility with systems that may read from this file directly
AUDIT_RULES_MAIN="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN" ]]; then
    MAIN_UPDATED=0
    
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_32BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$RULE_32BIT" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN"
        MAIN_UPDATED=1
    fi
    
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_64BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$RULE_64BIT" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN"
        MAIN_UPDATED=1
    fi
    
    if [[ $MAIN_UPDATED -eq 1 ]]; then
        echo "Updated $AUDIT_RULES_MAIN with rename audit rules"
    fi
fi

# Load the rules into the running kernel
# Use augenrules to merge rules from rules.d and load them
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
    echo "Audit rules loaded using augenrules"
else
    # Fallback to auditctl if augenrules is not available
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    echo "Audit rules loaded using auditctl"
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded
echo ""
echo "Verifying loaded audit rules for rename syscall:"
auditctl -l | grep -E "rename.*delete" || echo "Warning: Rules may not have loaded correctly"

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running
systemctl enable auditd 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl is-active --quiet auditd || systemctl start auditd

echo ""
echo "Audit configuration for file deletion (rename) complete."
echo "Rules will persist across reboots via $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# -F auid!=unset (or auid!=-1): Exclude processes with unset audit UID
# -k delete: Key for searching/grouping these events

RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S rename -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"
RULE_64BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"

# Create or update the rules file
# Using a dedicated file in rules.d for better organization

# Check if rules already exist and are correct
RULES_NEEDED=0

if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]]; then
    # Check if both rules are present
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_32BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        RULES_NEEDED=1
    fi
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_64BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        RULES_NEEDED=1
    fi
else
    RULES_NEEDED=1
fi

if [[ $RULES_NEEDED -eq 1 ]]; then
    # Create the rules file with proper content
    cat > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" << EOF
## Audit rules for file deletion via rename syscall
## Generated by remediation script

# 32-bit syscall rule for rename
$RULE_32BIT

# 64-bit syscall rule for rename
$RULE_64BIT
EOF

    # Set proper permissions on the rules file
    chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    chown root:root "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

    echo "Audit rules file created/updated: $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
else
    echo "Audit rules already configured correctly in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Also check and add rules to /etc/audit/audit.rules if it exists and is used
# This ensures compatibility with systems that may read from this file directly
AUDIT_RULES_MAIN="/etc/audit/audit.rules"

if [[ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN" ]]; then
    MAIN_UPDATED=0
    
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_32BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$RULE_32BIT" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN"
        MAIN_UPDATED=1
    fi
    
    if ! grep -qF -- "$RULE_64BIT" "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$RULE_64BIT" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_MAIN"
        MAIN_UPDATED=1
    fi
    
    if [[ $MAIN_UPDATED -eq 1 ]]; then
        echo "Updated $AUDIT_RULES_MAIN with rename audit rules"
    fi
fi

# Load the rules into the running kernel
# Use augenrules to merge rules from rules.d and load them
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
    echo "Audit rules loaded using augenrules"
else
    # Fallback to auditctl if augenrules is not available
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    echo "Audit rules loaded using auditctl"
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded
echo ""
echo "Verifying loaded audit rules for rename syscall:"
auditctl -l | grep -E "rename.*delete" || echo "Warning: Rules may not have loaded correctly"

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running
systemctl enable auditd 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl is-active --quiet auditd || systemctl start auditd

echo ""
echo "Audit configuration for file deletion (rename) complete."
echo "Rules will persist across reboots via $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure persistent account lockout on RHEL 8
# This ensures failed login tallies survive reboots by using pam_faillock with dir=/var/log/faillock

set -euo pipefail

# Ensure we're running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Create the persistent faillock directory if it doesn't exist
FAILLOCK_DIR="/var/log/faillock"
if [[ ! -d "$FAILLOCK_DIR" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$FAILLOCK_DIR"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the faillock directory
chmod 0751 "$FAILLOCK_DIR"
chown root:root "$FAILLOCK_DIR"

# Function to update pam_faillock entries in a PAM file
update_pam_faillock() {
    local pam_file="$1"
    local temp_file
    temp_file=$(mktemp)
    
    if [[ ! -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
        echo "Warning: $pam_file does not exist, skipping" >&2
        return
    fi
    
    # Process the file line by line
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        if echo "$line" | grep -q "pam_faillock.so"; then
            # Check if dir= is already set
            if echo "$line" | grep -q "dir="; then
                # Replace existing dir= parameter with the persistent location
                line=$(echo "$line" | sed -E 's|dir=[^ ]+|dir=/var/log/faillock|g')
            else
                # Add dir= parameter before the first option or at the end
                # Insert dir=/var/log/faillock after pam_faillock.so
                line=$(echo "$line" | sed -E 's|(pam_faillock\.so)|\1 dir=/var/log/faillock|')
            fi
        fi
        echo "$line" >> "$temp_file"
    done < "$pam_file"
    
    # Replace original file with updated content
    cp "$temp_file" "$pam_file"
    rm -f "$temp_file"
    
    # Restore SELinux context if applicable
    if command -v restorecon &>/dev/null; then
        restorecon "$pam_file" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
}

# Check if authselect is being used (RHEL 8 standard)
if command -v authselect &>/dev/null && authselect current &>/dev/null; then
    # Get current profile
    CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "")
    
    if [[ -n "$CURRENT_PROFILE" ]]; then
        echo "Detected authselect profile: $CURRENT_PROFILE"
        
        # Check if using a custom profile or standard profile
        if [[ "$CURRENT_PROFILE" == custom/* ]]; then
            CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME="${CURRENT_PROFILE#custom/}"
            CUSTOM_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/authselect/custom/$CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME"
        else
            # Create a custom profile based on current one if not already custom
            CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME="persistent-faillock"
            CUSTOM_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/authselect/custom/$CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME"
            
            if [[ ! -d "$CUSTOM_PROFILE_DIR" ]]; then
                echo "Creating custom authselect profile: $CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME"
                authselect create-profile "$CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME" -b "$CURRENT_PROFILE" --symlink-meta
            fi
        fi
        
        # Update the custom profile's PAM files
        for pam_template in "$CUSTOM_PROFILE_DIR"/{system-auth,password-auth}; do
            if [[ -f "$pam_template" ]]; then
                echo "Updating $pam_template"
                update_pam_faillock "$pam_template"
            fi
        done
        
        # Get current features
        CURRENT_FEATURES=$(authselect current 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '(?<=with-).+' | tr '\n' ' ' || echo "")
        FEATURE_ARGS=""
        if [[ -n "$CURRENT_FEATURES" ]]; then
            for feature in $CURRENT_FEATURES; do
                FEATURE_ARGS="$FEATURE_ARGS with-$feature"
            done
        fi
        
        # Apply the custom profile
        echo "Applying custom authselect profile"
        if [[ "$CURRENT_PROFILE" != "custom/$CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME" ]]; then
            authselect select "custom/$CUSTOM_PROFILE_NAME" $FEATURE_ARGS --force
        else
            authselect apply-changes
        fi
    fi
else
    # Fallback: Direct PAM file modification (non-authselect systems)
    echo "authselect not in use, modifying PAM files directly"
    
    for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
        if [[ -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
            echo "Updating $pam_file"
            update_pam_faillock "$pam_file"
        fi
    done
fi

# Also check /etc/security/faillock.conf (RHEL 8.2+)
FAILLOCK_CONF="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
if [[ -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Updating $FAILLOCK_CONF"
    
    # Check if dir is already configured
    if grep -qE "^\s*dir\s*=" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        # Update existing dir setting
        sed -i -E 's|^\s*dir\s*=.*|dir = /var/log/faillock|' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    elif grep -qE "^\s*#\s*dir\s*=" "$FAILLOCK_CONF"; then
        # Uncomment and update existing commented dir setting
        sed -i -E 's|^\s*#\s*dir\s*=.*|dir = /var/log/faillock|' "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    else
        # Add dir setting
        echo "dir = /var/log/faillock" >> "$FAILLOCK_CONF"
    fi
fi

# Restore SELinux context on the faillock directory
if command -v restorecon &>/dev/null; then
    restorecon -R "$FAILLOCK_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
echo "============="
echo "Faillock directory: $FAILLOCK_DIR"
ls -ld "$FAILLOCK_DIR"

if [[ -f "$FAILLOCK_CONF" ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Faillock.conf dir setting:"
    grep -E "^\s*dir\s*=" "$FAILLOCK_CONF" || echo "Not found in faillock.conf"
fi

echo ""
echo "PAM configuration check:"
for pam_file in /etc/pam.d/system-auth /etc/pam.d/password-auth; do
    if [[ -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
        echo "  $pam_file:"
        grep "pam_faillock" "$pam_file" | head -4 | sed 's/^/    /'
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "Persistent faillock configuration complete."
echo "Failed login attempts will now be recorded in $FAILLOCK_DIR and survive reboots."

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to make audit rules immutable on RHEL 8
# The -e 2 flag locks the audit configuration, requiring a reboot to change rules

set -euo pipefail

# Define the immutable rule
IMMUTABLE_RULE="-e 2"

# On RHEL 8, auditd uses augenrules by default which reads from /etc/audit/rules.d/
# The rules are processed in alphabetical order, so we need to ensure -e 2 is loaded last
# Using a filename starting with 99- ensures it's processed last

RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
IMMUTABLE_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/99-finalize.rules"

# Ensure the rules directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${RULES_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"
    chmod 750 "${RULES_DIR}"
fi

# Function to check if immutable rule already exists in the correct file
check_immutable_rule() {
    if [[ -f "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ]]; then
        if grep -q "^-e 2$" "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to remove -e 2 from other rules files to avoid duplicates and ensure proper ordering
remove_duplicate_immutable_rules() {
    for rules_file in "${RULES_DIR}"/*.rules; do
        if [[ -f "${rules_file}" ]] && [[ "${rules_file}" != "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ]]; then
            # Remove any -e 2 lines from other files
            if grep -q "^-e 2$" "${rules_file}" 2>/dev/null; then
                sed -i '/^-e 2$/d' "${rules_file}"
            fi
        fi
    done
}

# Main logic
echo "Configuring audit rules to be immutable..."

# Remove any -e 2 rules from other files to prevent duplicates and ordering issues
remove_duplicate_immutable_rules

# Check if the immutable rule is already properly configured
if check_immutable_rule; then
    echo "Audit immutable rule is already configured in ${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
else
    # Create or update the finalize rules file with the immutable flag
    # The -e 2 must be the last rule loaded
    echo "Adding immutable rule to ${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
    
    # If file exists but doesn't have the rule, append it
    # If file doesn't exist, create it with the rule
    if [[ -f "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ]]; then
        # Remove any existing -e entries to avoid conflicts
        sed -i '/^-e [0-9]$/d' "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
        # Append the immutable rule at the end
        echo "${IMMUTABLE_RULE}" >> "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
    else
        # Create the file with the immutable rule
        echo "## This file locks the audit configuration" > "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
        echo "## Audit configuration can only be changed after a reboot" >> "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
        echo "${IMMUTABLE_RULE}" >> "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"

# Also check /etc/audit/audit.rules for systems that might use auditctl directly
# This is a generated file on RHEL 8 when using augenrules, but we ensure consistency
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    # Check if this system uses augenrules (default on RHEL 8)
    if grep -q "^USE_AUGENRULES=\"yes\"" /etc/sysconfig/auditd 2>/dev/null || \
       [[ ! -f /etc/sysconfig/auditd ]]; then
        # augenrules is used, the audit.rules file will be regenerated
        echo "System uses augenrules - rules will be regenerated from ${RULES_DIR}/"
    fi
fi

# Regenerate the audit rules to include our changes
# This loads the rules from /etc/audit/rules.d/ and creates /etc/audit/audit.rules
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Regenerating audit rules with augenrules..."
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
    # Note: If rules are already locked, the load will fail, which is expected behavior
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
if [[ -f "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ]] && grep -q "^-e 2$" "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"; then
    echo "✓ Immutable rule (-e 2) is configured in ${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
else
    echo "✗ Failed to configure immutable rule"
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the rule is in the generated audit.rules file
if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]] && grep -q "^-e 2$" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
    echo "✓ Immutable rule is present in generated ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Check current audit status
echo ""
echo "Current audit status:"
if command -v auditctl &>/dev/null; then
    CURRENT_FLAG=$(auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^enabled" | awk '{print $2}' || echo "unknown")
    case "${CURRENT_FLAG}" in
        2)
            echo "✓ Audit rules are currently LOCKED (immutable mode active)"
            echo "  A system reboot is required to change audit rules"
            ;;
        1)
            echo "○ Audit is enabled but rules are NOT yet locked"
            echo "  Rules will become immutable after next reboot or auditd restart"
            ;;
        0)
            echo "○ Audit is disabled"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "○ Could not determine current audit status"
            ;;
    esac
fi

echo ""
echo "Configuration complete. The audit rules will be immutable after the next"
echo "auditd service restart or system reboot."

exit 0

# Check if the immutable rule is already properly configured

    echo "Audit immutable rule is already configured in ${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
else
    # Create or update the finalize rules file with the immutable flag
    # The -e 2 must be the last rule loaded
    echo "Adding immutable rule to ${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
    
    # If file exists but doesn't have the rule, append it
    # If file doesn't exist, create it with the rule
    if [[ -f "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ]]; then
        # Remove any existing -e entries to avoid conflicts
        sed -i '/^-e [0-9]$/d' "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
        # Append the immutable rule at the end
        echo "${IMMUTABLE_RULE}" >> "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
    else
        # Create the file with the immutable rule
        echo "## This file locks the audit configuration" > "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
        echo "## Audit configuration can only be changed after a reboot" >> "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
        echo "${IMMUTABLE_RULE}" >> "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
    fi
fi

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"

# Also check /etc/audit/audit.rules for systems that might use auditctl directly
# This is a generated file on RHEL 8 when using augenrules, but we ensure consistency
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/audit.rules"
if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]]; then
    # Check if this system uses augenrules (default on RHEL 8)
    if grep -q "^USE_AUGENRULES=\"yes\"" /etc/sysconfig/auditd 2>/dev/null || \
       [[ ! -f /etc/sysconfig/auditd ]]; then
        # augenrules is used, the audit.rules file will be regenerated
        echo "System uses augenrules - rules will be regenerated from ${RULES_DIR}/"
    fi
fi

# Regenerate the audit rules to include our changes
# This loads the rules from /etc/audit/rules.d/ and creates /etc/audit/audit.rules
if command -v augenrules &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Regenerating audit rules with augenrules..."
    augenrules --load 2>/dev/null || true
    # Note: If rules are already locked, the load will fail, which is expected behavior
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
if [[ -f "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}" ]] && grep -q "^-e 2$" "${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"; then
    echo "✓ Immutable rule (-e 2) is configured in ${IMMUTABLE_FILE}"
else
    echo "✗ Failed to configure immutable rule"
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the rule is in the generated audit.rules file
if [[ -f "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}" ]] && grep -q "^-e 2$" "${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"; then
    echo "✓ Immutable rule is present in generated ${AUDIT_RULES_FILE}"
fi

# Check current audit status
echo ""
echo "Current audit status:"
if command -v auditctl &>/dev/null; then
    CURRENT_FLAG=$(auditctl -s 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^enabled" | awk '{print $2}' || echo "unknown")
    case "${CURRENT_FLAG}" in
        2)
            echo "✓ Audit rules are currently LOCKED (immutable mode active)"
            echo "  A system reboot is required to change audit rules"
            ;;
        1)
            echo "○ Audit is enabled but rules are NOT yet locked"
            echo "  Rules will become immutable after next reboot or auditd restart"
            ;;
        0)
            echo "○ Audit is disabled"
            ;;
        *)
            echo "○ Could not determine current audit status"
            ;;
    esac
fi

echo ""
echo "Configuration complete. The audit rules will be immutable after the next"
echo "auditd service restart or system reboot."

exit 0
```

# This script configures pwquality to require at least one numeric digit

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"
PWQUALITY_CONF_D="/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d"
CUSTOM_CONF="${PWQUALITY_CONF_D}/50-dcredit.conf"

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure pwquality.conf.d directory exists
if [[ ! -d "$PWQUALITY_CONF_D" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$PWQUALITY_CONF_D"
    chmod 755 "$PWQUALITY_CONF_D"
fi

# Function to set dcredit in main config file
set_dcredit_main_conf() {
    if grep -qE "^\s*dcredit\s*=" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Update existing dcredit line
        sed -i 's/^\s*dcredit\s*=.*/dcredit = -1/' "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    else
        # Add dcredit line if not present
        echo "dcredit = -1" >> "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    fi
}

# Function to remove any conflicting dcredit settings in conf.d
cleanup_confd() {
    # Remove any existing dcredit settings from conf.d files to avoid conflicts
    if [[ -d "$PWQUALITY_CONF_D" ]]; then
        for conf_file in "$PWQUALITY_CONF_D"/*.conf; do
            if [[ -f "$conf_file" ]]; then
                if grep -qE "^\s*dcredit\s*=" "$conf_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                    sed -i '/^\s*dcredit\s*=/d' "$conf_file"
                    # Remove file if empty
                    if [[ ! -s "$conf_file" ]]; then
                        rm -f "$conf_file"
                    fi
                fi
            fi
        done
    fi
}

# First, clean up any conflicting settings in conf.d
cleanup_confd

# Set dcredit in the main configuration file
set_dcredit_main_conf

if grep -qE "^\s*dcredit\s*=\s*-1\s*$" "$PWQUALITY_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Successfully configured password policy to require at least one digit (dcredit = -1)"
else
    echo "Warning: Configuration may not have been applied correctly" >&2
    echo "Current dcredit settings:"
    grep -rE "^\s*dcredit" /etc/security/pwquality.conf /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No dcredit setting found"
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure proper permissions on the config file
chmod 644 "$PWQUALITY_CONF"

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
   echo "This script must be run as root" 
   exit 1
fi

# Define the dconf database directory and files
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"

# Define the profile file
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/user"

# Define the settings file and locks file
DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
DCONF_LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/screensaver"

# Create necessary directories if they don't exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# Create or update the dconf profile to use local database
# The profile should include the local database before the user database
if [[ -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]]; then
    # Check if the profile already contains the necessary entries
    if ! grep -q "^user-db:user$" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" || ! grep -q "^system-db:local$" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        # Backup existing profile
        cp "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}.bak"
        # Create proper profile with user-db first, then system-db
        cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
    fi
else
    # Create new profile
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
fi

# Set proper permissions on the profile file
chmod 644 "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"

# Create or update the screensaver settings file
# Setting idle-delay to 900 seconds (15 minutes) as a reasonable default
# This can be adjusted based on organizational policy
cat > "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" << 'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/session]
idle-delay=uint32 900
EOF

# Set proper permissions on the settings file
chmod 644 "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"

# Create or update the locks file to prevent users from changing the setting
cat > "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}" << 'EOF'
/org/gnome/desktop/session/idle-delay
EOF

# Set proper permissions on the locks file
chmod 644 "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"

# Update the dconf database to apply the changes
dconf update

# Verify the configuration
echo "Configuration applied successfully."
echo ""
echo "Settings file (${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}):"
cat "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
echo ""
echo "Locks file (${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}):"
cat "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"
echo ""
echo "Profile file (${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}):"
cat "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
echo ""
echo "The session idle-delay setting is now locked at the system level."
echo "Users will not be able to override this setting through their preferences."

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to enforce password quality policy on RHEL 8
# Ensures passwords cannot contain more than 3 consecutive identical characters

PWQUALITY_CONF="/etc/security/pwquality.conf"

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Check if pwquality.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "$PWQUALITY_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $PWQUALITY_CONF does not exist" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# The maxrepeat parameter controls the maximum number of consecutive identical characters
# Setting maxrepeat=3 means a maximum of 3 consecutive identical characters is allowed
# (i.e., 4 or more consecutive identical characters will be rejected)

# Check if maxrepeat is already set correctly
if grep -q "^maxrepeat[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*3[[:space:]]*$" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    echo "maxrepeat is already set to 3 in $PWQUALITY_CONF"
    exit 0
fi

# Check if maxrepeat exists but with different value (commented or uncommented)
if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*maxrepeat[[:space:]]*=" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    # Replace existing maxrepeat line (whether commented or not)
    sed -i 's/^[[:space:]]*#*[[:space:]]*maxrepeat[[:space:]]*=.*/maxrepeat = 3/' "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    echo "Updated maxrepeat to 3 in $PWQUALITY_CONF"
else
    # Add maxrepeat setting to the end of the file
    echo "maxrepeat = 3" >> "$PWQUALITY_CONF"
    echo "Added maxrepeat = 3 to $PWQUALITY_CONF"
fi

# Verify the change was applied
if grep -q "^maxrepeat[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*3" "$PWQUALITY_CONF"; then
    echo "Password quality policy updated successfully"
    echo "Passwords with more than 3 consecutive identical characters will now be rejected"
    exit 0
else
    echo "Error: Failed to update password quality policy" >&2
    exit 1
fi
