echo "Settings applied:"
echo "  - Idle delay: ${IDLE_DELAY} seconds (15 minutes)"
echo "  - Screen lock enabled: true"
echo "  - Lock delay after idle: 0 seconds"
echo "  - Settings are locked system-wide and cannot be overridden by users."
echo ""
echo "Note: Users may need to log out and back in for changes to take effect."

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure audit rules for file deletion (unlink) monitoring
# on RHEL 8 systems

set -e

# Determine the system architecture
ARCH=$(uname -m)

# Define the audit rules file
AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/delete.rules"

# Define the rules based on architecture
if [ "$ARCH" = "x86_64" ]; then
    # 64-bit system - need both 32-bit and 64-bit rules
    RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S unlink -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"
    RULE_64BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S unlink -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"
    RULES=("$RULE_32BIT" "$RULE_64BIT")
else
    # 32-bit system - only need 32-bit rules
    RULE_32BIT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S unlink -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k delete"
    RULES=("$RULE_32BIT")
fi

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Create or update the rules file
# First, check if file exists and has content
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    EXISTING_CONTENT=$(cat "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE")
else
    EXISTING_CONTENT=""
fi

# Build the new content
NEW_CONTENT=""
for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    NEW_CONTENT+="$rule"$'\n'
done

# Remove trailing newline for comparison
NEW_CONTENT=$(echo -n "$NEW_CONTENT" | sed '/^$/d')

# Check if rules already exist (idempotency)
RULES_CHANGED=0

for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
    if ! grep -qxF "$rule" "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        RULES_CHANGED=1
        break
    fi
done

if [ "$RULES_CHANGED" -eq 1 ]; then
    # Write the rules to the file
    echo "# Audit rules for file deletion monitoring (unlink syscall)" > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    echo "# Generated by remediation script" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
        echo "$rule" >> "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    done
    
    echo "Audit rules have been written to $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    
    # Set proper permissions
    chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    
    # Load the rules into the running configuration
    # Use augenrules to merge and load rules
    if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
        augenrules --load
        echo "Audit rules have been loaded using augenrules"
    else
        # Fallback to auditctl if augenrules is not available
        for rule in "${RULES[@]}"; do
            auditctl $rule 2>/dev/null || true
        done
        echo "Audit rules have been loaded using auditctl"
    fi
else
    echo "Audit rules are already properly configured in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Ensure auditd service is enabled and running
if systemctl is-enabled auditd &>/dev/null; then
    echo "auditd service is already enabled"
else
    systemctl enable auditd
    echo "auditd service has been enabled"
fi

if systemctl is-active auditd &>/dev/null; then
    echo "auditd service is already running"
else
    systemctl start auditd
    echo "auditd service has been started"
fi

# Verify the rules are active
echo ""
echo "Verifying active audit rules for unlink syscall:"
auditctl -l | grep -E "unlink.*delete" || echo "Warning: Rules may not be active yet. A system restart might be required."

echo ""
echo "Configuration complete. The audit rules will persist across reboots."

#!/bin/bash

# Remediation script to disable sending of IPv4 ICMP redirects
# This script is idempotent and applies changes at runtime and persistently

set -e

SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-icmp-redirects.conf"
PARAMETER_ALL="net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects"
PARAMETER_DEFAULT="net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects"
VALUE="0"

# Function to set runtime parameter
set_runtime_parameter() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local current_value
    
    current_value=$(sysctl -n "$param" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    if [[ "$current_value" != "$value" ]]; then
        sysctl -w "${param}=${value}" >/dev/null
        echo "Runtime: Set ${param} = ${value}"
    else
        echo "Runtime: ${param} already set to ${value}"
    fi
}

# Function to ensure persistent configuration
ensure_persistent_config() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local config_line="${param} = ${value}"
    local needs_update=true
    
    # Check if the file exists and contains the correct setting
    if [[ -f "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE" ]]; then
        # Check if the parameter is already correctly set in the file
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${value}[[:space:]]*$" "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
            needs_update=false
            echo "Persistent: ${param} already configured in ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
        fi
    fi
    
    if [[ "$needs_update" == true ]]; then
        # Remove any existing entries for this parameter (including commented or different values)
        if [[ -f "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE" ]]; then
            # Create temp file without the parameter
            grep -vE "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=" "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE" > "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}.tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
            mv "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}.tmp" "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
        fi
        
        # Append the correct configuration
        echo "$config_line" >> "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
        echo "Persistent: Added ${param} = ${value} to ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    fi
}

# Also check and remove conflicting settings in other sysctl configuration files
remove_conflicting_settings() {
    local param="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local sysctl_files=(/etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf)
    
    for file in "${sysctl_files[@]}"; do
        # Skip our own configuration file and non-existent files
        [[ "$file" == "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE" ]] && continue
        [[ ! -f "$file" ]] && continue
        
        # Check if file contains a conflicting setting (different value)
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*${param}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*[^${value}]" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Comment out the conflicting line
            sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*\(${param}[[:space:]]*=.*\)$/# Disabled by security remediation: \1/" "$file"
            echo "Removed conflicting setting for ${param} in ${file}"
        fi
    done
}

echo "Starting ICMP redirect remediation..."

# Ensure the sysctl.d directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d

# Remove conflicting settings from other files
remove_conflicting_settings "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$VALUE"
remove_conflicting_settings "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT" "$VALUE"

# Set persistent configuration
ensure_persistent_config "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$VALUE"
ensure_persistent_config "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT" "$VALUE"

# Apply runtime settings
set_runtime_parameter "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$VALUE"
set_runtime_parameter "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT" "$VALUE"

# Also apply to all current interfaces at runtime
for iface in /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/*/send_redirects; do
    if [[ -f "$iface" ]]; then
        iface_name=$(echo "$iface" | cut -d'/' -f7)
        current_val=$(cat "$iface")
        if [[ "$current_val" != "0" ]]; then
            echo 0 > "$iface"
            echo "Runtime: Disabled send_redirects for interface ${iface_name}"
        fi
    fi
done

echo "ICMP redirect remediation complete."
echo "Verification:"
sysctl "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT"

remove_conflicting_settings "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$VALUE"
remove_conflicting_settings "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT" "$VALUE"

# Set persistent configuration
ensure_persistent_config "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$VALUE"
ensure_persistent_config "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT" "$VALUE"

# Apply runtime settings
set_runtime_parameter "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$VALUE"
set_runtime_parameter "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT" "$VALUE"

# Also apply to all current interfaces at runtime
for iface in /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf/*/send_redirects; do
    if [[ -f "$iface" ]]; then
        iface_name=$(echo "$iface" | cut -d'/' -f7)
        current_val=$(cat "$iface")
        if [[ "$current_val" != "0" ]]; then
            echo 0 > "$iface"
            echo "Runtime: Disabled send_redirects for interface ${iface_name}"
        fi
    fi
done

echo "ICMP redirect remediation complete."
echo "Verification:"
sysctl "$PARAMETER_ALL" "$PARAMETER_DEFAULT"

# Ensure rsyslog is installed
if ! rpm -q rsyslog &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y rsyslog

if ! rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls
fi

# Define the TLS configuration file path
TLS_CONF_FILE="/etc/rsyslog.d/10-tls-forwarding.conf"

# Create or update the TLS configuration for rsyslog
# This configuration enables TLS for all forwarded log traffic
cat > "$TLS_CONF_FILE" << 'EOF'
# Load the gtls stream driver module for TLS support
module(load="gtls")

# Global TLS configuration
global(
    DefaultNetstreamDriver="gtls"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverCAFile="/etc/pki/rsyslog/ca-cert.pem"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverCertFile="/etc/pki/rsyslog/client-cert.pem"
    DefaultNetstreamDriverKeyFile="/etc/pki/rsyslog/client-key.pem"
)

# Set the default netstream driver mode to TLS
# This ensures all forwarding actions use encrypted transport
# StreamDriver.Mode 1 = TLS encryption required
# StreamDriver.AuthMode can be "anon", "x509/fingerprint", "x509/certvalid", or "x509/name"

# Example forwarding action template with TLS
# Uncomment and modify the following to forward to your remote collector:
# action(
#     type="omfwd"
#     target="remote-log-collector.example.com"
#     port="6514"
#     protocol="tcp"
#     StreamDriver="gtls"
#     StreamDriverMode="1"
#     StreamDriverAuthMode="x509/name"
#     StreamDriverPermittedPeers="remote-log-collector.example.com"
# )
EOF

# Create the directory for rsyslog certificates if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -d "/etc/pki/rsyslog" ]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/pki/rsyslog
    chmod 700 /etc/pki/rsyslog
fi

# Check if the main rsyslog.conf has any existing forwarding rules
# and ensure they use TLS by updating the global settings
RSYSLOG_CONF="/etc/rsyslog.conf"

# Backup original config if not already backed up
if [ ! -f "${RSYSLOG_CONF}.orig" ]; then
    cp "$RSYSLOG_CONF" "${RSYSLOG_CONF}.orig"
fi

# Ensure the gtls module is loaded in main config if not using conf.d
# Check if imtcp or omfwd is configured without TLS and warn
if grep -qE '^\s*\*\.\*\s+@@' "$RSYSLOG_CONF" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "WARNING: Legacy forwarding syntax found in $RSYSLOG_CONF"
    echo "Consider updating to use action() syntax with TLS in $TLS_CONF_FILE"
fi

# Create a drop-in configuration to enforce TLS on any omfwd actions
# This sets global defaults that apply to all forwarding
cat > "/etc/rsyslog.d/00-tls-defaults.conf" << 'EOF'
# Enforce TLS defaults for all network operations
# This file is loaded before other configurations

# Load GnuTLS driver
module(load="gtls")

# Set global defaults for network stream driver
global(
    DefaultNetstreamDriver="gtls"
)

# Template for secure forwarding
template(name="TLSForwardFormat" type="string"
    string="<%PRI%>%TIMESTAMP:::date-rfc3339% %HOSTNAME% %syslogtag%%msg:::sp-if-no-1st-sp%%msg%\n"
)
EOF

# Set proper permissions on configuration files
chmod 644 "$TLS_CONF_FILE"
chmod 644 "/etc/rsyslog.d/00-tls-defaults.conf"

# Validate rsyslog configuration
if rsyslogd -N1 2>&1 | grep -q "error"; then
    echo "ERROR: rsyslog configuration validation failed"
    rsyslogd -N1
    exit 1
fi

# Enable and restart rsyslog service
systemctl enable rsyslog

# Restart rsyslog to apply changes
systemctl restart rsyslog

# Verify rsyslog is running
if systemctl is-active --quiet rsyslog; then
    echo "rsyslog service is running with TLS configuration"
else
    echo "ERROR: rsyslog service failed to start"
    systemctl status rsyslog
    exit 1
fi

# Verify TLS module is loaded
if rsyslogd -N1 2>&1 | grep -qi "gtls"; then
    echo "TLS (gtls) module configuration detected"
fi

echo "TLS configuration for rsyslog forwarding has been applied."
echo "NOTE: Ensure the following certificate files exist with proper permissions:"
echo "  - /etc/pki/rsyslog/ca-cert.pem (CA certificate)"
echo "  - /etc/pki/rsyslog/client-cert.pem (Client certificate)"
echo "  - /etc/pki/rsyslog/client-key.pem (Client private key)"
echo ""
echo "Update $TLS_CONF_FILE with your remote collector details."

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure audit backlog limit on RHEL 8
# Sets audit_backlog_limit=8192 for all current and future kernels

AUDIT_PARAM="audit_backlog_limit=8192"
GRUB_DEFAULT="/etc/default/grub"
GRUBBY_CMD="/usr/sbin/grubby"

# Function to check if a parameter exists in GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
param_exists_in_grub_default() {
    if grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=.*audit_backlog_limit=" "$GRUB_DEFAULT" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to get current value of audit_backlog_limit from /etc/default/grub
get_current_value() {
    grep "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$GRUB_DEFAULT" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'audit_backlog_limit=\K[0-9]+' | head -1
}

# Update /etc/default/grub for future kernel installs
if [ -f "$GRUB_DEFAULT" ]; then
    if param_exists_in_grub_default; then
        current_value=$(get_current_value)
        if [ "$current_value" != "8192" ]; then
            # Replace existing audit_backlog_limit with correct value
            sed -i 's/audit_backlog_limit=[0-9]*/audit_backlog_limit=8192/g' "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
        fi
    else
        # Add audit_backlog_limit to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
        if grep -q "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$GRUB_DEFAULT"; then
            # Append to existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
            sed -i "s/\(^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"[^\"]*\)\"/\1 ${AUDIT_PARAM}\"/" "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
        else
            # Add new GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line
            echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${AUDIT_PARAM}\"" >> "$GRUB_DEFAULT"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Use grubby to update all currently installed kernels
if [ -x "$GRUBBY_CMD" ]; then
    # Get all kernels
    kernels=$($GRUBBY_CMD --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep "^kernel=" | cut -d'"' -f2)
    
    for kernel in $kernels; do
        if [ -n "$kernel" ]; then
            # Check current args for this kernel
            current_args=$($GRUBBY_CMD --info="$kernel" 2>/dev/null | grep "^args=" | cut -d'"' -f2)
            
            if echo "$current_args" | grep -q "audit_backlog_limit="; then
                # Check if value is already 8192
                current_val=$(echo "$current_args" | grep -oP 'audit_backlog_limit=\K[0-9]+')
                if [ "$current_val" != "8192" ]; then
                    # Remove old value and add new one
                    $GRUBBY_CMD --update-kernel="$kernel" --remove-args="audit_backlog_limit=$current_val" 2>/dev/null
                    $GRUBBY_CMD --update-kernel="$kernel" --args="$AUDIT_PARAM" 2>/dev/null
                fi
            else
                # Add the parameter
                $GRUBBY_CMD --update-kernel="$kernel" --args="$AUDIT_PARAM" 2>/dev/null
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    # Also update DEFAULT kernel entry to ensure future kernels get this setting
    $GRUBBY_CMD --update-kernel=DEFAULT --args="$AUDIT_PARAM" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Regenerate grub.cfg if grub2-mkconfig is available
# This ensures consistency across all boot methods
if [ -x /usr/sbin/grub2-mkconfig ]; then
    if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
        # UEFI system
        if [ -f /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg ]; then
            grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg 2>/dev/null
        elif [ -f /boot/efi/EFI/centos/grub.cfg ]; then
            grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/efi/EFI/centos/grub.cfg 2>/dev/null
        fi
    else
        # BIOS system
        if [ -f /boot/grub2/grub.cfg ]; then
            grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg 2>/dev/null
        fi
    fi
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure audit rules for file ownership change syscalls
# Monitors chown, fchown, fchownat, lchown for users with UID >= 1000

AUDIT_RULES_FILE="/etc/audit/rules.d/10-ownership-change.rules"

# Define the audit rules for 32-bit and 64-bit syscalls
# These rules monitor chown, fchown, fchownat, lchown syscalls
# for users with auid >= 1000 and auid != unset (4294967295)

RULES_CONTENT="-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S chown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchownat -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lchown -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod"

# Ensure the audit rules directory exists
mkdir -p /etc/audit/rules.d

# Check if the file exists and has the correct content
if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    CURRENT_CONTENT=$(cat "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE")
    if [ "$CURRENT_CONTENT" = "$RULES_CONTENT" ]; then
        echo "Audit rules already configured correctly in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    else
        echo "Updating audit rules in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
        echo "$RULES_CONTENT" > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
else
    echo "Creating audit rules file $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    echo "$RULES_CONTENT" > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Ensure auditd service is enabled
systemctl enable auditd 2>/dev/null

# Regenerate the audit rules and load them
# augenrules merges all rules in /etc/audit/rules.d/ and loads them
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
else
    # Fallback: directly load rules using auditctl
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null
fi

echo "Audit rules for ownership change syscalls have been configured"
echo "Rules are tagged with key: perm_mod"
echo "Rules will persist across reboots"

if [ -f "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" ]; then
    CURRENT_CONTENT=$(cat "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE")
    if [ "$CURRENT_CONTENT" = "$RULES_CONTENT" ]; then
        echo "Audit rules already configured correctly in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    else
        echo "Updating audit rules in $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
        echo "$RULES_CONTENT" > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    fi
else
    echo "Creating audit rules file $AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
    echo "$RULES_CONTENT" > "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the rules file
chmod 640 "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE"

# Ensure auditd service is enabled
systemctl enable auditd 2>/dev/null

# Regenerate the audit rules and load them
# augenrules merges all rules in /etc/audit/rules.d/ and loads them
if command -v augenrules &> /dev/null; then
    augenrules --load
else
    # Fallback: directly load rules using auditctl
    auditctl -R "$AUDIT_RULES_FILE" 2>/dev/null
fi

echo "Audit rules for ownership change syscalls have been configured"
echo "Rules are tagged with key: perm_mod"
echo "Rules will persist across reboots"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to disable and blacklist USB mass-storage devices on RHEL 8

set -e

USB_STORAGE_MODULE="usb-storage"
MODPROBE_CONF_DIR="/etc/modprobe.d"
BLACKLIST_FILE="${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}/usb-storage.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}" ]; then
    mkdir -p "${MODPROBE_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Create or update the configuration file to disable and blacklist usb-storage
# Using install /bin/false prevents the module from being loaded
# blacklist prevents automatic loading

DESIRED_CONTENT="# Disable USB mass-storage to prevent data exfiltration and malicious software
# This file is managed by security remediation script
install ${USB_STORAGE_MODULE} /bin/false
blacklist ${USB_STORAGE_MODULE}"

# Check if file exists and has correct content
if [ -f "${BLACKLIST_FILE}" ]; then
    CURRENT_CONTENT=$(cat "${BLACKLIST_FILE}")
    if [ "${CURRENT_CONTENT}" = "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" ]; then
        echo "Configuration file ${BLACKLIST_FILE} already has correct content."
    else
        echo "Updating configuration file ${BLACKLIST_FILE}..."
        echo "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${BLACKLIST_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "Creating configuration file ${BLACKLIST_FILE}..."
    echo "${DESIRED_CONTENT}" > "${BLACKLIST_FILE}"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the configuration file
chmod 644 "${BLACKLIST_FILE}"

# Unload the module if it's currently loaded
if lsmod | grep -q "^usb_storage"; then
    echo "Unloading usb_storage module..."
    modprobe -r usb_storage 2>/dev/null || echo "Warning: Could not unload usb_storage module (may be in use)"
else
    echo "usb_storage module is not currently loaded."
fi

# Update module dependency database
echo "Updating module dependencies..."
depmod -a

echo "USB mass-storage has been disabled and blacklisted."
echo "The change will persist across reboots."

# Disable IPv4 packet forwarding - idempotent remediation script
# For RHEL 8 systems not intended to act as routers

set -e

SYSCTL_PARAM="net.ipv4.ip_forward"
DESIRED_VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv4-forwarding.conf"

# Apply the setting at runtime
current_value=$(sysctl -n "$SYSCTL_PARAM" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

if [ "$current_value" != "$DESIRED_VALUE" ]; then
    echo "Setting $SYSCTL_PARAM to $DESIRED_VALUE at runtime..."
    sysctl -w "$SYSCTL_PARAM=$DESIRED_VALUE"
else
    echo "$SYSCTL_PARAM is already set to $DESIRED_VALUE at runtime."
fi

# Ensure the setting persists across reboots
# Check if the setting already exists in sysctl configuration files
# We'll use a dedicated drop-in file to ensure our setting takes precedence

if [ -f "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE" ]; then
    # Check if the file already has the correct setting
    if grep -q "^${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${DESIRED_VALUE}$" "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Persistent configuration already exists in $SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
    else
        # File exists but may have wrong value or different format
        echo "Updating $SYSCTL_CONF_FILE with correct value..."
        # Remove any existing entries for this parameter
        sed -i "/^${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=/d" "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
        echo "$SYSCTL_PARAM = $DESIRED_VALUE" >> "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
    fi
else
    # Create the configuration file
    echo "Creating $SYSCTL_CONF_FILE for persistent configuration..."
    echo "# Disable IPv4 packet forwarding" > "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
    echo "$SYSCTL_PARAM = $DESIRED_VALUE" >> "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"
fi

# Set proper permissions on the configuration file
chmod 644 "$SYSCTL_CONF_FILE"

# Also check and comment out any conflicting settings in main sysctl.conf
if [ -f /etc/sysctl.conf ]; then
    if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" /etc/sysctl.conf 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Commenting out conflicting entry in /etc/sysctl.conf..."
        sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*\(${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=.*\)$/# Disabled by security remediation: \1/" /etc/sysctl.conf
    fi
fi

# Verify the runtime setting
final_value=$(sysctl -n "$SYSCTL_PARAM")
if [ "$final_value" = "$DESIRED_VALUE" ]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: IPv4 forwarding is disabled (runtime value: $final_value)"
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to disable IPv4 forwarding (current value: $final_value)"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Remediation complete. IPv4 packet forwarding is disabled both at runtime and persistently."

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to lock down GNOME screensaver lock-delay setting
# This prevents ordinary users from changing the screen lock delay

set -e

# Define paths
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/user"
DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
DCONF_LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/screensaver"

# Create directories if they don't exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"

# Create or update the dconf profile to use local database
# The profile should have system-db:local before user-db:user
if [[ -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]]; then
    # Check if system-db:local is already present
    if ! grep -q "^system-db:local" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        # Prepend system-db:local to existing file
        TEMP_FILE=$(mktemp)
        echo "system-db:local" > "${TEMP_FILE}"
        cat "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" >> "${TEMP_FILE}"
        mv "${TEMP_FILE}" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
    # Ensure user-db:user is present
    if ! grep -q "^user-db:user" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        echo "user-db:user" >> "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
    fi
else
    # Create new profile file
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
fi

# Create or update the screensaver settings file
# Set lock-delay to 0 (immediate lock after screen blanks)
cat > "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" << 'EOF'
[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=uint32 0
lock-enabled=true
EOF

# Create or update the locks file to prevent user modifications
cat > "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}" << 'EOF'
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay
/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-enabled
EOF

# Set appropriate permissions
chmod 644 "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
chmod 644 "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
chmod 644 "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"

# Update the dconf database to apply changes
dconf update

echo "GNOME screensaver lock-delay setting has been locked down."
echo "Users are now prevented from changing the screen lock delay."

#!/bin/bash

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"
SSHD_CONFIG_DIR="/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"
PARAM="GSSAPIAuthentication"
VALUE="no"

# Function to check if a parameter is set in a file
param_in_file() {
    local file="$1"
    local param="$2"
    grep -Eq "^\s*${param}\s+" "$file" 2>/dev/null
}

# Function to get the value of a parameter in a file
get_param_value() {
    local file="$1"
    local param="$2"
    grep -E "^\s*${param}\s+" "$file" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | tail -1
}

# Remove any existing GSSAPIAuthentication settings from the main config and drop-in files
# to ensure no conflicting settings exist

# Handle main sshd_config file
if [ -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]; then
    # Remove any existing GSSAPIAuthentication lines (commented or not) to avoid duplicates
    sed -i "/^\s*${PARAM}\s/d" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
    sed -i "/^#\s*${PARAM}\s/d" "$SSHD_CONFIG"
fi

# Handle drop-in directory files
if [ -d "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR" ]; then
    for conf_file in "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"/*.conf; do
        if [ -f "$conf_file" ]; then
            if param_in_file "$conf_file" "$PARAM"; then
                sed -i "/^\s*${PARAM}\s/d" "$conf_file"
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

# Create or update the drop-in configuration file for this setting
# Using a drop-in file is the preferred method on RHEL 8 for custom configurations
DROPIN_FILE="${SSHD_CONFIG_DIR}/50-gssapi.conf"

# Ensure the drop-in directory exists
mkdir -p "$SSHD_CONFIG_DIR"

# Write the configuration to the drop-in file
echo "${PARAM} ${VALUE}" > "$DROPIN_FILE"

# Set proper permissions on the drop-in file
chmod 600 "$DROPIN_FILE"
chown root:root "$DROPIN_FILE"

# Validate the SSH configuration
if sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
    # Restart the SSH service to apply changes
    systemctl restart sshd
else
    echo "Error: SSH configuration validation failed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure sshd is enabled to start on boot
systemctl enable sshd 2>/dev/null

exit 0

DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"

if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" << 'EOF'

system-db:local

else

    if ! grep -q "^system-db:local$" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        # Add system-db:local after user-db:user if not present
        if grep -q "^user-db:user$" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
            sed -i '/^user-db:user$/a system-db:local' "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
        else
            # If user-db:user is also missing, recreate the file

user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
        fi

# The setting org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-delay controls the delay
# Setting it to uint32 0 means immediate lock when screensaver activates

if [[ -f "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" ]]; then

    if grep -q "^\[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver\]" "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"; then

            if grep -q "^lock-delay=" "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"; then

            fi
        fi
    else

        echo "lock-delay=uint32 0" >> "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure GNOME screensaver lock delay to 0 seconds system-wide on RHEL 8

set -e

# Define the dconf database directory and profile
DCONF_DB_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d"
DCONF_LOCKS_DIR="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks"
DCONF_PROFILE_DIR="/etc/dconf/profile"
DCONF_PROFILE_FILE="${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}/user"
DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE="${DCONF_DB_DIR}/00-screensaver"
DCONF_LOCKS_FILE="${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}/screensaver"

# Create necessary directories if they don't exist
mkdir -p "${DCONF_DB_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_LOCKS_DIR}"
mkdir -p "${DCONF_PROFILE_DIR}"

# Configure the dconf profile to use system database
# Check if profile file exists and has correct content
if [[ ! -f "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" ]]; then
    cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
else
    # Ensure system-db:local is present in the profile
    if ! grep -q "^system-db:local$" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
        # Add system-db:local after user-db:user if not present
        if grep -q "^user-db:user$" "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"; then
            sed -i '/^user-db:user$/a system-db:local' "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}"
        else
            # If user-db:user is also missing, recreate the file
            cat > "${DCONF_PROFILE_FILE}" << 'EOF'
user-db:user
system-db:local
EOF
        fi
    fi
fi

# Configure the screensaver lock delay setting
# The setting org.gnome.desktop.screensaver lock-delay controls the delay
# Setting it to uint32 0 means immediate lock when screensaver activates
SETTINGS_CONTENT="[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]
lock-delay=uint32 0"

# Check if file exists and has correct content
if [[ -f "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}" ]]; then
    # Check if the screensaver section and lock-delay setting exist
    if grep -q "^\[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver\]" "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
        # Section exists, check if lock-delay is set correctly
        if grep -q "^lock-delay=uint32 0$" "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
            : # Setting already correct, do nothing
        else
            # Update or add the lock-delay setting in the existing section
            if grep -q "^lock-delay=" "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"; then
                # Replace existing lock-delay setting
                sed -i 's/^lock-delay=.*/lock-delay=uint32 0/' "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
            else
                # Add lock-delay after the section header
                sed -i '/^\[org\/gnome\/desktop\/screensaver\]/a lock-delay=uint32 0' "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
            fi
        fi
    else
        # Section doesn't exist, append it
        echo "" >> "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
        echo "[org/gnome/desktop/screensaver]" >> "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
        echo "lock-delay=uint32 0" >> "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
    fi
else
    # File doesn't exist, create it
    echo "${SETTINGS_CONTENT}" > "${DCONF_SETTINGS_FILE}"
fi

# Lock the setting so users cannot override it
LOCK_CONTENT="/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay"

if [[ -f "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}" ]]; then
    if ! grep -q "^${LOCK_CONTENT}$" "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"; then
        echo "${LOCK_CONTENT}" >> "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"
    fi
else
    echo "${LOCK_CONTENT}" > "${DCONF_LOCKS_FILE}"
fi

# Update the dconf database to apply changes
dconf update

echo "GNOME screensaver lock-delay has been set to 0 seconds system-wide."
echo "The setting has been locked to prevent user override."
echo "Changes will take effect on next login or after running 'dconf update'."
```

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure systemd login manager idle session termination on RHEL 8
# This script sets StopIdleSessionSec to terminate sessions after 15 minutes of inactivity

set -euo pipefail

LOGIND_CONF="/etc/systemd/logind.conf"
LOGIND_CONF_DIR="/etc/systemd/logind.conf.d"
CUSTOM_CONF="${LOGIND_CONF_DIR}/99-idle-timeout.conf"

# Define idle timeout in seconds (15 minutes = 900 seconds)
# This is a reasonable balance between security and usability
IDLE_TIMEOUT="900"

# Ensure the logind.conf.d directory exists
if [[ ! -d "${LOGIND_CONF_DIR}" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "${LOGIND_CONF_DIR}"
    chmod 755 "${LOGIND_CONF_DIR}"
fi

# Create or update the custom configuration file
# Using a drop-in file is the recommended approach for customizations
cat > "${CUSTOM_CONF}" << EOF
# Automatically terminate user login sessions after ${IDLE_TIMEOUT} seconds of inactivity
# This reduces the risk of unauthorized access to unattended sessions
# Managed by automated remediation script - do not edit manually

[Login]
StopIdleSessionSec=${IDLE_TIMEOUT}
EOF

# Set proper permissions on the configuration file
chmod 644 "${CUSTOM_CONF}"

# Verify the configuration file was created correctly
if [[ ! -f "${CUSTOM_CONF}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to create configuration file ${CUSTOM_CONF}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Validate that the setting is present in the file
if ! grep -q "^StopIdleSessionSec=${IDLE_TIMEOUT}$" "${CUSTOM_CONF}"; then
    echo "ERROR: Configuration file does not contain expected setting" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Reload systemd-logind to apply the new configuration without requiring a reboot
# This makes the change take effect immediately for new sessions
if systemctl is-active --quiet systemd-logind; then
    systemctl restart systemd-logind
fi

# Verify the configuration was applied by checking loginctl
CURRENT_SETTING=$(loginctl show-session 2>/dev/null | grep -i "StopIdleSessionSec" || busctl get-property org.freedesktop.login1 /org/freedesktop/login1 org.freedesktop.login1.Manager StopIdleSessionSec 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' || echo "")

# Alternative verification method - parse the config directly
if [[ -z "${CURRENT_SETTING}" ]]; then
    # Check if systemd-logind will read our configuration
    SYSTEMD_CHECK=$(systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf 2>/dev/null | grep -E "^StopIdleSessionSec=" | tail -1 || echo "")
    if [[ "${SYSTEMD_CHECK}" == "StopIdleSessionSec=${IDLE_TIMEOUT}" ]]; then
        echo "SUCCESS: StopIdleSessionSec configured to ${IDLE_TIMEOUT} seconds"
        exit 0
    fi
fi

echo "SUCCESS: Idle session termination configured to ${IDLE_TIMEOUT} seconds (15 minutes)"
echo "Configuration file: ${CUSTOM_CONF}"
echo "Note: New sessions will be automatically terminated after ${IDLE_TIMEOUT} seconds of inactivity"

exit 0

# Idempotent RHEL 8 Smart Card Authentication Configuration Script
# Configures SSSD for CAC/PIV token authentication

if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

    echo "Warning: This script is designed for RHEL 8. Proceeding anyway..." >&2
fi

PACKAGES=(
    "sssd"
    "sssd-tools"
    "opensc"
    "pcsc-lite"
    "pcsc-lite-ccid"
    "openssl"
    "nss-tools"
    "pam_pkcs11"
    "authselect"
)

    if ! rpm -q "$pkg" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "Installing $pkg..."
        dnf install -y "$pkg"
    else
        echo "Package $pkg is already installed."
    fi
done

echo "Enabling and starting PC/SC Smart Card daemon..."

# Enable and start pcscd service
systemctl enable pcscd.socket pcscd.service --now 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl start pcscd.socket 2>/dev/null || true

echo "Configuring SSSD for smart card authentication..."

SSSD_CONF="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf"
SSSD_CONF_BACKUP="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf.backup.smartcard"

    cp "$SSSD_CONF" "$SSSD_CONF_BACKUP"

    cat > "$SSSD_CONF" << 'EOF'
# SMARTCARD_AUTH_CONFIGURED
# SSSD Configuration for Smart Card Authentication

[sssd]
services = nss, pam, ssh
domains = LOCAL
certificate_verification = no_ocsp

[nss]
filter_groups = root
filter_users = root

[pam]
pam_cert_auth = True
pam_cert_db_path = /etc/sssd/pki/sssd_auth_ca_db.pem
p11_child_timeout = 60

[domain/LOCAL]
id_provider = files
auth_provider = files

# Enable certificate/smart card authentication
pam_cert_auth = True

# Map certificates to local users
local_auth_policy = enable:smartcard

# Certificate verification settings
certificate_verification = no_ocsp

# Cache credentials for offline authentication
cache_credentials = True
EOF
    echo "Created new SSSD configuration with smart card support."
else
    echo "SSSD configuration already contains smart card settings."
fi

chmod 600 "$SSSD_CONF"
chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF"

else

    "authselect"
)

        dnf install -y "$pkg"
    else

    fi

systemctl enable pcscd.socket pcscd.service --now 2>/dev/null || true

SSSD_CONF_BACKUP="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf.backup.smartcard"

# SMARTCARD_AUTH_CONFIGURED
# SSSD Configuration for Smart Card Authentication

[sssd]
services = nss, pam, ssh
domains = LOCAL
certificate_verification = no_ocsp

[nss]
filter_groups = root
filter_users = root

[pam]
pam_cert_auth = True
pam_cert_db_path = /etc/sssd/pki/sssd_auth_ca_db.pem
p11_child_timeout = 60

[domain/LOCAL]
id_provider = files
auth_provider = files

# Enable certificate/smart card authentication
pam_cert_auth = True

# Map certificates to local users
local_auth_policy = enable:smartcard

# Certificate verification settings
certificate_verification = no_ocsp

# Cache credentials for offline authentication
cache_credentials = True
EOF
    echo "Created new SSSD configuration with smart card support."
else
    echo "SSSD configuration already contains smart card settings."
fi

# Set proper permissions on sssd.conf
chmod 600 "$SSSD_CONF"
chown root:root "$SSSD_CONF"

echo "Setting up certificate directory structure..."

# Create PKI directory for SSSD
mkdir -p /etc/sssd/pki
chmod 755 /etc/sssd/pki

# In production, this should contain your organization's CA certificates
CA_DB="/etc/sssd/pki/sssd_auth_ca_db.pem"
if [[ ! -f "$CA_DB" ]]; then
    # Copy system CA certificates as a starting point
    if [[ -f /etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt ]]; then
        cp /etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt "$CA_DB"
        echo "Initialized CA database from system CA bundle."
    else
        touch "$CA_DB"
        echo "Created empty CA database. Add your CA certificates to $CA_DB"
    fi
fi
chmod 644 "$CA_DB"

echo "Configuring PAM for smart card authentication..."

# Use authselect to configure PAM with smart card support
# Check current profile
CURRENT_PROFILE=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "none")

if [[ "$CURRENT_PROFILE" != *"sssd"* ]]; then
    echo "Selecting SSSD authselect profile with smart card support..."
    authselect select sssd with-smartcard with-smartcard-lock-on-removal --force 2>/dev/null || \
    authselect select sssd with-smartcard --force 2>/dev/null || \
    authselect select sssd --force
else
    echo "SSSD profile already selected. Enabling smart card features..."
    authselect enable-feature with-smartcard 2>/dev/null || true
    authselect enable-feature with-smartcard-lock-on-removal 2>/dev/null || true
fi

OPENSC_CONF="/etc/opensc.conf"
if [[ -f "$OPENSC_CONF" ]]; then
    # Backup if not already backed up
    if [[ ! -f "${OPENSC_CONF}.backup" ]]; then
        cp "$OPENSC_CONF" "${OPENSC_CONF}.backup"
    fi
fi

echo "Setting up p11-kit for PKCS#11 module integration..."

# Ensure p11-kit is configured to use OpenSC
P11_MODULE_DIR="/etc/pkcs11/modules"
mkdir -p "$P11_MODULE_DIR"

OPENSC_MODULE="$P11_MODULE_DIR/opensc.module"
if [[ ! -f "$OPENSC_MODULE" ]]; then
    cat > "$OPENSC_MODULE" << 'EOF'
module: opensc-pkcs11.so
critical: no
EOF
    echo "Created OpenSC PKCS#11 module configuration."
fi

echo "Configuring NSS database for smart card certificates..."

# Create NSS database directory
NSS_DB_DIR="/etc/pki/nssdb"
if [[ ! -d "$NSS_DB_DIR" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$NSS_DB_DIR"
fi

# Initialize NSS database if it doesn't exist
if [[ ! -f "$NSS_DB_DIR/cert9.db" ]]; then
    echo "Initializing NSS database..."
    certutil -d sql:"$NSS_DB_DIR" -N --empty-password 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Create a systemd drop-in for sssd to ensure proper ordering
SSSD_DROPIN_DIR="/etc/systemd/system/sssd.service.d"
mkdir -p "$SSSD_DROPIN_DIR"

SSSD_DROPIN="$SSSD_DROPIN_DIR/smartcard.conf"
if [[ ! -f "$SSSD_DROPIN" ]]; then
    cat > "$SSSD_DROPIN" << 'EOF'
[Unit]
After=pcscd.service pcscd.socket
Wants=pcscd.socket

[Service]
Environment="PKCS11_MODULE=/usr/lib64/opensc-pkcs11.so"
EOF
    echo "Created SSSD systemd drop-in for smart card support."
fi

echo "Reloading systemd configuration..."
systemctl daemon-reload

echo "Enabling and restarting services..."

# Enable services to start on boot
systemctl enable sssd.service
systemctl enable pcscd.socket
systemctl enable pcscd.service

# Restart SSSD to apply configuration
systemctl restart sssd.service 2>/dev/null || systemctl start sssd.service

echo "Configuring sudo for smart card authentication (optional)..."

# Configure sudo to potentially use smart card
SUDO_SMARTCARD="/etc/sudoers.d/smartcard"
if [[ ! -f "$SUDO_SMARTCARD" ]]; then
    cat > "$SUDO_SMARTCARD" << 'EOF'
# Allow smart card authentication for sudo
# Uncomment the following line to require smart card for sudo:
# Defaults pam_service=sudo-smartcard
EOF
    chmod 440 "$SUDO_SMARTCARD"
fi

echo "Configuring GDM for smart card login (if installed)..."

# Configure GDM for smart card if it's installed
if rpm -q gdm &>/dev/null; then
    GDM_CUSTOM="/etc/gdm/custom.conf"
    if [[ -f "$GDM_CUSTOM" ]]; then

    chmod 755 "${SYSTEMD_OVERRIDE_DIR}"

ENVIRONMENT_FILE="/etc/environment"

    # Remove any existing SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG line first
    sed -i '/^SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=/d' "${ENVIRONMENT_FILE}"
    echo "SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=32" >> "${ENVIRONMENT_FILE}"
fi

if [[ -f "${SYSCONFIG_SSHD}" ]]; then

        sed -i 's/^SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=.*/SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=32/' "${SYSCONFIG_SSHD}"
    elif grep -q "^#.*SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=" "${SYSCONFIG_SSHD}"; then
        # Uncomment and set value
        sed -i 's/^#.*SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=.*/SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=32/' "${SYSCONFIG_SSHD}"

        # Add the setting
        echo "SSH_USE_STRONG_RNG=32" >> "${SYSCONFIG_SSHD}"

else
    # Create the sysconfig file

# Configuration file for the sshd service.

# Use 32 bytes of entropy from /dev/random for SSH random number generation

EOF
    chmod 644 "${SYSCONFIG_SSHD}"

echo "Configuration applied to:"
echo "  - ${SYSCONFIG_SSHD}"
echo "  - ${SYSTEMD_OVERRIDE_FILE}"
echo "  - ${ENVIRONMENT_FILE}"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent bash script to configure auditd to monitor setxattr system calls

set -e

# Define the audit rules we need to add
RULE_ROOT_32='-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S setxattr -F auid=0 -k perm_mod'
RULE_ROOT_64='-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -F auid=0 -k perm_mod'
RULE_USER_32='-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S setxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod'
RULE_USER_64='-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -F auid>=1000 -F auid!=unset -k perm_mod'

# Determine the appropriate rules directory
# RHEL 8 uses /etc/audit/rules.d/ with augenrules
RULES_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"
RULES_FILE="${RULES_DIR}/30-setxattr.rules"

# Ensure the audit package is installed
if ! rpm -q audit &>/dev/null; then
    dnf install -y audit
fi

# Ensure the rules directory exists
mkdir -p "${RULES_DIR}"

# Function to check if a rule pattern exists in loaded rules
rule_loaded() {
    local pattern="$1"
    auditctl -l 2>/dev/null | grep -qF "$pattern"
}

# Function to check if a rule exists in a file
rule_in_file() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        grep -qxF "$rule" "$file" 2>/dev/null
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to add rule to file if not present
add_rule_to_file() {
    local rule="$1"
    local file="$2"
    
    # Check all files in rules.d to avoid duplicates
    local found=0
    for f in "${RULES_DIR}"/*.rules; do
        if [[ -f "$f" ]] && grep -qxF "$rule" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
            found=1
            break
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ $found -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "$rule" >> "$file"
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Track if we made changes
CHANGES_MADE=0

# Create or update the rules file
if [[ ! -f "$RULES_FILE" ]]; then
    touch "$RULES_FILE"
fi

# Add each rule if not already present
for rule in "$RULE_ROOT_32" "$RULE_ROOT_64" "$RULE_USER_32" "$RULE_USER_64"; do
    if add_rule_to_file "$rule" "$RULES_FILE"; then
        CHANGES_MADE=1
        echo "Added rule: $rule"
    else
        echo "Rule already present: $rule"
    fi
done

# Ensure auditd service is enabled
systemctl enable auditd

# Ensure auditd is running
if ! systemctl is-active auditd &>/dev/null; then
    systemctl start auditd
    CHANGES_MADE=1
fi

# Load the rules if changes were made
if [[ $CHANGES_MADE -eq 1 ]]; then
    # Check if augenrules is being used (default on RHEL 8)
    if grep -q "^USE_AUGENRULES" /etc/sysconfig/auditd 2>/dev/null; then
        USE_AUGENRULES=$(grep "^USE_AUGENRULES" /etc/sysconfig/auditd | cut -d'=' -f2 | tr -d '"' | tr -d ' ')
    else
        # Default to yes on RHEL 8
        USE_AUGENRULES="yes"
    fi
    
    if [[ "$USE_AUGENRULES" == "yes" ]]; then
        # Use augenrules to load rules
        augenrules --load
    else
        # Use auditctl to load rules directly
        auditctl -R "$RULES_FILE"
    fi
    
    echo "Audit rules have been loaded"
else
    echo "No changes needed - rules already configured"
fi

# Verify the rules are loaded
echo ""
echo "Verifying loaded setxattr rules:"
auditctl -l | grep setxattr || echo "Warning: No setxattr rules found in loaded rules"

# Verify persistence
echo ""
echo "Verifying persistent rules in ${RULES_FILE}:"
if [[ -f "$RULES_FILE" ]]; then
    cat "$RULES_FILE"
else
    echo "Warning: Rules file not found"
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Ensure the sshd_config file exists
if [[ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG does not exist"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to set or update a configuration option
set_sshd_option() {
    local option="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local config_file="$SSHD_CONFIG"
    
    # Check if the option exists (commented or uncommented)
    if grep -qE "^\s*#?\s*${option}\s+" "$config_file"; then
        # Option exists, update it
        sed -i "s/^\s*#\?\s*${option}\s\+.*/${option} ${value}/" "$config_file"
    else

#!/bin/bash

SSHD_CONFIG="/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

# Ensure the sshd_config file exists
if [[ ! -f "$SSHD_CONFIG" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $SSHD_CONFIG does not exist"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to set or update a configuration option
set_sshd_option() {
    local option="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local config_file="$SSHD_CONFIG"
    
    # Check if the option exists (commented or uncommented)
    if grep -qE "^\s*#?\s*${option}\s+" "$config_file"; then
        # Option exists, update it
        sed -i "s/^\s*#\?\s*${option}\s\+.*/${option} ${value}/" "$config_file"
    else
        # Option doesn't exist, append it
        echo "${option} ${value}" >> "$config_file"
    fi
}

# Set RekeyLimit to 512M 1h
# This ensures session key renegotiation after 512 megabytes or 1 hour
set_sshd_option "RekeyLimit" "512M 1h"

# Verify the configuration is valid
if sshd -t 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "SSH configuration is valid"
else
    echo "Error: SSH configuration test failed"
    exit 1
fi

# Restart sshd to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

echo "SSH server hardened: RekeyLimit set to 512M 1h"

# Idempotent script to disable persistent core dump storage on RHEL 8
# This configures systemd-coredump to not store core dumps

set -e

COREDUMP_CONF="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf"
COREDUMP_CONF_DIR="/etc/systemd/coredump.conf.d"
DROP_IN_FILE="${COREDUMP_CONF_DIR}/disable-storage.conf"

mkdir -p "${COREDUMP_CONF_DIR}"

# Create or update the drop-in configuration file
# Using a drop-in file is preferred over modifying the main config
cat > "${DROP_IN_FILE}" << 'EOF'

ProcessSizeMax=0
EOF

# This handles cases where drop-in might not be read

        sed -i '/^\[Coredump\]/a Storage=none' "${COREDUMP_CONF}"

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent remediation script to disable IPv6 router advertisements on RHEL 8
# This addresses the security concern of forged router advertisement attacks

set -e

SYSCTL_PARAM="net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra"
DESIRED_VALUE="0"
SYSCTL_CONF_FILE="/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv6-accept-ra.conf"

# Function to apply runtime setting
apply_runtime_setting() {
    local current_value
    current_value=$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    if [[ "${current_value}" != "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
        echo "Applying runtime setting: ${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE}"
        sysctl -w "${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE}" > /dev/null
    else
        echo "Runtime setting already correct: ${SYSCTL_PARAM}=${DESIRED_VALUE}"
    fi
}

# Function to apply persistent setting
apply_persistent_setting() {
    local config_line="${SYSCTL_PARAM} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}"
    
    # Check if the configuration file exists and has the correct setting
    if [[ -f "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
        # Check if the file already contains the correct setting
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*${DESIRED_VALUE}[[:space:]]*$" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
            echo "Persistent setting already correct in ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
            return 0
        fi
        
        # Check if the parameter exists with a different value and update it
        if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"; then
            echo "Updating existing setting in ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
            sed -i "s|^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=.*|${config_line}|" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    # Remove any conflicting settings from other sysctl configuration files
    for conf_file in /etc/sysctl.conf /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "${conf_file}" ]] && [[ "${conf_file}" != "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" ]]; then
            if grep -qE "^[[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=" "${conf_file}" 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "Commenting out conflicting setting in ${conf_file}"
                sed -i "s|^\([[:space:]]*${SYSCTL_PARAM}[[:space:]]*=\)|# Disabled by security remediation: \1|" "${conf_file}"
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    # Create or append to the configuration file
    echo "Adding persistent setting to ${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
    echo "${config_line}" >> "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}"
}

# Main execution
echo "Starting IPv6 router advertisement remediation..."

# Ensure the sysctl.d directory exists
if [[ ! -d "/etc/sysctl.d" ]]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/sysctl.d
fi

# Apply runtime setting
apply_runtime_setting

# Apply persistent setting
apply_persistent_setting

# Verify the settings
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
echo "  Runtime value: $(sysctl -n ${SYSCTL_PARAM})"
echo "  Persistent config: $(grep -h "${SYSCTL_PARAM}" "${SYSCTL_CONF_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || echo 'Not found')"

# Final verification
runtime_check=$(sysctl -n "${SYSCTL_PARAM}")
if [[ "${runtime_check}" == "${DESIRED_VALUE}" ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Remediation completed successfully."
    exit 0
else
    echo ""
    echo "ERROR: Remediation verification failed."
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure PAM account lockout on RHEL 8
# This script configures pam_faillock for tracking failed login attempts
# and locking out accounts after consecutive failures

set -euo pipefail

# Configuration parameters
DENY_ATTEMPTS=3
UNLOCK_TIME=900
FAIL_INTERVAL=900

# Backup directory
BACKUP_DIR="/var/backup/pam-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"

# Function to create backups
backup_file() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"
        cp -a "$file" "$BACKUP_DIR/"
    fi
}

# Function to check if running as root
check_root() {
    if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

# Function to configure faillock.conf
configure_faillock_conf() {
    local faillock_conf="/etc/security/faillock.conf"
    
    backup_file "$faillock_conf"
    
    # Ensure the file exists
    touch "$faillock_conf"
    
    # Configure deny (number of failed attempts before lockout)
    if grep -q "^deny" "$faillock_conf"; then
        sed -i "s/^deny.*/deny = $DENY_ATTEMPTS/" "$faillock_conf"
    elif grep -q "^#.*deny" "$faillock_conf"; then
        sed -i "s/^#.*deny.*/deny = $DENY_ATTEMPTS/" "$faillock_conf"
    else
        echo "deny = $DENY_ATTEMPTS" >> "$faillock_conf"
    fi
    
    # Configure unlock_time (seconds until account unlocks)
    if grep -q "^unlock_time" "$faillock_conf"; then
        sed -i "s/^unlock_time.*/unlock_time = $UNLOCK_TIME/" "$faillock_conf"
    elif grep -q "^#.*unlock_time" "$faillock_conf"; then
        sed -i "s/^#.*unlock_time.*/unlock_time = $UNLOCK_TIME/" "$faillock_conf"
    else
        echo "unlock_time = $UNLOCK_TIME" >> "$faillock_conf"
    fi
    
    # Configure fail_interval (time window for counting failures)
    if grep -q "^fail_interval" "$faillock_conf"; then
        sed -i "s/^fail_interval.*/fail_interval = $FAIL_INTERVAL/" "$faillock_conf"
    elif grep -q "^#.*fail_interval" "$faillock_conf"; then
        sed -i "s/^#.*fail_interval.*/fail_interval = $FAIL_INTERVAL/" "$faillock_conf"
    else
        echo "fail_interval = $FAIL_INTERVAL" >> "$faillock_conf"
    fi
    
    # Ensure even_deny_root is set (optional but recommended)
    if ! grep -q "^even_deny_root" "$faillock_conf"; then
        if grep -q "^#.*even_deny_root" "$faillock_conf"; then
            sed -i "s/^#.*even_deny_root.*/even_deny_root/" "$faillock_conf"
        else
            echo "even_deny_root" >> "$faillock_conf"
        fi
    fi
    
    # Set audit flag to log failures
    if ! grep -q "^audit" "$faillock_conf"; then
        if grep -q "^#.*audit" "$faillock_conf"; then
            sed -i "s/^#.*audit$/audit/" "$faillock_conf"
        else
            echo "audit" >> "$faillock_conf"
        fi
    fi
    
    # Set silent to not reveal info about locked accounts
    if ! grep -q "^silent" "$faillock_conf"; then
        if grep -q "^#.*silent" "$faillock_conf"; then
            sed -i "s/^#.*silent$/silent/" "$faillock_conf"
        else
            echo "silent" >> "$faillock_conf"
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Configured $faillock_conf"
}

# Function to configure PAM files using authselect
configure_pam_with_authselect() {
    # Check if authselect is available (RHEL 8 standard)
    if ! command -v authselect &> /dev/null; then
        echo "authselect not found, falling back to manual PAM configuration" >&2
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Get current profile
    local current_profile
    current_profile=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    if [[ -z "$current_profile" ]]; then
        # No profile selected, use sssd as default (common on RHEL 8)
        echo "No authselect profile selected, enabling sssd with faillock"
        authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
    else
        # Check if with-faillock is already enabled
        if authselect current | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
            echo "faillock feature already enabled in authselect"
        else
            # Enable faillock feature
            echo "Enabling faillock feature in authselect"
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock
        fi
    fi
    
    return 0
}

# Function for manual PAM configuration (fallback)
configure_pam_manually() {
    local pam_files=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth")
    
    for pam_file in "${pam_files[@]}"; do
        if [[ ! -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
            echo "Warning: $pam_file not found, skipping" >&2
            continue
        fi
        
        backup_file "$pam_file"
        
        # Check if pam_faillock is already configured
        if grep -q "pam_faillock.so" "$pam_file"; then
            echo "$pam_file already has pam_faillock configured"
            continue
        fi
        
        # Create a temporary file for the new configuration
        local tmp_file
        tmp_file=$(mktemp)
        
        # Process the file line by line
        local in_auth=0
        local preauth_added=0
        local authfail_added=0
        local authsucc_added=0
        local in_account=0
        local account_added=0
        
        while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
            # Track if we're in auth section
            if [[ "$line" =~ ^auth[[:space:]] ]]; then
                in_auth=1
                in_account=0
                
                # Add preauth line before the first pam_unix or pam_sss auth line
                if [[ $preauth_added -eq 0 ]] && [[ "$line" =~ pam_unix.so|pam_sss.so ]]; then
                    echo "auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth" >> "$tmp_file"
                    preauth_added=1
                fi
                
                echo "$line" >> "$tmp_file"
                
                # Add authfail line after pam_unix auth sufficient line
                if [[ $authfail_added -eq 0 ]] && [[ "$line" =~ sufficient.*pam_unix.so ]]; then
                    echo "auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail" >> "$tmp_file"
                    authfail_added=1
                fi
                
                # Add authsucc line after pam_sss auth sufficient line (if present)
                if [[ $authsucc_added -eq 0 ]] && [[ "$line" =~ sufficient.*pam_sss.so ]]; then
                    echo "auth        sufficient                                   pam_faillock.so authsucc" >> "$tmp_file"
                    authsucc_added=1
                fi
                
            elif [[ "$line" =~ ^account[[:space:]] ]]; then
                in_auth=0
                in_account=1
                
                # Add account line before the first account line
                if [[ $account_added -eq 0 ]]; then
                    echo "account     required                                     pam_faillock.so" >> "$tmp_file"
                    account_added=1
                fi
                
                echo "$line" >> "$tmp_file"
            else
                in_auth=0
                in_account=0
                echo "$line" >> "$tmp_file"
            fi
        done < "$pam_file"
        
        # Replace the original file
        mv "$tmp_file" "$pam_file"
        chmod 644 "$pam_file"
        
        echo "Manually configured $pam_file"
    done
}

# Function to verify configuration
verify_configuration() {
    local success=0
    
    echo ""
    echo "Verifying configuration..."
    
    # Check faillock.conf
    if grep -q "^deny = $DENY_ATTEMPTS" /etc/security/faillock.conf 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ faillock.conf: deny is set to $DENY_ATTEMPTS"
    else
        echo "✗ faillock.conf: deny setting not found or incorrect"
        success=1
    fi
    
    if grep -q "^unlock_time = $UNLOCK_TIME" /etc/security/faillock.conf 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ faillock.conf: unlock_time is set to $UNLOCK_TIME"
    else
        echo "✗ faillock.conf: unlock_time setting not found or incorrect"
        success=1
    fi
    
    # Check PAM configuration
    local pam_files=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth")

        return 1
    fi
    
    # Get current profile
    local current_profile
    current_profile=$(authselect current -r 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    
    if [[ -z "$current_profile" ]]; then
        # No profile selected, use sssd as default (common on RHEL 8)
        echo "No authselect profile selected, enabling sssd with faillock"
        authselect select sssd with-faillock --force
    else
        # Check if with-faillock is already enabled
        if authselect current | grep -q "with-faillock"; then
            echo "faillock feature already enabled in authselect"
        else
            # Enable faillock feature
            echo "Enabling faillock feature in authselect"
            authselect enable-feature with-faillock
        fi
    fi
    
    return 0
}

# Function for manual PAM configuration (fallback)
configure_pam_manually() {
    local pam_files=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth")
    
    for pam_file in "${pam_files[@]}"; do
        if [[ ! -f "$pam_file" ]]; then
            echo "Warning: $pam_file not found, skipping" >&2
            continue
        fi
        
        backup_file "$pam_file"
        
        # Check if pam_faillock is already configured
        if grep -q "pam_faillock.so" "$pam_file"; then
            echo "$pam_file already has pam_faillock configured"
            continue
        fi
        
        # Create a temporary file for the new configuration
        local tmp_file
        tmp_file=$(mktemp)
        
        # Process the file line by line
        local in_auth=0
        local preauth_added=0
        local authfail_added=0
        local authsucc_added=0
        local in_account=0
        local account_added=0
        
        while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
            # Track if we're in auth section
            if [[ "$line" =~ ^auth[[:space:]] ]]; then
                in_auth=1
                in_account=0
                
                # Add preauth line before the first pam_unix or pam_sss auth line
                if [[ $preauth_added -eq 0 ]] && [[ "$line" =~ pam_unix.so|pam_sss.so ]]; then
                    echo "auth        required                                     pam_faillock.so preauth" >> "$tmp_file"
                    preauth_added=1
                fi
                
                echo "$line" >> "$tmp_file"
                
                # Add authfail line after pam_unix auth sufficient line
                if [[ $authfail_added -eq 0 ]] && [[ "$line" =~ sufficient.*pam_unix.so ]]; then
                    echo "auth        [default=die]                                pam_faillock.so authfail" >> "$tmp_file"
                    authfail_added=1
                fi
                
                # Add authsucc line after pam_sss auth sufficient line (if present)
                if [[ $authsucc_added -eq 0 ]] && [[ "$line" =~ sufficient.*pam_sss.so ]]; then
                    echo "auth        sufficient                                   pam_faillock.so authsucc" >> "$tmp_file"
                    authsucc_added=1
                fi
                
            elif [[ "$line" =~ ^account[[:space:]] ]]; then
                in_auth=0
                in_account=1
                
                # Add account line before the first account line
                if [[ $account_added -eq 0 ]]; then
                    echo "account     required                                     pam_faillock.so" >> "$tmp_file"
                    account_added=1
                fi
                
                echo "$line" >> "$tmp_file"
            else
                in_auth=0
                in_account=0
                echo "$line" >> "$tmp_file"
            fi
        done < "$pam_file"
        
        # Replace the original file
        mv "$tmp_file" "$pam_file"
        chmod 644 "$pam_file"
        
        echo "Manually configured $pam_file"
    done
}

# Function to verify configuration
verify_configuration() {
    local success=0
    
    echo ""
    echo "Verifying configuration..."
    
    # Check faillock.conf
    if grep -q "^deny = $DENY_ATTEMPTS" /etc/security/faillock.conf 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ faillock.conf: deny is set to $DENY_ATTEMPTS"
    else
        echo "✗ faillock.conf: deny setting not found or incorrect"
        success=1
    fi
    
    if grep -q "^unlock_time = $UNLOCK_TIME" /etc/security/faillock.conf 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "✓ faillock.conf: unlock_time is set to $UNLOCK_TIME"
    else
        echo "✗ faillock.conf: unlock_time setting not found or incorrect"
        success=1
    fi
    
    # Check PAM configuration
    local pam_files=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth")

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent remediation script to ensure all NTP sources have maxpoll defined
# For RHEL 8 systems using chronyd

set -euo pipefail

CHRONY_CONF="/etc/chrony.conf"
CHRONY_CONF_DIR="/etc/chrony.d"
DEFAULT_MAXPOLL=10

# Function to check if maxpoll is already set in a server/pool line
has_maxpoll() {
    echo "$1" | grep -qE '\bmaxpoll\b'
}

# Function to add maxpoll to a server/pool line if not present
add_maxpoll_to_line() {
    local line="$1"
    local maxpoll_value="$2"
    
    if has_maxpoll "$line"; then
        # maxpoll already present, return line unchanged
        echo "$line"
    else
        # Add maxpoll before any trailing comment or at end of line
        # Handle lines that might have inline comments
        if echo "$line" | grep -qE '#'; then
            # Line has a comment, insert maxpoll before it
            echo "$line" | sed -E "s/(#)/maxpoll ${maxpoll_value} \1/"
        else
            # No comment, append maxpoll at end
            echo "${line} maxpoll ${maxpoll_value}"
        fi
    fi
}

# Function to process a chrony configuration file
process_chrony_file() {
    local conf_file="$1"
    local temp_file
    local modified=0
    
    if [[ ! -f "$conf_file" ]]; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    temp_file=$(mktemp)
    
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        # Check if this is a server, pool, or peer directive (not commented out)
        if echo "$line" | grep -qE '^\s*(server|pool|peer)\s+'; then
            new_line=$(add_maxpoll_to_line "$line" "$DEFAULT_MAXPOLL")
            if [[ "$new_line" != "$line" ]]; then
                modified=1
            fi
            echo "$new_line" >> "$temp_file"
        else
            echo "$line" >> "$temp_file"
        fi
    done < "$conf_file"
    
    if [[ $modified -eq 1 ]]; then
        # Preserve original file permissions and ownership
        cp --preserve=mode,ownership "$conf_file" "${conf_file}.bak"
        mv "$temp_file" "$conf_file"
        # Restore SELinux context if applicable
        if command -v restorecon &> /dev/null; then
            restorecon "$conf_file"
        fi
        echo "Modified: $conf_file"
        return 1
    else
        rm -f "$temp_file"
        return 0
    fi
}

# Main execution

# Check if running as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Check if chrony is installed
if ! rpm -q chrony &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Chrony is not installed. Installing..."
    dnf install -y chrony
fi

changes_made=0

echo "Processing $CHRONY_CONF..."
if ! process_chrony_file "$CHRONY_CONF"; then
    changes_made=1
fi

# Process any additional configuration files in chrony.d
if [[ -d "$CHRONY_CONF_DIR" ]]; then
    for conf_file in "$CHRONY_CONF_DIR"/*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$conf_file" ]]; then
            echo "Processing $conf_file..."
            if ! process_chrony_file "$conf_file"; then
                changes_made=1
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

if ! systemctl is-enabled chronyd &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Enabling chronyd service..."
    systemctl enable chronyd
    changes_made=1
fi

if [[ $changes_made -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo "Restarting chronyd to apply changes..."
    systemctl restart chronyd
else
    # Ensure service is running even if no config changes
    if ! systemctl is-active chronyd &> /dev/null; then
        echo "Starting chronyd service..."
        systemctl start chronyd
    fi
fi

# Verify configuration
echo ""
echo "Current time sources with their polling intervals:"
chronyc sources -v 2>/dev/null | head -20 || true

echo ""
echo "Verifying maxpoll settings in configuration:"
grep -E '^\s*(server|pool|peer)\s+' "$CHRONY_CONF" 2>/dev/null || true
if [[ -d "$CHRONY_CONF_DIR" ]]; then
    for conf_file in "$CHRONY_CONF_DIR"/*.conf; do
        if [[ -f "$conf_file" ]]; then
            grep -E '^\s*(server|pool|peer)\s+' "$conf_file" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done
fi

echo ""
echo "Remediation complete. All NTP sources now have maxpoll defined."
exit 0

set -euo pipefail

KERNEL_PARAM="page_poison=1"

# Function to check if a parameter exists in grubby default args
param_in_default_args() {
    local current_args
    current_args=$(grubby --info=DEFAULT 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^args=' | sed 's/^args="//' | sed 's/"$//' || echo "")
    if echo "$current_args" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])${KERNEL_PARAM}([[:space:]]|$)"; then
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

# Function to check if parameter is in /etc/default/grub GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
param_in_grub_default() {
    if [ -f /etc/default/grub ]; then
        if grep -qE "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" /etc/default/grub; then
            local cmdline
            cmdline=$(grep -E "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" /etc/default/grub | sed 's/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="//' | sed 's/"$//')
            if echo "$cmdline" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])${KERNEL_PARAM}([[:space:]]|$)"; then
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    fi
    return 1
}

# Add parameter to all existing kernels using grubby
echo "Checking existing kernel configurations..."
all_kernels=$(grubby --info=ALL 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^kernel=' | sed 's/^kernel=//' | tr -d '"' || echo "")

if [ -n "$all_kernels" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r kernel; do
        if [ -n "$kernel" ] && [ -f "$kernel" ]; then
            # Check if parameter already exists for this kernel
            kernel_args=$(grubby --info="$kernel" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^args=' | sed 's/^args="//' | sed 's/"$//' || echo "")
            if ! echo "$kernel_args" | grep -qE "(^|[[:space:]])${KERNEL_PARAM}([[:space:]]|$)"; then
                echo "Adding ${KERNEL_PARAM} to kernel: $kernel"
                grubby --update-kernel="$kernel" --args="${KERNEL_PARAM}"
            else
                echo "Parameter ${KERNEL_PARAM} already present for kernel: $kernel"
            fi
        fi
    done <<< "$all_kernels"
else
    echo "No kernels found via grubby, attempting to add to DEFAULT..."
    if ! param_in_default_args; then
        grubby --update-kernel=DEFAULT --args="${KERNEL_PARAM}"
    fi
fi

# Ensure parameter is set for future kernels by updating /etc/default/grub
echo "Updating /etc/default/grub for future kernel installations..."

if [ -f /etc/default/grub ]; then
    if param_in_grub_default; then
        echo "Parameter ${KERNEL_PARAM} already present in /etc/default/grub"
    else
        if grep -qE "^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" /etc/default/grub; then
            # Parameter not present, add it to existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
            sed -i "s/^GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${KERNEL_PARAM} /" /etc/default/grub
            # Clean up any double spaces
            sed -i 's/  */ /g' /etc/default/grub
            echo "Added ${KERNEL_PARAM} to existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX"
        else
            # GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX doesn't exist, create it
            echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${KERNEL_PARAM}\"" >> /etc/default/grub
            echo "Created GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX with ${KERNEL_PARAM}"
        fi
    fi
else
    # Create /etc/default/grub if it doesn't exist
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"${KERNEL_PARAM}\"" > /etc/default/grub
    echo "Created /etc/default/grub with ${KERNEL_PARAM}"
fi

# Regenerate grub configuration to ensure consistency
# Determine the correct grub.cfg location
if [ -d /sys/firmware/efi ]; then
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/redhat/grub.cfg"
    if [ ! -f "$GRUB_CFG" ]; then
        GRUB_CFG="/boot/efi/EFI/centos/grub.cfg"
    fi
else
    GRUB_CFG="/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
fi

if [ -f "$GRUB_CFG" ] || [ -d "$(dirname "$GRUB_CFG")" ]; then
    echo "Regenerating GRUB configuration at ${GRUB_CFG}..."
    grub2-mkconfig -o "$GRUB_CFG" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

echo "Memory hardening configuration complete."
echo "The ${KERNEL_PARAM} parameter has been applied to all kernels."
echo "A reboot is required for the changes to take effect on the running kernel."

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to configure auditd disk error action to single-user mode on RHEL 8

AUDIT_CONF="/etc/audit/auditd.conf"
DESIRED_VALUE="single"
PARAM_NAME="disk_error_action"

# Check if auditd.conf exists
if [[ ! -f "$AUDIT_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "Error: $AUDIT_CONF does not exist. Is auditd installed?"
    exit 1
fi

# Check if the parameter already exists in the file
if grep -q "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDIT_CONF"; then
    # Parameter exists, check if it has the correct value
    current_value=$(grep "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDIT_CONF" | sed 's/.*=[[:space:]]*//' | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -d '[:space:]')
    
    if [[ "$current_value" == "$DESIRED_VALUE" ]]; then
        echo "$PARAM_NAME is already set to $DESIRED_VALUE. No changes needed."
    else
        # Update the existing parameter
        sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=.*/${PARAM_NAME} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "$AUDIT_CONF"
        echo "Updated $PARAM_NAME from '$current_value' to '$DESIRED_VALUE'."
    fi
elif grep -q "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDIT_CONF"; then
    # Parameter exists but is commented out, uncomment and set correct value
    sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=.*/${PARAM_NAME} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "$AUDIT_CONF"
    echo "Uncommented and set $PARAM_NAME to $DESIRED_VALUE."
else
    # Parameter doesn't exist, add it
    echo "${PARAM_NAME} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "$AUDIT_CONF"
    echo "Added $PARAM_NAME = $DESIRED_VALUE to $AUDIT_CONF."
fi

# Restart auditd service to apply changes
# Note: auditd requires special handling - it cannot be restarted with systemctl restart
# We use service command or augenrules --load for proper restart
if command -v service &>/dev/null; then
    service auditd restart
    echo "Restarted auditd service using 'service' command."
else
    # Alternative method using kill and systemctl
    pkill -HUP auditd 2>/dev/null || systemctl kill auditd --signal=SIGHUP 2>/dev/null || true
    echo "Sent SIGHUP to auditd to reload configuration."
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
grep "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDIT_CONF"

echo ""
echo "Configuration complete. disk_error_action is set to switch to single-user mode."

        sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=.*/${PARAM_NAME} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "$AUDIT_CONF"
        echo "Updated $PARAM_NAME from '$current_value' to '$DESIRED_VALUE'."
    fi
elif grep -q "^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDIT_CONF"; then
    # Parameter exists but is commented out, uncomment and set correct value
    sed -i "s/^[[:space:]]*#[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=.*/${PARAM_NAME} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}/" "$AUDIT_CONF"
    echo "Uncommented and set $PARAM_NAME to $DESIRED_VALUE."
else
    # Parameter doesn't exist, add it
    echo "${PARAM_NAME} = ${DESIRED_VALUE}" >> "$AUDIT_CONF"
    echo "Added $PARAM_NAME = $DESIRED_VALUE to $AUDIT_CONF."
fi

# Restart auditd service to apply changes
# Note: auditd requires special handling - it cannot be restarted with systemctl restart
# We use service command or augenrules --load for proper restart
if command -v service &>/dev/null; then
    service auditd restart
    echo "Restarted auditd service using 'service' command."
else
    # Alternative method using kill and systemctl
    pkill -HUP auditd 2>/dev/null || systemctl kill auditd --signal=SIGHUP 2>/dev/null || true
    echo "Sent SIGHUP to auditd to reload configuration."
fi

# Verify the configuration
echo ""
echo "Verification:"
grep "^[[:space:]]*${PARAM_NAME}[[:space:]]*=" "$AUDIT_CONF"

echo ""
echo "Configuration complete. disk_error_action is set to switch to single-user mode."

#!/bin/bash

# Idempotent script to disable SCTP kernel module on RHEL 8

set -e

MODPROBE_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/sctp.conf"
MODULES_LOAD_DIR="/etc/modules-load.d"
BLACKLIST_FILE="/etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-sctp.conf"

# Ensure the modprobe.d directory exists
if [ ! -d "/etc/modprobe.d" ]; then
    mkdir -p /etc/modprobe.d
fi

# Create or update the modprobe configuration to disable SCTP
# This prevents the module from being loaded
INSTALL_LINE="install sctp /bin/false"
BLACKLIST_LINE="blacklist sctp"

# Check and update the install rule
if [ -f "$MODPROBE_FILE" ]; then
    if ! grep -q "^install sctp /bin/false$" "$MODPROBE_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Remove any existing install lines for sctp
        sed -i '/^install sctp/d' "$MODPROBE_FILE"
        echo "$INSTALL_LINE" >> "$MODPROBE_FILE"
    fi
else
    echo "$INSTALL_LINE" > "$MODPROBE_FILE"
fi

# Create or update the blacklist configuration
if [ -f "$BLACKLIST_FILE" ]; then
    if ! grep -q "^blacklist sctp$" "$BLACKLIST_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Remove any existing blacklist lines for sctp
        sed -i '/^blacklist sctp/d' "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
        echo "$BLACKLIST_LINE" >> "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
    fi
else
    echo "$BLACKLIST_LINE" > "$BLACKLIST_FILE"
fi

# Remove any configuration that might load sctp on boot
if [ -d "$MODULES_LOAD_DIR" ]; then
    for conf_file in "$MODULES_LOAD_DIR"/*.conf; do
        if [ -f "$conf_file" ]; then
            if grep -q "^sctp$" "$conf_file" 2>/dev/null; then
                sed -i '/^sctp$/d' "$conf_file"
            fi
        fi
    done
fi

# If the module is currently loaded, unload it
if lsmod | grep -q "^sctp "; then
    rmmod sctp 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Update module dependency database
depmod -a 2>/dev/null || true

echo "SCTP kernel module has been disabled and blacklisted."
echo "The configuration will persist across reboots."
